text: Oil and water usually collect in compressed air tanks. If you do not have an automatic tank drain, when should you drain the air tanks?
explanation: If your vehicle does not have automatic air tank drains, drain your air tanks at the end of each working day to remove moisture and oil. Otherwise, the brakes could fail.

text: What is the S-cam used for?
explanation: When you push the brake pedal, air is let into each brake chamber. Air pressure pushes the rod out, moving the slack adjuster, thus twisting the brake camshaft. This turns the s-cam forcing the brake shoes away from one another and presses them against the inside of the brake drum.

text: Which of the following makes the total stopping distance for air brakes longer than that for hydraulic brakes?
explanation: With air brakes there is an added delay or Brake Lag. This is the time required for the brakes to work after the brake pedal is pushed. With hydraulic brake, the brakes work instantly. With air brakes it takes a little time, one half second or more, for the air to flow through the lines to the brakes.

text: The brake system that applies and releases the brakes when the driver uses the brake pedal is the:
explanation: The service brake system applies and releases the brakes when you use the brake pedal during normal driving.

text: To make an emergency stop with air brakes, using the stab braking method, you should:
explanation: Stab braking means that you: apply your brakes all the way, and release brakes when wheels lock up. As soon as the wheels start rolling, apply the brakes fully again.

text: Brake lag distance at 55 mph is _____ feet?
explanation: The air brake lag distance at 55 mph on dry pavement adds about 32 feet.

text: If your vehicle has an alcohol evaporator, it is there to:
explanation: Some air brake systems have an alcohol evaporator to put alcohol into the air system. This helps to reduce the risk of ice in air brake valves and other parts during cold weather. Daily air tank drainage is still needed to get rid of water and oil.

text: What can legally hold a parking or emergency brake in position for a truck, truck tractor or bus?
explanation: All trucks, truck tractors, and buses must be equipped with emergency brakes and parking brakes. They must be held on by mechanical force because air pressure can eventually leak away. Spring brakes are usually used to meet these needs.

text: The braking power of the spring brakes:
explanation: The braking power of spring brakes depends on the brakes being in adjustment. If the brakes are not adjusted properly, neither the regular brakes nor the emergency/parking brakes will work right.

text: Which of the following should you do before leaving your vehicle unattended?
explanation: Never leave your vehicle unattended without applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels. Your vehicle might roll away and cause injury and damage

text: Under which conditions are front wheel brakes good?
explanation: Front wheel brakes have been shown to be ideal under all weather and driving conditions. Front wheel braking is unlikely to cause a skid even on icy roads.

text: If the air tanks are not drained,
explanation: Compressed air usually has some water and some compressor oil in it, which is bad for the air brake system. For example, the water can freeze in cold weather and cause brake failure.

text: An antilock braking system (ABS)
explanation: ABS is a computerized system that keeps your wheels from locking up during hard brake applications.

text: Which of the following can cause brakes to fade or fail?
explanation: Brakes can fade or fail from excessive heat caused by using them too much and not relying on the engine braking effect. Brake fade is also affected by adjustment.

text: What is the best way to test if your vehicle's spring brakes come on automatically?
explanation: On both tractor-trailers and single vehicles, to test whether the spring brakes will come on automatically, use the same method you use for testing the low air pressure warning signal: Step on and off the brake pedal, this time until you reach an even lower psi reading and the parking brake valve closes or pops out.

text: If your vehicle has a properly functioning dual air brake system and minimum-sized air tanks, the air pressure should build from 85 to 100 psi within ____ seconds.
explanation: When the engine is at operating rpms, the pressure should build from 85 to 100 psi within 45 seconds in dual air systems. If the vehicle has larger than minimum air tanks, the buildup time can be longer and still be safe.

text: The air compressor will stop pumping air into the air tanks at ____ psi.
explanation: The governor controls when the air compressor will pump air into the air storage tanks. When air tank pressure rises to the "cut-out" level around 125 psi, the governor stops the compressor from pumping air. When the tank pressure falls to the "cut-in" pressure around 100 psi, the governor allows the compressor to start pumping again.

text: Excessive heat caused by overuse of the service brakes can cause
explanation: Brakes can fade or fail from excessive heat caused by using them too much and not relying on the engine braking effect.

text: Excessive use of the service brakes may result in overheating, which can lead to
explanation: Brake fade results from excessive heat causing chemical changes in the brake lining, which reduce friction, and also causing expansion of the brake drums.

text: A typical air brake system is fully charged at
explanation: Pumping by the air compressor should start at about 100 psi and stop at about 125 psi.

text: During normal driving, spring brakes are usually held back by
explanation: Parking or emergency brakes must be held on by mechanical force because air pressure can eventually leak away. Spring brakes are usually used to meet these needs. When driving, powerful springs are held back by air pressure.

text: During your final air brake check, if the air pressure does not build up fast enough,
explanation: If air pressure does not build up fast enough, your pressure may drop too low during driving, requiring an emergency stop. Don't drive until you get the problem fixed.

text: To test the static air leakage rate, you should
explanation: With a fully-charged air system (typically 125 psi), turn off the engine, release the parking brake (push in); and time the air pressure drop.

text: Air brakes use _________ to make the brakes work.
explanation: Air brakes use compressed air to make the brakes work. Air brakes are a good and safe way of stopping large and heavy vehicles, but the brakes must be well maintained and used properly.

text: When you press the brake pedal,
explanation: When you push the brake pedal, air is let into each brake chamber.

text: The air compressor governor controls:
explanation: The governor controls when the air compressor will pump air into the air storage tanks. When air tank pressure rises to the "cut-out" level around 125 psi, the governor stops the compressor from pumping air. When the tank pressure falls to the "cut-in" pressure around 100 psi, the governor allows the compressor to start pumping again.

text: Of the choices below, the first thing to do when a low air pressure warning comes on is:
explanation: When the low air pressure warning light and buzzer first come on, bring the vehicle to a safe stop right away, while you can still control the brakes.

text: Air brake equipped vehicles must have:
explanation: All vehicles with air brakes have a pressure gauge connected to the air tank. If the vehicle has a dual air brake system, there will be a gauge for each half of the system.

text: Modern air brake systems combines three different systems.  They are the service brakes, the parking brakes and the:
explanation: Air brakes are really three different braking systems: service brake, parking brake, and emergency brake.

text: If you must make an emergency stop, you should brake so that you:
explanation: You should brake in a way that will keep your vehicle in a straight line and allow you to turn if it becomes necessary. You can use the "controlled braking" method or the "stab braking" method.

text: The driver must be able to see a warning that is given before air pressure in the service air tanks falls below:
explanation: Low air warning devices (buzzer, light, flag) should activate before air pressure drops below 60 psi or level specified by the manufacturer.

text: The most common type of foundation brakes found on heavy vehicles is the:
explanation: Wedge brakes and disc brakes are less common than s-cam brakes.

text: When driving down a long steep hill you should:
explanation: Snub braking method is used for steep downhill grades, in this method apply the brakes just hard enough to feel a definite slowdown. When your speed has been reduced to approximately five mph below your "safe" speed, release the brakes. When your speed has increased to your "safe" speed, repeat.

text: The modulating control valve is?
explanation: In some vehicles, a control handle on the dash board may be used to apply the spring brakes gradually. This is called a modulating valve. It is spring loaded, so you have a feel for the braking action. The more you move the control lever, the harder the spring brakes come on. They work this way, so you can control the spring brakes if the service brakes fail.

text: Which of the following will be true about your brake function if your antilock braking system (ABS) fails?
explanation: Without ABS you still have normal brake functions. Drive and brake as you always have.

text: Which of the following is not part of the braking process when you're driving a tractor-trailer combination vehicle with an antilock braking system (ABS)?
explanation: You should not drive faster to keep the tractor and trailer straight. When you drive a vehicle with ABS, you should brake as you always have. In other words: use only the braking force necessary to stop safely and stay in control; brake the same way regardless of whether you have ABS on the bus, tractor, the trailer, or both; as you slow down monitor your tractor and trailer and back off the brakes if it is safe to do so to stay in control.

text: Which of the following are true about a dual air brake system?
explanation: Most heavy-duty vehicles use dual air brake systems for safety. A dual air brake system has two separate air brake systems, which use a single set of brake controls. One system or primary system typically operates the regular brakes on the rear axle or axles. The other or secondary system operates the regular brakes on the front axle and possibly one rear axle.

text: How can you tell if your vehicle is equipped with an antilock braking system (ABS)?
explanation: The Department of Transportation requires that ABS be on: air brake vehicles built on or after March 1, 1998.

text: Spring brakes are
explanation: Spring brakes are an important backup system: powerful springs that automatically apply the brakes if they sense that air pressure has been lost for some reason, such as a leak. They will also apply the brakes if the psi gets too low, although ideally, you should take control of your brakes before that happens.

text: In the event of a brake system failure, the emergency brake system uses __________ to stop the vehicle.
explanation: The emergency brake system uses parts of the service and parking brake systems to stop the vehicle in a brake system failure.

text: Repeatedly pressing and releasing (fanning) the brake pedal may result in
explanation: Each time you release the brakes, some compressed air leaves the system and must be replenished by the air compressor. If you keep pressing and releasing the brake pedal, air may leave the system faster than the air compressor can replenish it, and the air pressure may drop to the point that the brakes won't work.

text: If the spring brakes are on, when should you push the brake pedal?
explanation: Never push the brake pedal down when the spring brakes are on. If you do, the brakes could be damaged by the combined forces of the springs and the air pressure.

text: You'll know that your brakes are fading if
explanation: As the overheated drums expand, the brake shoes and linings have to move farther to contact the drums, and the force of this contact is reduced. So, you will be pressing harder on the pedal.

text: A converter dolly with antilock brakes (ABS) is required to have
explanation: Converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998, are required to have antilock brakes. These dollies will have a yellow lamp on the left side of the dolly.

text: Which of the following is the most important thing about hard braking?
explanation: Emergency braking does not mean pushing down on the brake pedal as hard as you can. That will only keep the wheels locked up and cause a skid. If the wheels are skidding, you cannot control the vehicle.

text: In controlled braking, you
explanation: You apply the brakes as hard as you can without locking the wheels. Keep steering wheel movements very small while doing this. If you need to make a larger steering adjustment or if the wheels lock, release the brakes. Re-apply the brakes as soon as you can.

text: If oil and water collects in the air tanks, what could happen to the brakes?
explanation: Compressed air usually has some water and some compressor oil in it, which is bad for the air brake system. For example, the water can freeze in cold weather and cause brake failure.

text: S-cam drum brakes have an S-cam in each brake chamber. Why is it called an "S-cam?"
explanation: The S-cam is S-shaped. The s-cam forces the brake shoes away from one another and presses them against the inside of the brake drum.

text: To test the air service brakes,
explanation: Pull forward at 5 mph, apply the service brake and stop. Check to see that the vehicle does not pull to either side and that it stops when brake is applied.

text: When can you leave your truck unattended without first applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels?
explanation: Never leave your vehicle unattended without applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels. Your vehicle might roll away and cause injury and damage.

text: If your truck or bus has dual parking control valves it means that you can use pressure from a separate tank to:
explanation: When main air pressure is lost, the spring brakes come on. Some vehicles have a separate air tank which can be used to release the spring brakes. This is so you can move the vehicle in an emergency.

text: The brake pads should be _____ for the brakes to be on?
explanation: To stop, the brake shoes and linings are pushed against the inside of the drum. This causes friction, which slows the vehicle.

text: You should know that your brakes are fading when:
explanation: Increasing application pressure to hold the same speed means the brakes are fading.

text: The application pressure gauge shows the driver how much pressure:
explanation: Application Pressure Gauge shows how much air pressure you are applying to the brakes.

text: If the air compressor should develop a leak, what keeps the air in the tanks?
explanation: Installed on the compressor side of the air tank is a one-way check valve, it allows air into the tanks, but does not allow it to flow back to the compressor.

text: Why drain water from the compressed air tanks?
explanation: Compressed air usually has some water and some compressor oil in it, which is bad for the air brake system, the water can freeze in cold weather and cause brake failure. The water and oil tend to collect in the bottom of the air tank. Be sure that you drain the air tanks completely.

text: During normal driving, spring brakes are usually held back by:
explanation: When driving, powerful springs are held back by air pressure. If the air pressure is removed, the springs put on the brakes.

text: Brake drums must not have cracks longer than _____ the width of the friction area?
explanation: Brake drums or discs must not have cracks longer than one half the width of the friction area.

text: After the initial drop, the maximum acceptable leakage rates per minute are
explanation: With a fully-charged air system typically 125 psi, turn off the engine, release the parking brake; and time the air pressure drop. The loss rate should be less than two psi in one minute for single vehicles brakes released and three with the brakes applied, and less than three psi in one minute for combination vehicles with the brakes released and four with the brakes applied.

text: How do brakes work on a long, steep downgrade?
explanation: The use of brakes on a long and/or steep downgrade is only a supplement to the braking effect of the engine.

text: How would you check your truck's slack adjusters?
explanation: Use gloves and pull hard on each slack adjuster that you can reach. If a slack adjuster moves more than about one inch where the push rod attaches to it, it probably needs adjustment.

text: The parking brake control
explanation: A parking brake control in the cab allows the driver to let the air out of the spring brakes. This lets the springs put the brakes on.

text: If the low air pressure warning signal is not working,
explanation: If the warning signal doesn't work, you could lose air pressure and you would not know it. This could cause sudden emergency braking in a single-circuit air system. In dual systems the stopping distance will be increased. Only limited braking can be done before the spring brakes come on.

text: Your vehicle has a dual air brake system. One of the systems loses air pressure. What will happen?
explanation: A dual air brake system has two separate air brake systems, which use a single set of brake controls. Each system has its own air tanks, hoses, lines, etc. One system typically operates the regular brakes on the rear axle or axles. The other system operates the regular brakes on the front axle and possibly one rear axle.

text: Under ideal conditions, the average driver of a truck or bus equipped with air brakes and traveling at 55 mph would require what stopping distance?
explanation: The total minimum distance your vehicle has traveled, in ideal conditions; with everything considered, including perception distance, reaction distance and braking distance, until you can bring your vehicle to a complete stop. At 55 mph, your vehicle will travel about 450 feet.

text: Modern air brake systems are three different systems combined: the service brakes, parking brakes, and _______ brakes.
explanation: Air brakes are really three different braking systems: service brake, parking brake, and emergency brake.

text: To check the free play of manual slack adjusters on S-cam brakes you should:
explanation: For manual slack adjustors, the brake pushrod should not move more than one inch, with the brakes released, when pulled by hand.

text: Your truck or bus has a dual air brake system. If a low air pressure warning comes on for only one system, what should you do?
explanation: The warning light and buzzer should come on before the air pressure drops below 60 psi in either system. If this happens while driving, you should stop right away and safely park the vehicle. If one air system is very low on pressure, either the front or the rear brakes will not be operating fully. This means it will take you longer to stop. Bring the vehicle to a safe stop and have the air brakes system fixed.

text: If your vehicle has an alcohol evaporator, every day during cold weather you should:
explanation: Check the alcohol container and fill up as necessary, every day during cold weather.

text: With S-cam drum brakes, when the brake chamber fills with air, air pressure pushes the push rod out, moving the ________ and thus rotating the ________.
explanation: In S-cam brakes when you push the brake pedal, air is let into each brake chamber. Air pressure pushes the rod out, moving the slack adjuster, thus twisting the brake camshaft. This turns the s-cam. The s-cam forces the brake shoes away from one another and presses them against the inside of the brake drum.

text: In which of the following situations should you NOT apply the parking brakes?
explanation: Don't use the parking brakes if the brakes are very hot (from just having come down a steep grade), or if the brakes are very wet in freezing temperatures. If they are used while they are very hot, they can be damaged by the heat. If they are used in freezing temperatures when the brakes are very wet, they can freeze so the vehicle cannot move. Use wheel chocks instead.

text: Which of the following can cause brakes to fail or fade?
explanation: Brakes can fade or fail from excessive heat caused by using them too much and not relying on the engine braking effect, they are also affected by adjustment, and low air pressure.

text: If you need to stop quickly and your vehicle lacks antilock brakes, you can use
explanation: You should brake in a way that will keep your vehicle in a straight line and allow you to turn if it becomes necessary. You can use the "controlled braking" method or the "stab braking" method.

text: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes will come fully on when the air pressure drops to a range of
explanation: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes will come fully on when air pressure drops to a range of 20 to 45 psi (typically 20 to 30 psi).

text: The modulating control valve allows you to control the
explanation: In some vehicles a control handle on the dash board may be used to apply the spring brakes gradually. This is called a modulating valve. It is spring-loaded, so you have a feel for the braking action. The more you move the control lever, the harder the spring brakes come on.

text: Air loss in a straight truck or bus should not be more than _____ with the engine off and the brakes applied.
explanation: With a fully-charged air system, turn off the engine, release the parking brake, and time the air pressure drop. The loss rate should be less than two psi in one minute for single vehicle brakes released, and three with the brakes applied.

text: What activates the stop switch?
explanation: Drivers behind you must be warned when you put your brakes on. The air brake system does this with an electric switch that works by air pressure. The switch turns on the brake lights when you put on the air brakes.

text: A straight truck or bus air brake system should not leak at a rate of more than ____ psi per minute with the engine off and the brakes released.
explanation: With a fully-charged air system, turn off the engine, release the parking brake, and time the air pressure drop. The loss rate should be less than two psi in one minute for single vehicle brakes released, and three with the brakes applied.

text: Vehicles with air brakes must have:
explanation: All vehicles with air brakes have a pressure gauge connected to the air tank. If the vehicle has a dual air brake system, there will be a gauge for each half of the system.

text: Slack adjusters should not have any more than _____ of play?
explanation: Park on level ground and chock the wheels to prevent the vehicle from moving, release the parking brakes so you can move the slack adjusters, using gloves and pull hard on each slack adjuster that you can reach, and if a slack adjuster moves more than about one inch where the push rod attaches to it then adjustments are needed.

text: What is the best way to test your vehicle's low air pressure warning signal?
explanation: Shut the engine off when you have enough air pressure so that the low-pressure warning signal is not on. Turn the electrical power on and step on and off the brake pedal to reduce air tank pressure. The low air pressure warning signal must come on before the pressure drops to less than 60 psi in the air tank.

text: Which vehicles must have low air pressure warning signals?
explanation: A low air pressure warning signal is required on vehicles with air brakes.

text: On a long or steep downgrade, once you have reached your "safe" speed, brake until you are traveling
explanation: Snub braking method is used for steep downhill grades, in this method apply the brakes just hard enough to feel a definite slowdown. When your speed has been reduced to approximately five mph below your "safe" speed, release the brakes. When your speed has increased to your "safe" speed, repeat.

text: To check the slack adjusters on S-cam drum brakes, you should first
explanation: To check slack adjusters on S-cam Brakes. Park on level ground and chock the wheels to prevent the vehicle from moving and release the parking brake.

text: Before starting down a hill, you should be in the proper gear
explanation: The use of brakes on a long and/or steep downgrade is only a supplement to the braking effect of the engine. Once the vehicle is in the proper low gear you should only have to apply the brakes just hard enough to feel a definite slowdown.

text: To apply the parking brakes under normal conditions,
explanation: A parking brake control in the cab allows the driver to let the air out of the spring brakes. This lets the springs put the brakes on.

text: Slack adjusters are
explanation: Slack adjusters are an important part of your air brake system that allows you to adjust the brakes to ensure that they are safe. They are located in different places, depending on the type of brakes that you have.

text: Which of the following is NOT part of the air brake system?
explanation: Air brakes are really three different braking systems: service brake, parking brake, and emergency brake.

text: Air braking takes more time than hydraulic braking because air brakes:
explanation: With air brakes there is an added delay called Brake Lag. This is the time required for the brakes to work after the brake pedal is pushed.

text: In air brake vehicles, the parking brakes should be used:
explanation: Any time you park use the parking brakes. Pull the parking brake control knob out to apply the parking brakes, push it in to release.

text: The brake pedal in an air brake system:
explanation: You put on the brakes by pushing down the brake pedal. Pushing the pedal down harder applies more air pressure. Letting up on the brake pedal reduces the air pressure and releases the brakes.

text: Your vehicle has a dual air brake system, if a low air pressure warning comes on for the secondary system, you should?
explanation: Even if the low-pressure alarm is for the secondary system you need to stop at a safe location, park, and get the issue fixed. It is unsafe for the vehicle to remain in motion with a low air alarm.

text: The tractor protection valve
explanation: The tractor protection valve keeps air in the tractor or truck brake system should the trailer break away or develop a bad leak, and it will close automatically if air pressure is low (typically 20-45 psi).

text: What is the first thing you should do if the low air pressure warning comes on?
explanation: If the low air pressure warning comes on, stop and safely park your vehicle as soon as possible.

text: When should you use the parking brake?
explanation: Never leave your vehicle unattended without applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels. Your vehicle might roll away and cause injury and damage. Exceptions are when the brakes are very hot, or wet and it is very cold outside. In this case use wheel chocks.

text: The supply pressure gauge shows the driver how much pressure:
explanation: All vehicles with air brakes have a pressure gauge connected to the air tank, and it tells you how much pressure is in the air tanks.

text: Which of the following is NOT part of the drum brake?
explanation: Drum brakes are on each of your vehicle's axles and contain about 10 different parts, including the axle, slack adjuster, and brake drum itself. While a safety valve is part of the air brake system, it is not part of the drum brake.

text: The safety release valve will blow at _____ psi?
explanation: A safety relief valve is installed in the first tank the air compressor pumps air to. The safety valve protects the tank and the rest of the system from too much pressure. The valve is usually set to open at 150 psi. If the safety valve releases air, something is wrong. Have the fault fixed by a mechanic.

text: In an emergency stop you should?
explanation: Stab braking means that you: apply your brakes all the way, and release brakes when wheels lock up. As soon as the wheels start rolling, apply the brakes fully again.

text: What are spring brakes?
explanation: When driving, powerful springs are held back by air pressure. If the air pressure is removed, the springs put on the brakes. A leak in the air brake system, which causes all the air to be lost, will also cause the springs to put on the brakes.

text: When should you use the hand valve to park a combination vehicle?
explanation: The trailer hand valve, also called the trolley valve or Johnson bar, works the trailer brakes. The trailer hand valve should be used only to test the trailer brakes. Never use the hand valve for parking because all the air might leak out unlocking the brakes.

text: Which answer is correct for the air valves when testing the trailer service brakes only?
explanation: Check for normal air pressure, release the parking brakes, release the service brake, make sure the trailer air supply is in / open, move the vehicle forward slowly, and apply trailer brakes with the hand control or trolley valve. You should feel the brakes come on. This tells you the trailer brakes are connected and working.

text: You are coupling a semi-trailer to your tractor but have not yet backed under. The trailer is at the right height when:
explanation: The trailer should be low enough that it is raised slightly by the tractor when the tractor is backed under it. Raise or lower the trailer as needed. If the trailer is too low, the tractor may strike and damage the trailer nose; if the trailer is too high, it may not couple correctly.

text: How should you test the tractor trailer connection for security?
explanation: Raise trailer landing gear slightly off ground. Pull tractor gently forward while the trailer brakes are still locked to check that the trailer is locked onto the tractor.

text: Which answer is correct for the air valves when testing the trailer spring / parking brakes only?
explanation: To test the trailer spring brakes, you need to have the service brake off or released, parking brake off, trailer air supply out shutting the door to the trailer, and trolley released.

text: Your emergency air line breaks or gets pulled apart while you are driving. The loss of pressure will cause the:
explanation: A major leak in the emergency line will cause the tractor protection valve to close and the trailer emergency brakes to come on. But the brakes will hold only if there is air pressure in the trailer air tank.

text: You supply air to the trailer tanks by:
explanation: From cab, push in air supply knob or move tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position to supply air to the trailer brake system.

text: There are two things that a driver can do to prevent a roll over. They are: (1) keep the cargo as close to the ground as possible; and (2):
explanation: The following two things will help you prevent rollover--keep the cargo as close to the ground as possible and drive slowly around turns.

text: The fifth wheel locking lever is not locked after the jaws close around the kingpin; this means:
explanation: Check that the locking lever is in the lock position. If the locking lever does not go into the locked position the coupling is not complete.

text: When backing your tractor under a trailer, you should expect:
explanation: The trailer should be low enough that it is raised slightly by the tractor when the tractor is backed under it. If the trailer is too low, the tractor may strike and damage the trailer nose; if the trailer is too high, it may not couple correctly.

text: Before you start to uncouple, you must
explanation: Make sure surface of parking area can support weight of trailer.

text: During uncoupling, you should disconnect the electrical cable and
explanation: Hang electrical cable with plug down to prevent moisture from entering it.

text: As part of your vehicle inspection test, if your vehicle is equipped with air brakes and has a trailer, you will inspect the air connections between the truck or tractor and the trailer. Make sure that the ________ are locked in place and free of damage or air leaks.
explanation: Glad hands are coupling devices used to connect the service and emergency air-lines from the truck or tractor to the trailer.

text: If your vehicle gets stuck on a railroad track, you should
explanation: If for any reason you get stuck on the tracks, get out of the vehicle and away from the tracks. Check signposts or signal housing at the crossing for emergency notification information. Call 911 or another emergency number. Give the location of the crossing using all identifiable landmarks, especially the DOT number, if posted.

text: If your test of the tractor protection valve is successful,
explanation: The tractor protection valve keeps air in the tractor or truck brake system should the trailer break away or develop a bad leak. The tractor protection valve is controlled by the "trailer air supply" control valve in the cab. The control valve allows you to open and shut the tractor protection valve. The tractor protection valve will close automatically if air pressure is low (in the range of 20 to 45 psi). When the tractor protection valve closes, it stops any air from going out of the tractor.

text: You should not use the trailer hand brake to straighten out a jackknifing trailer, because
explanation: When the wheels of a trailer lock up, the trailer will tend to swing around. This is more likely to happen when the trailer is empty or lightly loaded. This type of jackknife is often called a "trailer jackknife." Applying the trailer brakes would extend the skid and trailer jackknife.

text: You should not use the trailer hand valve while driving because
explanation: Do not use it in driving because of the danger of making the trailer skid.

text: A trailer is most likely to jackknife when it is
explanation: Large combination vehicles take longer to stop when they are empty than when they are fully loaded. When empty, the very stiff suspension springs and strong brakes give poor traction and make it very easy to lock up the wheels causing the tractor to jackknife.

text: When backing a tractor under a trailer, you should
explanation: Use lowest reverse gear. Back tractor slowly under trailer to avoid hitting the kingpin too hard, and stop when the kingpin is locked into the fifth wheel.

text: The best way to tell if your trailer has started to skid is to
explanation: The earliest way to notice that your trailer is jackknifing is to see it swinging out in your mirrors.

text: Describe what the trailer air supply control does.
explanation: The trailer air supply control on newer vehicles is a red eight-sided knob, which you use to control the tractor protection valve. You push it in to supply the trailer with air, and pull it out to shut the air off and put on the trailer emergency brakes.

text: If your vehicle is equipped with dummy couplers, why should you connect the glad hands to them?
explanation: Some vehicles have "dead end" or dummy couplers to which the hoses may be attached when they are not in use. This will prevent water and dirt from getting into the coupler and the air-lines.

text: When coupling a tractor-semitrailer, to test the trailer air brakes, you should place the tractor protection valve control in what position?
explanation: To test the trailer air brake system, first charge it with air. Push in the red trailer air supply knob or place the tractor protection valve control in the "normal" position. Wait until the air pressure reaches normal before you start your tests.

text: Trucks roll over more easily when fully loaded and are
explanation: When more cargo is piled up in a truck, the "center of gravity" moves higher up from the road. The truck becomes easier to turn over. Fully loaded rigs are ten times more likely to roll over in a crash than empty rigs.

text: To stop a trailer skid, you should
explanation: Once you realize your vehicle is in a skid, you should release the brakes so that the wheels can grip the road again.

text: After you supply air to the trailer, make sure the airlines are not crossed and the trailer brakes are working. This is done by:
explanation: Shut the engine off so you can hear the brakes. Apply and release trailer brakes and listen for sounds of trailer brakes being applied and released. You should hear the brakes move when applied and air escape when the brakes are released.

text: The air leakage rate for a combination vehicle (engine off, brakes off) should be no more than ____ PSI per minute.
explanation: With a fully-charged air system, turn off the engine, release the parking brake; and time the air pressure drop. The loss rate should be less than three psi in one minute for combination vehicles, we do not add the additional second here because the brakes are released.

text: If the service airline comes apart while you are driving a combination vehicle, but the emergency line stays together, what will happen right away?
explanation: You may not notice a major leak in the service line until you try to put the brakes on. Then, the air loss from the leak will lower the air tank pressure quickly. If it goes low enough, the trailer emergency brakes will come on.

text: You are coupling a tractor and semi-trailer and have connected the air lines. Before backing under the trailer, you should:
explanation: From cab, push in air supply knob or move tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position to supply air to the trailer brake system. Check brake system for crossed air-lines. Shut engine off so you can hear the brakes. Check air brake system pressure gauge for signs of major air loss.

text: You have coupled with a semi-trailer. In what position should you put the front trailer supports before driving away?
explanation: Use low gear range to begin raising the landing gear, once free of weight, switch to the high gear range. Raise the landing gear all the way up. Never drive with landing gear only part way up as it may catch on railroad tracks or other things. After raising landing gear, secure the crank handle safely.

text: The driver crosses the airlines when hooking up to an old trailer. What will happen?
explanation: If the trailer has no spring brakes you would be able to drive away, but service breaks for the trailer only will not work.

text: Why should you be sure that the fifth wheel plate is greased as required?
explanation: Be sure that the fifth wheel plate is greased as required. Failure to keep the fifth wheel plate lubricated could cause steering problems because of friction between the tractor and trailer.

text: To prevent rollover, cargo should be
explanation: As part of your Vehicle inspection, make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured properly.

text: Where should the tractor be when you inspect the landing gear after uncoupling the trailer?
explanation: Pull tractor forward until fifth wheel comes out from under the trailer but stop with tractor frame under trailer preventing the trailer from falling to ground if landing gear should collapse or sink.

text: An antilock braking system (ABS) is useful
explanation: Brake the same way, regardless of whether you have ABS on the tractor, the trailer, or both.

text: You should use chocks when parking a trailer without spring brakes because
explanation: Trailers that do not have spring brakes have emergency brakes, which work from the air stored in the trailer air tank. A major leak in the emergency line will cause the tractor protection valve to close and the trailer emergency brakes to come on, but the brakes will hold only if there is air pressure in the trailer air tank. Eventually, the air will leak away and then there will be no brakes. Therefore, it is very important for safety that you use wheel chocks when you park trailers without spring brakes.

text: To unlock the fifth wheel, pull the release handle to the _______ position.
explanation: To unlock the fifth-wheel pull the release handle to "open" position.

text: What is a tractor jackknife?
explanation: Rear wheel braking skids occur when the rear drive wheels lock. Because locked wheels have less traction than rolling wheels, the rear wheels usually slide sideways to "catch up" with the front wheels. With vehicles towing trailers, a drive-wheel skid can let the trailer push the towing vehicle sideways, causing a sudden jackknife.

text: What gear should the tractor engine be in after you uncouple the trailer and are inspecting the trailer supports?
explanation: Before inspection the trailer while coupling you need to secure the vehicle. Put transmission in neutral. Put parking brakes on. Shut off engine and take key with you so someone else won't move truck while you are under it.

text: The air leakage rate for a combination vehicle (engine off, brakes on) should not be more than ____ PSI per minute.
explanation: With a fully-charged air system, turn off the engine, release the parking brake; and time the air pressure drop. The loss rate should be less than three psi in one minute for combination vehicles, adding one second if the brakes are applied.

text: Why should you lock the tractor glad hands to each other or to the storage bracket when you are not towing a trailer?
explanation: If there are no dummy couplers, the glad hands can sometimes be locked together. It is very important to keep the air supply clean and free of moisture.

text: You are driving a combination vehicle when the trailer breaks away, pulling apart both air lines. You would expect the trailer brakes to come on and:
explanation: The tractor protection valve keeps air in the tractor or truck brake system should the trailer break away or develop a bad leak. The tractor protection valve is controlled by the trailer air supply control valve in the cab. The control valve allows you to open and shut the tractor protection valve. The tractor protection valve will close automatically if air pressure is low (in the range of 20 to 45 psi). When the tractor protection valve closes, it stops any air from going out of the tractor. It also lets the air out of the trailer emergency line. This causes the trailer emergency brakes to come on, with possible loss of control.

text: The hand valve should be used:
explanation: The trailer hand valve, also called the trolley valve or Johnson bar, works the trailer brakes. The trailer hand valve should be used only to test the trailer brakes.

text: Combination vehicles take longer to stop when they are empty than when they are fully loaded, because
explanation: Large combination vehicles take longer to stop when they are empty than when they are fully loaded. When lightly loaded, the very stiff suspension springs and strong brakes give poor traction and make it very easy to lock up the wheels.

text: To uncouple a loaded trailer, after the landing gear has made firm contact with the ground, you should
explanation: If trailer is loaded, after the landing gear makes firm contact with the ground, turn crank in low gear a few extra turns. This will lift some weight off the tractor making it easier to unlatch fifth wheel, and to recouple to the trailer.

text: The tractor protection valve is designed to close automatically when the air pressure falls into the pressure range specified by the manufacturer, typically
explanation: – 45 psi.

text: If the brakes did not release when you pushed the trailer air supply valve, you should
explanation: If you do cross the air-lines, supply air will be sent to the service line instead of going to charge the trailer air tanks. Air will not be available to release the trailer spring brakes. If the spring brakes don't release when you push the trailer air supply control, check the air-line connections.

text: In general, the higher your truck's center of gravity, the
explanation: The height of the vehicle's center of gravity is very important for safe handling. A high center of gravity means you are more likely to tip over.

text: When you test the tractor protection valve, the red air supply control knob should go into the ________ position.
explanation: –45 psi, the trailer air supply control knob should pop out, or the valve control should go from the "normal" to the "emergency" position.

text: When coupling, the proper position of the fifth wheel is
explanation: Check if fifth wheel is in proper position for coupling: The fifth wheel should be tilted down toward rear of tractor.

text: The front trailer supports are up, and the trailer is resting on the tractor, make sure:
explanation: Check for enough clearance between rear of tractor frame and landing gear and  between the top of the tractor tires and the nose of the trailer.

text: You are coupling a tractor to a semi-trailer and have backed up but are not under  it. What should you hook up before backing under?
explanation: Check glad hand seals and connect tractor emergency and service air-line to trailer emergency and service glad hand. Make sure air-lines are safely supported where they won't be crushed or caught while tractor is backing under the trailer.

text: Before you back under a trailer, make sure the:
explanation: Pull out the air supply knob or move the tractor protection valve control from normal to emergency.

text: On a double or triple vehicle, which wheels off-track the most?
explanation: If there is more than one trailer, the rear wheels of the last trailer will off-track the most

text: Which statement best illustrates the "crack-the-whip" effect?
explanation: Trucks with trailers have a dangerous crack-the-whip effect caused by rearward amplification. When you make a quick lane change, the crack-the-whip effect can turn the trailer over. There are many accidents where only the trailer has overturned.

text: To test the tractor protection valve, charge the trailer air brake system, turn off the engine, and
explanation: – 45 psi. This will cause the spring brakes to come on.

text: Which of these vehicles off-tracks the most?
explanation: When a vehicle goes around a corner the rear wheels follow a different path than the front wheels, and this is called off-tracking or "cheating." Longer vehicles will off-track more. If there is more than one trailer, the rear wheels of the last trailer will off-track the most.

text: Almost half of all truck driver deaths are the result of
explanation: More than half of truck driver deaths in crashes are the result of truck rollovers.

text: At night, when a vehicle is carrying a load that extends four feet or more beyond the rear of the body, there must be _______ at the extreme end of the load.
explanation: According to federal regulations, if the load projects more than 4 feet behind the rear of the vehicle, the end of the load must be marked as follows: (1) with red flags, each 18 inches square, in the daytime; or (2) with two red lights at night.

text: In normal driving, some drivers use the hand valve before applying the brake pedal to prevent a jackknife. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: The trailer hand valve, also called the trolley valve or Johnson bar, works the trailer brakes. The trailer hand valve should be used only to test the trailer brakes. Do not use it in driving because of the danger of making the trailer skid.

text: You should not back a tractor under a trailer until the whole air system is:
explanation: Make sure air pressure is up to normal.

text: After you supply air to the trailer, make sure the airlines are not crossed and the trailer brakes are working. This can be done by:
explanation: Wait until the air pressure is normal, then shut engine off so you can hear the brakes. Check brake system for crossed air-lines by: applying and releasing trailer brakes and listen for sound of trailer brakes being applied and released. You should hear the brakes move when applied and air escape when the brakes are released.

text: What is the safest way for you to make a turn without entering another traffic lane?
explanation: If you are driving a truck or bus that cannot make the right turn without swinging into another lane, turn wide as you complete the turn. Keep the rear of your vehicle close to the curb. This will stop other drivers from passing you on the right.

text: Even before antilock braking systems (ABS) were required, some vehicles already had them. How can you check if your vehicle has ABS?
explanation: It may be difficult to tell if some units are equipped with ABS. Look under the vehicle for the electronic control unit (ECU) and wheel speed sensor wires coming from the back of the brakes.

text: What is the emergency air line?
explanation: The emergency line also called the supply line has two purposes. First, it supplies air to the trailer air tanks. Second, the emergency line controls the emergency brakes on combination vehicles

text: You are uncoupling a trailer. After you shut off the trailer air supply and lock the trailer brakes, you should
explanation: Shut off trailer air supply to lock trailer brakes. Ease pressure on fifth wheel locking jaws by backing up gently, applying the parking brake while tractor is pushing against the kingpin.

text: A tractor with a(n) _______ trailer requires the shortest amount of stopping distance.
explanation: The heavier the vehicle, the more work the brakes must do to stop it, and the more heat they absorb. But the brakes, tires, springs, and shock absorbers on heavy vehicles are designed to work best when the vehicle is fully loaded. Empty trucks require greater stopping distances because an empty vehicle has less traction.

text: The tractor protection valve will close and the trailer _______ brakes will come on when there is a major leak in the brake line.
explanation: When the tractor protection valve closes, it stops any air from going out of the tractor. It also lets the air out of the trailer emergency line. This causes the trailer emergency brakes to come on

text: You have a major leak in the service line, and you put on the brakes. Service air pressure will escape and cause the:
explanation: You may not notice a major leak in the service line until you try to put the brakes on. Then, the air loss from the leak will lower the air tank pressure quickly. If it goes low enough, the trailer emergency brakes will come on.

text: Semi-trailers made before 1975 that are equipped with air brakes:
explanation: Converter dollies and trailers built before 1975 are not required to have spring brakes.

text: How much space should be between the apron and fifth wheel after coupling?
explanation: Make sure there is no space between upper and lower fifth wheel. If there is space, something is wrong, the kingpin may be on top of the closed fifth wheel jaws, and trailer would come loose very easily.

text: On a trailer, where is the yellow antilock brake (ABS) malfunction lamp located?
explanation: Trailers will have yellow ABS malfunction lamps on the left side, either on the front or rear corner.

text: What are the other names for the service air line?
explanation: The service line, also called the control line or signal line, carries air controlled by the foot brake or the trailer hand brake.

text: You should not back a tractor under a trailer until the whole air system is
explanation: Make sure air pressure is up to normal before backing under the trailer.

text: Air and electrical lines from the tractor to the trailer should be
explanation: Make sure air and electrical lines are not tangled, pinched, dragging against tractor parts, and have enough slack for turns.

text: Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Be very careful about driving bobtail tractors or tractors without semitrailers. Tests have shown that bobtails can be very hard to stop smoothly and can take longer to stop than a tractor-semitrailer loaded to maximum gross weight.

text: Which part of the kingpin should the locking jaws close around?
explanation: Go under trailer and investigate the back of the fifth wheel. Make sure the fifth wheel jaws have closed around the shank of the kingpin.

text: Under good driving conditions, you should leave at least one second of space between your vehicle and the vehicle ahead for every ___ feet of your vehicle's length.
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: You supply air to the trailer tanks by
explanation: The emergency line, also called the supply line, has two purposes. First, it supplies air to the trailer air tanks. Second, the emergency line controls the emergency brakes on combination vehicles.

text: When you get ready to back under the semi-trailer you should line up:
explanation: Put the tractor directly in front of the trailer. Never back under the trailer at an angle because you might push the trailer sideways and break the landing gear.

text: Air lines on a combination vehicle are often color coded to keep them from getting mixed up. The emergency line is _____; the service line is _____.
explanation: Emergency lines are often coded with red (red hose, red couplers, or other parts) to keep from getting them mixed up with the blue service line.

text: Low underneath clearance vehicles can be risky at railroad crossings because
explanation: Railroad tracks can also cause problems, particularly when pulling trailers with a low underneath clearance. Don't take a chance on getting hung up halfway across.

text: After you have coupled the trailer, you should start to raise the landing gear by using
explanation: After the landing gear makes firm contact with the ground, turn crank in low gear a few extra turns. This will lift some weight off the tractor making it easier to unlatch fifth wheel, and to recouple to the trailer.

text: At what angle do you hold your airline glad hands when connecting them?
explanation: When connecting the glad hands, press the two seals together with the couplers at a 90-degree angle to each other.

text: If you do not do the final air brake check for air leaks and fix them before driving,
explanation: If the air pressure falls more than the specified psi in one minute for combination vehicles, the air loss rate is too much. Check for air leaks and fix before driving the vehicle. Otherwise, you could lose your brakes while driving.

text: Which type of truck-trailer combination has the greatest chance of a "crack-the-whip" rollover?
explanation: Triples have a rearward amplification of 3.5. This means you can roll the last trailer of triples 3.5 times as easily as a five-axle tractor. While the rearward amplification of a double is around 2.

text: Compared with a straight truck or bus, there are _______ things to inspect in combination vehicles.
explanation: There are more things to inspect on a combination vehicle than on a bus or straight truck. First, of course, there is the entire coupling system: the kingpin, the fifth wheel, etc. Next, there is the landing gear of each trailer. Not to mention a few more axels to check.

text: If the trailer has antilock brakes (ABS) but the tractor doesn't,
explanation: When only the trailer has ABS, the trailer is less likely to swing out, but if you lose steering control or start a tractor jackknife, let up on the brakes if you can safely do so until you regain control.

text: The safety catch for the fifth wheel locking lever must be _____ for a coupling to be complete.
explanation: Check that the safety latch is in position over locking lever. On some fifth wheels the catch must be put in place by hand.

text: To drive a triple combination vehicle, you must have
explanation: To legally pull a trailer with a GVWR exceeding 10,000 pounds, you'll need a Class A CDL. To pull triples, the  doubles/triples endorsement on your CDL is also required.

text: You should check the height of the trailer before connecting a converter dolly to a second or third trailer. The trailer height is right when:
explanation: Make sure trailer height is correct. It must be slightly lower than the center of the fifth wheel, so trailer is raised slightly when dolly is pushed under.

text: The best way to stop all skids is to:
explanation: When any vehicle starts to skid, you must act to restore traction to the wheels.

text: When does the drivers manual advise disconnecting the steering axle brakes to help keep the rig straight?
explanation: You should never disconnect the steering axel brakes.

text: When driving with more than one trailer, which trailer should be the first one behind the tractor?
explanation: For the safest handling on the road, the more heavily loaded semitrailer should be in first position behind the tractor. The lighter trailer should be in the rear.

text: Driving a truck with double or triple trailers requires a driver to:
explanation: Be more careful in adverse conditions like bad weather, slippery conditions, and mountain driving, you must be especially careful if you drive double and triple trailers. You will have greater length and more dead axles to pull with your drive axles than other drivers. There is more chance for skids and loss of traction.

text: Which of these will result in the best control on curves?
explanation: Slow down to a safe speed, and downshift to the right gear before entering the curve. This lets you use some power through the curve to help the vehicle be more stable while turning.

text: You are driving a 100' vehicle with double trailers at 30 MPH. The road is dry, and visibility is good. You should keep at least _____ seconds of space ahead of you:
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: You are driving with double trailers and must use your brakes to avoid a crash. For emergency braking you should:
explanation: You should brake in a way that will keep your vehicle in a straight line and allow you to turn if it becomes necessary. You can use the controlled braking method or the stab braking method.

text: You are doing a walk around inspection of a double or triple trailer rig. You should be sure the converter dolly air tank drain valves are _____ and the pintle hook is _____.
explanation: Converter dolly air tank drain valve are closed, and make sure pintle hook is latched.

text: A converter dolly is
explanation: A converter gear on a dolly is a coupling device of one or two axles and a fifth wheel by which a semitrailer can be coupled to the rear of a tractor trailer combination forming a double bottom rig.

text: If a converter dolly has spring brakes, you release the brakes by
explanation: Release dolly brakes by opening the air tank petcock, or, if the dolly has spring brakes, use the dolly parking brake control.

text: You have connected the converter dolly to the front semitrailer and then connected the rear semitrailer to the converter dolly. How should you test the coupling?
explanation: Test coupling by pulling against pin of the rear semitrailer

text: If your landing gear is power operated, you should check for ________ during your walk-around inspection.
explanation: If the landing gear is power operated, make sure there are no air or hydraulic leaks.

text: Coupling a triple trailer is different from coupling other combinations because
explanation: A triple combination vehicle has four units (not counting the converter dollies): a tractor and three trailers. You couple a triple as follows: 1. Couple the first trailer to the tractor the same way that you couple any combination vehicle. 2. Couple the third trailer to the second trailer using the method for coupling doubles (which will involve the use of a converter dolly). 3. Move another converter dolly into position. 3. Couple the tractor and first trailer to the second and third trailer using the method for coupling doubles (which will involve another converter dolly).

text: In order, the four steps to test your trailer's service brakes are as follows
explanation: Wait for normal air pressure, release the parking brake, move the vehicle forward slowly about five mph, and apply the brakes firmly using the trailers hand control. Note any vehicle pulling to one side, unusual feel, or delayed stopping action.

text: To uncouple the rear trailer, the first thing you do is
explanation: Park rig in a straight line on firm level ground.

text: You are driving a 100-foot twin trailer combination at 50 mph. The road is dry, and visibility is good. You should keep at least _____ seconds of space ahead of you.
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: Some large trucks have convex or spot mirrors.
explanation: The convex mirrors are located below the outside flat mirrors. They are used to monitor the left and right sides at a wide angle. They provide a view of traffic and clearances. These mirrors present a view of people and objects that does not accurately reflect their size and distance.

text: Which of these statements about managing space to the sides is true?
explanation: Commercial vehicles are often wide and take up most of a lane. Safe drivers will manage what little space they have. You can do this by keeping your vehicle centered in your lane and avoid driving alongside others.

text: Which of these statements about handling of doubles and triples is true?
explanation: To prevent trailers from rolling over, you must steer gently and go slowly around corners, on ramps, off ramps, and curves. A safe speed on a curve for a straight truck or a single trailer combination vehicle may be too fast for a set of doubles or triples.

text: When should you check your mirrors while making a lane change?
explanation: Check your mirrors: Before you change lanes to make sure there is enough room. After you have signaled, to check that no one has moved into your blind spot. Right after you start the lane change, to doublecheck that your path is clear. After you complete the lane change.

text: Before you can supply air to the air tanks of a second trailer you need to:
explanation: Shut-off valves at rear of trailers, in service and emergency lines. Rear of front trailers: open. Rear of last trailer: closed.

text: If you must drive through deep puddles or flowing water, which of these should you not do?
explanation: Slow down and place transmission in a low gear. Gently put on the brakes. This presses linings against brake drums or discs and keeps mud, silt, sand, and water from getting in.

text: During an inspection of a double or triple combination, check the usual things for any combination vehicle and check
explanation: To inspect a double or triple, first inspect it just as you would inspect any combination vehicle. Then check that the shut-off valves are open on all trailers but the last trailer. Check each converter dolly to be sure that the pintle hook is properly latched.

text: When you pull two or three trailers, you should look farther ahead because
explanation: Doubles and triples must be driven very smoothly to avoid rollover or jackknife. Therefore, look far ahead so you can slow down or change lanes gradually when necessary.

text: If you want to couple a second trailer to your combination vehicle, secure it by using?
explanation: Make sure trailer brakes are locked and/or wheels chocked.

text: Which of these statements about quick steering movements and doubles / triples is true?
explanation: Rearward amplification causes the crack-the-whip effect. Triples have a rearward amplification of 3.5 meaning you can roll the last trailer of triples 3.5 times as easily as a five-axle tractor. A Double has a reward amplification of 2 being twice as likely to roll over as a five-axel trailer.

text: The best way to keep brakes from overheating while going down a steep grade is to:
explanation: The use of brakes on a long and/or steep downgrade is only a supplement to the braking effect of the engine. The following are the proper braking techniques: Apply the brakes just hard enough to feel a definite slowdown. When your speed has been reduced to approximately five mph below your "safe" speed, release the brakes. When your speed has increased to your "safe" speed, repeat.

text: Converter dollies:
explanation: Converter dollies often do not have spring brakes.

text: You are driving a set of doubles, and a set of trailer wheels goes into a skid. Which of these is most likely to occur?
explanation: Doubles and triples must be driven very smoothly to avoid rollover or jackknife. Therefore, look far ahead so you can slow down or change lanes gradually when necessary.

text: Antilock brakes (ABS) have been required on all converter dollies built since
explanation: Truck tractors with air brakes built on or after March 1, 1997 require ABS, and other air brake vehicles such as trucks, buses, trailers, and converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998 require ABS.

text: Why must you inspect your double or triple combination vehicle more extensively than a vehicle with only one such semitrailer?
explanation: There are more things to inspect on a double or triple combination vehicle than on a single vehicle. Many of these items are simply more of what you would find on a single vehicle. For example, tires, wheels, lights, reflectors, etc. However, there are also some new things to check.

text: Which of these statements about seeing ahead is true?
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds. At lower speeds, that's about one block. At highway speeds it's about a quarter of a mile.

text: The crack the whip effects trucks with trailers. Which of the following is most likely to tip over?
explanation: Due to rearward amplification a double is twice as likely, and a triple is 3.5 times more likely to roll over than a five-axel trailer.

text: A converter dolly consists of a ______ wheel and ________ axles.
explanation: A converter gear on a dolly is a coupling device of one or two axles and a fifth wheel by which a semitrailer can be coupled to the rear of a tractor trailer combination forming a double bottom rig.

text: The first step in uncoupling a converter dolly is
explanation: Lower dolly landing gear.

text: How can you be sure you supplied air to a second trailer?
explanation: Use the tractor parking brake and/or chock the wheels to hold the vehicle. Wait for air pressure to reach normal, then push in the red "trailer air supply" knob. This will supply air to the emergency (supply) lines. Open the emergency line shut-off valve at the rear of the last trailer. You should hear air escaping, showing the entire system is charged.

text: If a converter dolly is still under the second trailer and you unlock the pintle hook, what will probably happen?
explanation: Never unlock the pintle hook with the dolly still under the rear trailer. The dolly tow bar may fly up, possibly causing injury, and making it very difficult to re-couple.

text: You are visually checking the coupling of a converter dolly to the rear trailer. How much space should be between the upper and lower fifth wheel?
explanation: Check that the trailer is lying flat on the fifth wheel skid plate with no gap.

text: When you uncouple twin trailers, the last step is to
explanation: Slowly pull clear of dolly.

text: Converter dollies
explanation: All converter dollies built since 1998 are required to have antilock brakes.

text: You want to hook your combination to a second trailer that has no spring brakes. To do this without wheel chocks you should:
explanation: If the second trailer doesn't have spring brakes, drive the tractor close to the trailer, connect the emergency line, charge the trailer air tank, and disconnect the emergency line. This will set the trailer emergency brakes if the slack adjusters are correctly adjusted.

text: Empty trucks:
explanation: Empty trucks require greater stopping distances because an empty vehicle has less traction.

text: When parking your double or triple combination, you should look for a parking space
explanation: Make sure you do not get in a spot you cannot pull straight through. You need to be aware of how parking lots are arranged to avoid a long and difficult escape.

text: With the hand valve on, you should test the trailer brakes by opening the service line valve at the rear of the rig. When you do this, you should hear:
explanation: Open the service line valve, listen for air to escape, and then close the valve.

text: Which of these in not a good thing to do when driving on slippery roads?
explanation: When your drive wheels have poor traction, the retarder may cause them to skid. Therefore, you should turn the retarder off whenever the road is wet, icy, or snow covered.

text: Double and triple combination vehicles are ________ stable than other types of commercial vehicles.
explanation: Take special care when pulling two and three trailers. There are more things that can go wrong, and doubles/triples are less stable than other commercial vehicles.

text: Whenever you stop for a break during your trip, you should
explanation: For an e-route inspection: check the tires, wheels, and truck body for signs of heat whenever you stop during a trip.

text: High beams should be:
explanation: Use high beams when it is safe and legal to do so. Use them when you are not within 500 feet of an approaching vehicle.

text: The safety release valve is located on the Air tanks and is used by?
explanation: A safety relief valve is installed in the first tank the air compressor pumps air to. The safety valve protects the tank and the rest of the system from too much pressure. The valve is usually set to open automatically at 150 psi.

text: Which of these is a proper use of vehicle lights and flashers?
explanation: Warn following drivers by flashing your brake lights, don't stop suddenly. If you see a hazard that will require slowing down, warn the drivers behind by flashing your brake lights. At dawn, dusk, in rain, or snow, you need to make yourself easier to see so turn on your lights.

text: Which of the following is a key steering component?
explanation: The gear box is part of the steering system, and other choices are parts of the suspension system.

text: How can you start moving without rolling backward?
explanation: Don't roll back when you start. You may hit someone behind you. If you have a manual transmission vehicle, partly engage the clutch before you take your right foot off the brake. Put on the parking brake whenever necessary to keep from rolling back. Release the parking brake only when you have applied enough engine power to keep from rolling back.

text: Which is NOT one of the four basic skills that operating a commercial motor vehicle requires?
explanation: To drive a vehicle safely, you must be able to control its speed and direction. Safe operation of a commercial vehicle requires skill in: accelerating, steering, stopping, and backing safely.

text: What is the average reaction distance?
explanation: ¾ second to 1 second. At 55 mph this accounts for 61 feet traveled.

text: The most common cause of serious vehicle skids is:
explanation: A skid happens whenever the tires lose their grip on the road. Most serious skids result from driving too fast for road conditions. Drivers who adjust their driving to conditions don't overaccelerate and don't have to over-brake or oversteer from too much speed.

text: The distance that you should look ahead of your vehicle while driving amounts to about _____ at highway speed.
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds. At lower speeds, that's about one block. At highway speeds it's about a quarter of a mile.

text: You must park on the side of a level, straight, two lane road. Where should you place the three reflective triangles?
explanation: If you stop on a two-lane road carrying traffic in both directions or on an undivided highway, place warning devices within 10 feet of the front or rear corners to mark the location of the vehicle and 100 feet behind and ahead of the vehicle, on the shoulder or in the lane you stopped in.

text: If you are convicted for driving under the influence while driving a commercial vehicle, and this is your first offense, you will most likely get:
explanation: You will lose your CDL for at least one year for a first offense for: Driving a CMV if your blood alcohol concentration is .04% or higher or driving a CMV under the influence of alcohol.

text: Front end header boards:
explanation: Front-end header boards ("headache racks") protect you from your cargo in case of a crash or emergency stop. The front-end structure should block the forward movement of any cargo you carry.

text: The formula for stopping distance with air brakes is?
explanation: Perception Distance + Reaction Distance + Brake Lag Distance + Braking Distance = Total Stopping Distance.

text: Which of these statements about driving in areas with strong winds is true?
explanation: Strong winds make it difficult to stay in your lane. The problem is usually worse for lighter vehicles. This problem can be especially bad coming out of tunnels.

text: Which of these statements about crossing or entering traffic with a heavy vehicle is true?
explanation: Because of slow acceleration and the space large vehicles require, you may need a much larger gap to enter traffic than you would in a car. Acceleration varies with the load, allow more room if your vehicle is heavily loaded. Before you start across a road, make sure you can get all the way across before traffic reaches you.

text: If a vehicle continues to drive in your blind spot you should?
explanation: Backing off just means you slow down, if the vehicle was in a blind spot this change in speed should force it out of the blind spot.

text: You are driving a 40-foot vehicle at 45 mph. Driving conditions are ideal. What is the least amount of space that you should keep in front of your vehicle to be safe?
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: Axle weight means the following?
explanation: Axle Weight is the weight transmitted to the ground by one axle or one set of axles

text: The new BAC or blood alcohol concentration for commercial drivers to be considered intoxicated when driving a commercial vehicle is:
explanation: It is illegal to operate a CMV if your blood alcohol concentration (BAC) is .04% or more. If you operate a CMV, you shall be deemed to have given your consent to alcohol testing.

text: Which of these statements about brakes is true?
explanation: Applying the brakes causes friction to slow the vehicle, the faster it is moving, or the heavier it is the more heat that must be absorbed to stop the vehicle.

text: Your vehicle is in a traffic emergency and may collide with another vehicle if you do not act. Which of these is a good rule to remember at such a time?
explanation: When you don't have enough room to stop, you may have to steer away from what's ahead. Remember, you can almost always turn to miss an obstacle more quickly than you can stop.

text: A moving vehicle ahead of you has a red triangle with an orange center on the rear. What does this mean?
explanation: Some vehicles, by their nature, are slow and some of these will have the "slow moving vehicle" symbol to warn you. This is a red triangle with an orange center.

text: The parking brake should be tested while the vehicle is:
explanation: To test the parking brakes: Fasten safety belt, set parking brake, release trailer parking brake, Place vehicle into a low gear, gently pull forward against parking brake to make sure the parking brake holds. If it doesn't hold vehicle, it is faulty; get it fixed.

text: A driver's grid log, if required:
explanation: Enforcement of the HOS (Hours of Service) rules is generally handled by DOT officers of their respective states, although any ordinary police officer may inspect a driver's log book.

text: Under-loaded front axles can cause the following:
explanation: Under-loaded front axles can make the steering axle weight too light to steer safely.

text: What should you do when your vehicle hydroplanes?
explanation: You can regain control by releasing the accelerator and pushing in the clutch. This will slow your vehicle and let the wheels turn freely. If the vehicle is hydroplaning, do not use the brakes to slow down.

text: You are traveling down a long, steep hill. Your brakes get so hot that they fail. What should you do?
explanation: Know escape ramp locations on your route. Signs show drivers where ramp are located. Escape ramps save lives, equipment and cargo.

text: Which of these statements about certain types of cargo is true?
explanation: Hanging meat can be a very unstable load with a high center of gravity, particular caution is needed on sharp curves such as off ramps and on ramps. Livestock can move around in a trailer, causing unsafe handling, and special care is necessary because livestock can lean on curves.

text: You do not have a hazardous materials endorsement on your commercial driver's license. When can you drive a vehicle hauling hazardous materials?
explanation: You can drive a vehicle that carries hazardous materials if it does not require placards. If it requires placards, you cannot drive it unless your driver license has the hazardous materials endorsement.

text: You should stop driving:
explanation: If you look sleepy, or you just feel sleepy, stop driving! You are in a very dangerous condition. The only safe cure is to sleep.

text: The amount of liquid to load into a tank depends on:
explanation: A full tank of dense liquid (such as some acids) may exceed legal weight limits. The amount of liquid to load into a tank depends on: the amount the liquid will expand in transit, the weight of the liquid, legal weight limits.

text: The basic two reasons to cover your cargo are 1. to protect the cargo from the weather and 2.
explanation: There are two basic reasons for covering cargo: to protect people from spilled cargo, and to protect the cargo from weather. Spill protection is a safety requirement in many states. Be familiar with the laws in the states you drive in.

text: There are three types of vehicle inspections, which one is one of them?
explanation: Pre-trip is an inspection before you leave, en-route occurs during your daily travel, and post-trip happens at the end of your day.

text: Inspect your cargo after you have driven
explanation: Inspect the cargo and its securing devices within the first 50 miles after beginning a trip. Make any adjustments needed.

text: According to the driver's manual, why should you limit the use of your horn?
