explanation: Your horn can let others know you're there. It can help to avoid a crash. Use your horn when needed. However, it can startle others and could be dangerous when used unnecessarily.

text: Which of these can cause a fire?
explanation: Under-inflated tires, loose fuel connections, and electrical short-circuits can all lead to fires.

text: You are checking your brakes and suspension system for a pre-trip inspection. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Brake pads should not be dangerously thin or have fluid on them, and a missing leaf in the leaf spring suspension is very dangerous.

text: You are driving on a two-lane road. An oncoming driver drifts into your lane and is headed straight for you. Which of these is generally the best action?
explanation: If an oncoming driver has drifted into your lane, a move to your right is best. If that driver realizes what has happened, the natural response will be to return to his or her own lane.

text: For an average commercial vehicle, being driven at 55 mph on dry pavement it will take about _____ to bring the vehicle to a stop.
What happens when tar heats up?
explanation: Tar in the road pavement frequently rises to the surface in very hot weather. Spots where tar "bleeds" to the surface are very slippery.

text: In snow and ice, you should reduce your speed by?
explanation: On packed snow, reduce speed by a half, or more. If the surface is icy, reduce speed to a crawl and stop driving as soon as you can safely do so.

text: How many tie-downs are required for a 20-foot load?
explanation: Cargo should have at least one tie-down for each ten feet of cargo. Make sure you have enough tiedowns to meet this need. For a 20-foot load you would need at least 2 tie downs.

text: It has just reached freezing. Which of the following areas may be slippery?
explanation: Here are some signs of slippery roads: shaded areas, bridges, melting Ice, black Ice makes the road look wet, and just after rain begins.

text: Which of the following statements about an escape ramp is FALSE?
explanation: Your best hope is an escape ramp. If there is one, there will be signs telling you about it. Use it. Ramps are usually located a few miles from the top of the downgrade. Escape ramps save lives, equipment and cargo.

text: You should inspect wheel bearing seals for
explanation: Check wheel bearing seals to make sure they are not leaking.

text: Before transporting a sealed load, you must check
explanation: You cannot inspect sealed loads, but you should check that you don't exceed gross weight and axle weight limits.

text: While driving at night, which lights should you use as often as you can?
explanation: Some drivers make the mistake of always using low beams. This seriously cuts down on their ability to see ahead. Use high beams when it is safe and legal to do so. Use them when you are not within 500 feet of an approaching vehicle.

text: If the engine is not overheated, is it completely safe to remove the radiator cap?
explanation: Never remove the radiator cap or any part of the pressurized system until the system has cooled. Steam and boiling water can spray under pressure and cause severe burns.

text: Your emergency kit does NOT need to include
explanation: Vehicles must be equipped with emergency equipment. Look for: fire extinguisher(s), spare electrical fuses (unless equipped with circuit breakers), and warning devices for parked vehicles.

text: What is counter steering?
explanation: Be prepared to "counter-steer," that is, to turn the wheel back in the other direction, once you've passed whatever was in your path. You should think of emergency steering and counter-steering as two parts of one driving action.

text: You are driving on a straight, level highway at 50 mph.  There are no vehicles in front of you. Suddenly a tire blows out on your vehicle. What should you do first?
explanation: It's natural to want to brake in an emergency, however, braking when a tire has failed could cause loss of control. Once the vehicle has slowed down brake very gently, pull off the road, and stop.

text: What is the I.P.D.E. method of driving?
explanation: Identify, Predict, Decide, and Execute (IPDE) is the step-by-step process behind the principles of defensive driving and complexities of visual perception in traffic.

text: Which of these statements about using mirrors is true?
explanation: Convex mirrors present a view of people and objects that does not accurately reflect their size and distance from the vehicle.

text: No matter how small the cargo is, it should have at least:
explanation: No matter how small the cargo, it should have at least two tie-downs.

text: You have left the road in an emergency condition, it is impossible for you to come to a stop prior to returning to the road, which of the following is the best action to take?
explanation: If possible, avoid using the brakes until your speed has dropped to about 20 mph, and gently return to the road.

text: Tie downs must be of the proper type and strength. The combined strength of cargo tie downs must be strong enough to lift.
explanation: Tie-downs must be of the proper type and proper strength. Federal regulations require the aggregate working load limit of any securement system used to secure an article or group of articles against movement must be at least one and one-half times the weight of the article or group of articles.

text: Your brakes can get wet when you drive through a heavy rain. What can this cause when the brakes are applied?
explanation: When driving in heavy rain or deep standing water, your brakes will get wet. Water in the brakes can cause the brakes to be weak, to apply unevenly, or to grab. This can cause lack of braking power, wheel lockups, pulling to one side or the other, and jackknife if you pull a trailer.

text: How far should a driver look ahead of the vehicle while driving?
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds.

text: Which of these statements about speed management is true?
explanation: It will take longer to stop, and it will be harder to turn without skidding, when the road is slippery. Reduce speed by about one-third on a wet road. On packed snow, reduce speed by a half, or more. If the surface is icy, reduce speed to a crawl and stop driving as soon as you can safely do so.

text: You are driving a new truck with a manual transmission.  What gear will you probably have to use to take a long downhill grade?
explanation: Drivers of modern trucks may have to use lower gears going down a hill than would be required to go up the hill.

text: How do you correct a rear wheel acceleration skid?
explanation: Skids caused by acceleration usually happen on ice or snow. Taking your foot off the accelerator can easily stop them. If it is very slippery, push the clutch in. Otherwise, the engine can keep the wheels from rolling freely and regaining traction.

text: Which of these is a good rule to follow when using a fire extinguisher?
explanation: Here are some rules to follow in putting out a fire: When using the extinguisher stay as far away from the fire as possible and aim at the source or base of the fire not up in the flames.

text: Too much weight on the steering axle can cause the following:
explanation: Poor weight balance can make vehicle handling unsafe. Too much weight on the steering axle can cause hard steering. It can damage the steering axle and tires.

text: Covering the brake means?
explanation: Covering the brake involves taking your right foot off the accelerator and holding it over the brake pedal. Your foot should hover over the pedal and not rest on it in any way.

text: Which of these statements about drugs is true?
explanation: Use of drugs can lead to traffic accidents resulting in death, injury, and property damage. Furthermore, it can lead to arrest, fines, and jail sentences. It can also mean the end of a person's driving career.

text: To prevent shifting, there should be at least one tie down for every _____ feet of cargo.
explanation: Cargo should have at least one tie-down for each ten feet of cargo. Make sure you have enough tiedowns to meet this need.

text: Where or when should you test the stopping action of your service brakes?
explanation: Wait for normal air pressure, release the parking brake, move the vehicle forward slowly about five mph, and apply the brakes firmly using the brake pedal. Note any vehicle "pulling" to one side, unusual feel, or delayed stopping action.

text: You are driving a heavy vehicle and must exit a highway using an off-ramp that curves downhill. You should:
explanation: When exiting the expressway: Make necessary traffic checks, use proper signals, decelerate smoothly in the exit lane.

text: When should you wear seat belts?
explanation: Use Your Seatbelt! The driver's seat should have a seat belt. Always use it for safety.

text: On which fires can you use the ABC fire extinguisher?
explanation: Class A is for trash, wood and paper, Class B is for liquids and gases, and Class C is for energized electrical sources, so all answers are correct.

text: Some traffic emergencies may require you to leave the road. Which of these is a good thing to remember?
explanation: In some emergencies, you may have to drive off the road. It may be less risky than facing a collision with another vehicle. Most shoulders are strong enough to support the weight of a large vehicle and, therefore, offer an available escape route.

text: Dry bulk tanks require special care because:
explanation: Dry bulk tanks require special care because they have a high center of gravity, and the load can shift. Be extremely cautious (slow and careful) going around curves and making sharp turns.

text: What would you use to put out a gasoline fire?
explanation: Gasoline fires may be extinguished by smothering with wet rags, woolen cloth, sand, earth or ashes.

text: Some liquid tanks are divided into several smaller tanks by:
explanation: Some liquid tanks are divided into several smaller tanks by bulkheads. When loading and unloading the smaller tanks, the driver must pay attention to weight distribution.

text: When driving a commercial vehicle with a height over 13 feet you should:
explanation: Hitting overhead objects is a danger. Make sure you always have overhead clearance.

text: You must inspect your cargo:
explanation: As part of your Vehicle inspection, make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured properly. You need to inspect again: after you have driven for 3 hours or 150 miles, and after every break you take during driving.

text: Cargo covers:
explanation: There are two basic reasons for covering cargo: to protect people from spilled cargo, and to protect the cargo from weather. Spill protection is a safety requirement in many states. Be familiar with the laws in the states you drive in.

text: Which of these is a good rule to follow when driving at night?
explanation: You should always be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead. At night, you can't see as far with low beams as you can with high beams. When you must use low beams, slow down.

text: Stab braking:
explanation: With the stab braking method, you apply your brakes all the way and release brakes when wheels lock up.

text: Your vehicle has hydraulic brakes. While traveling on a level road, you press the brake pedal and find that it goes to the floor. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Sometimes pumping the brake pedal will generate enough hydraulic pressure to stop the vehicle.

text: You are driving a heavy vehicle with a manual transmission. You must stop the vehicle on the shoulder while driving on an upgrade. Which of these is a good rule to follow when putting it back in motion?
explanation: Keeping the parking brake set until you can feel the pull of the vehicle against the brakes will help you get moving on an uphill grade without rolling backwards.

text: If you are being tailgated, you should:
explanation: If you find yourself being tailgated, here are some things you can do to reduce the chances of a crash: avoid quick changes, increase your following distance opening up room in front of you will make it easier for the tailgater to get around you, don't speed up.

text: You are checking your wheels and rims for a pre-trip inspection. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: When checking the wheels and rims check for: damaged rims. rust around wheel nuts may mean the nuts are loose--check tightness, missing clamps, spacers, studs, or lugs, mismatched, bent, or cracked lock rings, and wheels or rims that have had welding repairs are not safe.

text: If you are confronted by an aggressive driver, what should you do?
explanation: First and foremost, make every attempt to get out of their way. Do not challenge them by speeding up, avoid eye contact, ignore gestures and refuse to react to them, and report aggressive drivers to the appropriate authorities.

text: You don't want to be a distracted driver, so you
explanation: Activities inside of the vehicle that can distract your attention include: talking to passengers; adjusting the radio, CD player or climate controls; eating, drinking or smoking; reading maps or other literature; picking up something that fell; reading or sending text messages. To name a few.

text: What is a common cause of tire fires?
explanation: Under-inflated tires and duals that touch are the most common causes of tire fires.

text: Do empty trucks have the best braking?
explanation: Empty trucks require greater stopping distances because an empty vehicle has less traction.

text: How far ahead should you look while driving?
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds.

text: Which of the following should you NOT do if you experience a tire failure?
explanation: To respond to tire failure: you must immediately: hold the steering wheel firmly, stay off the brake, and check the tires after you've come to a stop.

text: What might happen if you swing wide to the left before you turn right?
explanation: If you are driving a truck or bus that cannot make the right turn without swinging into another lane, turn wide as you complete the turn. Don't turn wide to the left as you start the turn. A following driver may think you are turning left and try to pass you on the right.

text: What will help someone who is drunk sober up?
explanation: The liver can only process one-third an ounce of alcohol per hour, which is considerably less than the alcohol in a standard drink. This is a fixed rate, so only time, not black coffee or a cold shower, will sober you up.

text: Total stopping distance equals
explanation: Stopping distance is the sum of your Perception Distance + Reaction Distance + Braking Distance. In vehicles with air brakes you must include the brake lag distance.

text: Convex curved mirrors
explanation: Many large vehicles have curved mirrors that show a wider area than flat mirrors. This is often helpful, but everything appears smaller in a convex mirror than it would if you were looking at it directly. Things also seem farther away than they really are.

text: Containerized loads
explanation: Containerized loads generally are used when freight is carried part way by rail or ship. Delivery by truck occurs at the beginning and/or end of the journey.

text: How many red reflective triangles should you carry?
explanation: Safety requirements call for three red reflective triangles.

text: How does "bleeding tar" affect the road surface and driving conditions?
explanation: Tar in the road pavement frequently rises to the surface in very hot weather. Spots where tar "bleeds" to the surface are very slippery.

text: Dry bulk tanks and hanging meat
explanation: Hanging meat and dry bulk can be a very unstable load with a high center of gravity. Particular caution is needed on sharp curves such as off ramps and on ramps. Go slowly.

text: A GPS device
explanation: Most GPS devices are made for cars or other smaller vehicles, and so not have the size or weight of large vehicles programmed in. Make sure your GPS device is designed for large commercial vehicles.

text: A major cause of fatal crashes is
explanation: Driving too fast is a major cause of fatal crashes. You must adjust your speed depending on driving conditions. These include traction, curves, visibility, traffic and hills.

text: While driving, ice builds up on your wipers and they no longer clean the windshield. You should:
explanation: Before the ice buildup becomes dangerous you should stop in a safe location and clear off the ice. With ice on the wiper blade it might not clear the windshield enough for safe driving.

text: Is it legal to drive with one-fourth of a vehicle's leaf springs broken or missing?
explanation: Check for missing or broken leaves in any leaf spring. If one-fourth or more are missing, it will put the vehicle "out of service", but any defect could be dangerous.

text: The distance that you should scan ahead in a congested area is about?
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. At lower speeds, that's about one block. At highway speeds it's about a quarter of a mile.

text: A vehicle is loaded with very little weight on the drive axle. What may result:
explanation: Too little weight on the driving axles can cause poor traction. The drive wheels may spin easily.

text: Truck escape ramps:
explanation: Escape ramps are made to stop runaway vehicles safely without injuring drivers and passengers.

text: What effects can wet brakes cause?
explanation: Water in the brakes can cause the brakes to be weak, to apply unevenly, or to grab. This can cause lack of braking power, wheel lockups, pulling to one side or the other, and jackknife if you pull a trailer.

text: When you check the condition of visible parts during your pre-trip inspection, make sure that the ___________ are secured against snagging, rubbing, or wearing.
explanation: Air lines and electrical wiring needs to be secured against snagging, rubbing, wearing.

text: You do not have a Hazardous Materials Endorsement on your commercial driver's license. You can drive a vehicle hauling hazardous materials when
explanation: You can drive a vehicle that carries hazardous materials if it does not require placards. If it requires placards, you cannot drive it unless your driver license has the hazardous materials endorsement

text: Which of the following violations at railroad crossings can lead to loss of your CDL for at least 60 days?
explanation: You will lose your CDL for at least 60 days for your first violation of the following six offenses: failing to stop if the tracks are not clear, failing to slow down and check that the tracks are clear of an approaching train, failing to stop before driving onto the crossing, failing to have sufficient space to drive completely through the crossing without stopping, failing to obey a traffic control device or the directions of an enforcement official at the crossing, failing to negotiate a crossing because of insufficient undercarriage clearance.

text: When should you check your mirrors for a lane change?
explanation: You need to check your mirrors to make sure no one is alongside you or about to pass you. Check your mirrors: before you change lanes, after you have signaled, right after you start the lane change, and after you complete the lane change.

text: Which of these tells you that cargo contains hazardous materials?
explanation: If the cargo contains hazardous materials, you must inspect for proper papers, labeling, and placarding.

text: Which of these is NOT required knowledge for drivers needing a HAZMAT endorsement?
explanation: Hazardous materials drivers must also know which products they can load together, and which they cannot, when to use placards.

text: Which of these statements is true about medicines used to treat the common cold?
explanation: This includes a variety of prescription and over-the counter drugs or cold medicines, which may make the driver drowsy or otherwise affect safe driving ability.

text: Which of these statements is true about other drivers?
explanation: Be alert for drivers whose vision is blocked. Vans loaded station wagons, and cars with the rear window blocked are examples. Rental trucks should be watched carefully. Their drivers are often not used to the limited vision they have to the sides and rear of the truck.

text: Which of these statements is NOT true about engine belts in hot weather?
explanation: Learn how to check v-belt tightness on your vehicle by pressing on the belts. Loose belts will not turn the water pump and/or fan properly. This will result in overheating. Also, check belts for cracking or other signs of wear.

text: Which of these statements is true about warning other drivers of a stopped vehicle?
explanation: If line of sight view is obstructed due to hill or curve, move the rear-most triangle to a point back down the road (up to 500 feet) so warning is provided.

text: Which of these is NOT a good rule to follow when caring for the injured at an accident scene?
explanation: If a qualified person is at the accident and helping the injured, stay out of the way unless asked to assist. Otherwise, do the best you can to help any injured parties: don't move a severely injured person unless the danger of fire or passing traffic, stop heavy bleeding by applying, and keep the injured person warm.

text: Which of the following statements is true?
explanation: If possible, try to schedule trips for the hours you are normally awake. Many heavy motor vehicle accidents occur between midnight and 6 a.m.

text: Which of these statements is NOT true about bad weather driving conditions?
explanation: Right after it starts to rain, the water mixes with oil left on the road by vehicles. This makes the road very slippery. If the rain continues, it will wash the oil away.

text: What does "axle weight" mean?
explanation: Axle weight is the weight transmitted from one axle or a set of axles to the ground.

text: With a BAC of _____, most people lose consciousness, and some die.
explanation: At a BAC of .40 most people will lose consciousness, and some will die.

text: Loads that are over-length, over-width, or overweight may require
explanation: Over-length, over-width, and/or overweight loads require special transit permits. Driving is usually limited to certain times. Special equipment may be necessary such as "wide load" signs, flashing lights, flags, etc. Such loads may require a police escort or pilot vehicles bearing warning signs and/or flashing lights. These special loads require special driving care.

text: When getting in to start the engine and inspect inside the cab, you must
explanation: To get in and start the engine, make sure: the parking brake is on, gearshift is in neutral.

text: Which of these is NOT something you should do if your headlights are not working properly?
explanation: Use high beams when it is safe and legal to do so. Use them when you are not within 500 feet of another vehicle.

text: Name some suspension system defects.
explanation: Leaking shock absorbers and broken or missing leaves are two of the more common defects that may occur in a suspension system.

text: What's the safest way to use turn signals when you intend to turn?
explanation: There are three good rules for using turn signals: signal early, signal continuously, cancel your signal after the turn is completed.

text: Which of these is especially true about your tires in hot weather?
explanation: Check the tire mounting and air pressure, inspect the tires every two hours or every 100 miles, air pressure increases with temperature, do not let air out or the pressure will be too low when the tires cool off, if a tire is too hot to touch remain stopped until the tire cools off.

text: What should you do if a car coming toward you at night keeps its high beams on?
explanation: Don't look directly at bright lights when driving. Look at the right side of the road. Watch the sidelines when someone coming toward you has very bright lights on.

text: When you're driving in cold weather, your tires must
explanation: Make sure you have enough tread on your tires. The drive tires must provide traction to push the rig over wet pavement and through snow. The steering tires must have traction to steer the vehicle. Enough tread is especially important in winter conditions.

text: When driving in an area with few streetlights on a clear night, if you cannot see well with your headlights, you should
explanation: Some drivers make the mistake of always using low beams. This seriously cuts down on their ability to see ahead. Use high beams when it is safe and legal to do so. Use them when you are not within 500 feet of another vehicle. Keep the interior light off and adjust your instrument lights as low as you can to still be able to read the gauges.

text: When a doctor prescribes medication for you and you know you will be driving, you should
explanation: Possession and use of any drug given to a driver by a doctor is permitted if the doctor informs the driver that it will not affect safe driving ability.

text: What does "communicating" mean in safe driving?
explanation: Other drivers can't know what you are going to do until you tell them. Signaling what you intend to do is important for safety. Also, other drivers may not notice your vehicle even when it's in plain sight. To help prevent accidents, let them know you're there.

text: What is the proper way to hold the steering wheel?
explanation: Hold the steering wheel firmly with both hands. Your hands should be on opposite sides of the wheel. If you hit a curb or a pothole, the wheel could pull away from your hands unless you have a firm hold.

text: When inspecting mirrors and windshields for cracks, dirt, illegal stickers, and other obstructions to your vision, you should
explanation: Inspect mirrors and windshield for cracks, dirt, illegal stickers, or other obstructions to seeing clearly. Clean and adjust as necessary.

text: When you are passing another vehicle, pedestrian, or bicyclist, you should assume that they
explanation: Whenever you are about to pass a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicyclist, assume they don't see you, or that they could suddenly move in front of you. When it is legal, tap the horn lightly or, at night, flash your lights from low to high beam and back. Drive carefully enough to avoid a crash even if they don't see or hear you.

text: What should you do before driving if you are sleepy?
explanation: The average person needs seven or eight hours of sleep every 24 hours. Leaving on a long trip when you're already tired is dangerous. If you have a long trip scheduled, make sure that you get enough sleep before you go.

text: If you must stop on a road or the shoulder of any road, you must put out your emergency warning devices within
explanation: If you must stop on a road or the shoulder of any road, you must put out your emergency warning devices within ten minutes.

text: Trucks and buses are subject to certain laws, restrictions, and regulations. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Be familiar with the laws in the states you drive in. These laws can cover aspects such as vehicle weight, cargo, and allowed routes. The regulations can vary from one jurisdiction to another, so know which jurisdictions you'll be driving through.

text: To prevent brake fade, you should
explanation: You must select an appropriate safe speed, then use a low gear, and proper braking techniques.

text: You are driving a new truck with a manual transmission. To take a long, steep downhill grade,
explanation: Newer trucks have low friction parts, streamlined shapes for fuel economy, more powerful engines meaning  they can go up hills in higher gears. For that reason, drivers of modern trucks may have to use lower gears going down a hill than would be required to go up the hill.

text: Which of the following is true about a vehicle with a high center of gravity?
explanation: It is very important to distribute the cargo, so it is as low as possible. Put the heaviest parts of the cargo under the lightest parts.

text: One can recognize hazardous materials by looking at the container's
explanation: Emergency personnel look for these things on the shipping paper. That is why it is vital that the proper shipping name, identification number, label, and placards are correct.

text: Optional safety equipment may include emergency phone numbers, tire chains, and
explanation: Check for optional emergency items such as: chains, tire changing equipment, list of emergency phone numbers, and accident reporting kit.

text: The best way to stop all skids is to
explanation: When any vehicle starts to skid, you must act to restore traction to the wheels.

text: If your brakes get wet on a rainy day, what can result?
explanation: Water in the brakes can cause the brakes to be weak, to apply unevenly, or to grab. This can cause lack of braking power, wheel lockups, pulling to one side or the other, and jackknife if you pull a trailer.

text: Implied consent means that when you operate a CMV on public roads, you
explanation: If you operate a CMV, you shall be deemed to have given your consent to alcohol testing.

text: It takes the body about _____ hours to get rid of the alcohol in four drinks.
explanation: —the rest builds up in your blood.

text: Heavy vehicles must sometimes travel more slowly than other traffic. Which of these is NOT a good rule to follow when you are driving such a vehicle?
explanation: Some drivers try to help out others by signaling when it is safe to pass. You should not do this. You could cause an accident. You could be blamed, and it could cost you many thousands of dollars.

text: You must park on the side of a level, straight, four lanes, divided highway. Where should you place the three reflective triangles?
explanation: If you must stop on or by a one-way or divided highway, place warning devices 10 feet, 100 feet, and 200 feet toward the approaching traffic.

text: A stale green light means?
explanation: A stale green light is a traffic light that is green when you first see it, thus you do not know how long it has been green - so we have to assume it will change at any moment.

text: A vehicle is loaded with most of the weight on the steering axle. What may result?
explanation: Too much weight on the steering axle can cause hard steering. It can damage the steering axle and tires.

text: When approaching a curve, you should:
explanation: Slow down to a safe speed, and downshift to the right gear before entering the curve. This lets you use some power through the curve to help the vehicle be more stable while turning. It also allows you to speed up as soon as you are out of the curve.

text: Should the pre-trip inspection report from the last driver be reviewed during your pre-trip inspection?
explanation: You must review the inspection report made by the previous driver. Only if defects reported earlier have been certified as repaired or not needed to be repaired, should you sign the previous driver's report.

text: The load means the following?
explanation: The tire load is the maximum safe weight a tire can carry at a specified pressure. This rating is stated on the side of each tire.

text: On average, the total stopping distance with air brakes is?
explanation: The total stopping distance is the total minimum distance your vehicle has traveled, in ideal conditions; with everything considered, including perception distance, reaction distance and braking distance, until you can bring your vehicle to a complete stop. At 55 mph, your vehicle will travel a minimum of 450 feet.

text: To correct a rear wheel or drive wheel braking skid, you should:
explanation: Do the following to correct a drive-wheel braking skid: stop braking letting the rear wheels roll again and keep the rear wheels from sliding; counter-steer as a vehicle turns back on course, it tends to keep on turning. Unless you turn the steering wheel quickly the other way, you may find yourself skidding in the opposite direction.

text: Which of the following is NOT something you should check during a trip?
explanation: Your CDL will be disqualified after two or more convictions of any state law on texting while operating a CMV. No motor carrier shall allow or require its drivers to engage in texting while driving.

text: Which of these is NOT part of the basic method for shifting up?
explanation: Basic method for shifting up: release accelerator, push in clutch and shift to neutral at the same time. Release clutch. Let engine and gears slow down to the rpm required for the next gear. Push in clutch and shift to the higher gear at the same time. Release clutch and press accelerator at the same time.

text: You should put the starter switch key into your pocket while you perform the pre-trip inspection because
explanation: Shut off engine and take key with you so someone else won't move truck while you are under it.

text: What is the best way to figure out how many seconds of following distance you have?
explanation: To know how much space you have, wait until the vehicle ahead passes a shadow on the road, a pavement marking, or some other clear landmark. Then count off the seconds like this: "one thousand-and-one, one thousand-and-two" and so on, until you reach the same spot.

text: Signs of distracted drivers include
explanation: Watch for: vehicles that may drift over the lane divider lines or within their own lane; vehicles traveling at inconsistent speeds; drivers who are preoccupied with maps, food, cigarettes, cell phones, or other objects; drivers who appear to be involved in conversations with their passengers.

text: The most important hand signal that you and a helper should agree on is
explanation: Before you begin backing, work out a set of hand signals that you both understand. Agree on a signal for "stop."

text: If you have been drinking alcohol, before you drive, you should
explanation: The liver can only process one-third an ounce of alcohol per hour, which is considerably less than the alcohol in a standard drink. This is a fixed rate, so only time, not black coffee or a cold shower, will sober you up.

text: Blocking the cargo to keep it from shifting must be done
explanation: Blocking is used in the front, back, and/or sides of a piece of cargo to keep it from sliding. Blocking is shaped to fit snugly against cargo. It is secured to the cargo deck to prevent cargo movement.

text: Every time you park your vehicle and shut the engine off, you should
explanation: Never leave your vehicle unattended without applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels. Your vehicle might roll away and cause injury and damage.

text: If you are being tailgated, you should
explanation: If you find yourself being tailgated: avoid quick changes, signal early, reduce speed very gradually, increase your following distance, and don't speed up.

text: How long does it take a typical tractor-trailer to clear a single railroad track? A double railroad track?
explanation: It takes a typical tractor trailer unit at least 14 seconds to clear a single track and more than 15 seconds to clear a double track.

text: How can you test the parking brake?
explanation: To test the parking brake: fasten safety belt, set parking brake, place vehicle into a low gear, and gently pull forward against parking brake to make sure the parking brake holds.

text: To safely adjust your speed to current driving conditions, what should you look for ahead?
explanation: Driving too fast is a major cause of fatal crashes. You must adjust your speed depending on driving conditions. These include traction, curves, visibility, traffic and hills.

text: What are some advantages of going right instead of left around an obstacle?
explanation: If an oncoming driver has drifted into your lane, a move to your right is best. If that driver realizes what has happened, the natural response will be to return to their own lane. No one is likely to be driving on the shoulder, but someone may be passing you on the left.

text: The best drivers are those who watch and prepare for hazards. This is called being
explanation: Always be prepared to act based on your plans. In this way, you will be a prepared, defensive driver who will improve your own safety as well as the safety of all road users.

text: Is it safe to add coolant to a coolant recovery tank or coolant overflow tank while the engine is at operating temperature?
explanation: Some vehicles have sight glasses, see-through coolant overflow containers, or coolant recovery containers. These permit you to check the coolant level while the engine is hot. If the container is not part of the pressurized system, the cap can be safely removed, and coolant added even when the engine is at operating temperature.

text: Name some important steering system parts.
explanation: The pitman arm, spindle, tie rod, and steering gear box are all parts of the steering system.

text: If you need to leave the road in a traffic emergency, you should
explanation: If possible, avoid using the brakes until your speed has dropped to about 20 mph, and gently return to the road.

text: Under federal regulations, if a vehicle is carrying a load that is more than two feet wide and extends more than four feet beyond the rear of the vehicle, _________ must be placed at the extreme end of the load.
explanation: If the load is more than two feet wide and extends more than four feet behind the rear of the vehicle, two square red flags must be placed at the end of the load.

text: When driving on grades, which of these is the best choice for a safe speed?
explanation: Your most important consideration is to select a speed that is not too fast for the: total weight of the vehicle and cargo, length of the grade, steepness of the grade, road conditions, and weather.

text: When approaching a traffic light that has been green for a long time, you should
explanation: If a traffic light has been green for a long time it will probably change before you get there. Start slowing down and be ready to stop.

text: What is the first thing you should try to do if your brakes fail while driving downhill?
explanation: Escape ramps have been built on many steep mountain downgrades. Escape ramps are made to stop runaway vehicles safely without injuring drivers and passengers. Escape ramps use a long bed of loose, soft material to slow a runaway vehicle, sometimes in combination with an upgrade.

text: What is the definition of "sleep debt?"
explanation: If you don't sleep enough, you "owe" more sleep to yourself. This debt can only be paid off by sleeping. You can't overcome it with willpower, and it won't go away by itself. The average person needs seven or eight hours of sleep every 24 hours.

text: Which of these items is NOT checked on a pre-trip inspection?
explanation: During your pre-trip inspection you will need to check cargo securement, and vehicle lights to make sure the vehicle is safe to go on the road. Fuel is a responsibility of the driver to transport the cargo and is not checked on the pre-trip inspection.

text: You should check the following for looseness, sticking, damage, or improper settings
explanation: Check all of the following for looseness, sticking, damage, or improper setting: steering wheel, clutch, and accelerator.

text: Which of these statements is NOT true?
explanation: BAC is determined by the amount of alcohol you drink, how fast you drink, and your weight. Alcohol goes directly into the blood stream from the stomach. The liver can only process one-third an ounce of alcohol per hour.

text: What is/are the important reason(-s) for doing a vehicle inspection?
explanation: A vehicle defect found during an inspection could save you problems later. You could have a breakdown on the road that will cost time and dollars, or even worse, a crash caused by the defect. Also, Federal and state laws require that drivers inspect their vehicles.

text: You should schedule your driving
explanation: If possible, try to schedule trips for the hours you are normally awake. Many heavy motor vehicle accidents occur between midnight and 6 a.m.

text: Which of these is NOT a proper use of vehicle lights?
explanation: If you find yourself being tailgated, here are some things you can do to reduce the chances of a crash: avoid quick changes, increase your following distance, don't speed up, and don't turn on your taillights or flash your brake lights.

text: Which of these statements about drinking alcohol is true?
explanation: All of the following drinks contain the same amount of alcohol: A 12-ounce glass of 5% beer. A 5-ounce glass of 12% wine. A 1 1/2-ounce shot of 80 proof whiskey.

text: Which of these statements about using turn signals is true?
explanation: Signal early and leave the signal on until the maneuver is complete. Signal well before you turn. It is the best way to keep others from trying to pass you.

text: If an automatic transmission is forced into a lower gear while driving, what is not affected on a downgrade?
explanation: Forcing an automatic transmission into a lower gear at high speed could damage the transmission and also lead to loss of all engine braking effect.

text: Over length, over width, and overweight loads require:
explanation: Over-length, over-width, and/or overweight loads require special transit permits. Driving is usually limited to certain times. Special equipment may be necessary such as "wide load" signs, flashing lights, flags, etc.

text: When roads are slippery, you should:
explanation: It will take longer to stop, and it will be harder to turn without skidding, when the road is slippery. So, making gentle turns is key when the roads are slippery.

text: Which of these is not a danger when an automatic transmission is forced into a lower gear at a high speed?
explanation: Forcing an automatic transmission into a lower gear at high speed could damage the transmission and also lead to loss of all engine braking effect.

text: What are the proper collision procedures that are required for any collision in which you are involved but not seriously injured?
explanation: When you're in an accident and not seriously hurt, you need to act to prevent further damage or injury. The basic steps to be taken at any accident are to: protect the area, notify authorities, and care for the injured.

text: The Commercial Driver's Handbook suggests several things to do when you pass a vehicle. Which of these is NOT one of them?
explanation: Whenever you are about to pass a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicyclist, assume they don't see you. They could suddenly move in front of you. When it is legal, tap the horn lightly or, at night, flash your lights from low to high beam and back. And, drive carefully enough to avoid a crash even if they don't see or hear you.

text: Which of the following statements about backing is true?
explanation: Because you cannot see everything behind your vehicle, backing is always dangerous. Avoid backing whenever you can. When you have to back, here are a few simple safety rules: start in the proper position, look at your path, use mirrors on both sides, back slowly, back and turn toward the driver's side if possible, use a helper whenever possible.

text: What is black ice?
explanation: Black ice is a thin layer that is clear enough that you can see the road underneath it. It makes the road look wet. Any time the temperature is below freezing, and the road looks wet, watch out for black ice.

text: You should try to park your vehicle so that
explanation: Avoid backing whenever you can. When you park, try to park so you will be able to pull forward when you leave.

text: What checks should you perform on the steering gear box?
explanation: Check for missing nuts, bolts, cotter keys, or other parts.

text: What should you do if you get stuck on the railroad tracks?
explanation: If for any reason you get stuck on the tracks, get out of the vehicle and away from the tracks. Check signposts or signal housing at the crossing for emergency notification information. Call 911  and give the location of the crossing using all identifiable landmarks, especially the DOT number, if posted.

text: What is the minimum tread depth for tires other than front tires?
explanation: You must have at least 4/32-inch tread depth in every major groove on front tires and at least 2/32-inch on other tires

text: The distance that you should look ahead of your vehicle while driving is about _______ at highway speed.
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds. At lower speeds, that's about one block. At highway speeds it's about a quarter of a mile.

text: The total weight of the powered unit, the trailer, and the cargo is called
explanation: Gross Combination Weight (GCW) is the total weight of a powered unit, plus trailer(s), plus the cargo.

text: The reaction distance is the distance traveled from the time
explanation: ¾ second to 1 second.

text: In your mirror, you see a car approaching from the rear. The next time you check your mirror, you don't see the car. To change lanes, you should
explanation: Before you change lanes, you must first make sure that no vehicles are hiding in your blind spots.

text: Stab braking
explanation: With the stab braking method, you apply your brakes all the way and release brakes when wheels lock up.

text: Can federal inspectors inspect your truck or bus?
explanation: Federal and state inspectors also may inspect your vehicles. If they judge the vehicle to be unsafe, they will put it "out of service" until it is fixed.

text: If you are driving a long distance, you should
explanation: Short breaks can keep you alert. But the time to take them is before you feel really drowsy or tired. Stop often. Walk around and inspect your vehicle. It may help to do some physical exercises.

text: If you have a heavy load that is slowing you down on an upgrade, you should
explanation: Heavy vehicles are often tailgated when they can't keep up with the speed of traffic. This often happens when you're going uphill. If a heavy load is slowing you down, stay in the right lane if you can. Going uphill, you should not pass another slow vehicle unless you can get around quickly and safely.

text: Cargo that can shift should have at least __ tie-down(s).
explanation: No matter how small the cargo, it should have at least two tie-downs.

text: Converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998 are required to have anti-lock brakes. These dollies will have
explanation: Converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998, are required to have antilock brakes. These dollies will have a yellow lamp on the left side of the dolly.

text: Why is it important to use a helper when backing?
explanation: There are blind spots you can't see. That's why a helper is important. The helper should stand near the back of your vehicle where you can see the helper.

text: How long does it take a typical tractor-trailer to clear a double railroad track?
explanation: It takes a typical tractor-trailer unit at least 14 seconds to clear a single track and more than 15 seconds to clear a double track.

text: What is the definition of a hazard?
explanation: A hazard is any road condition or other road user (driver, bicyclist, pedestrian) that is a possible danger.

text: To maintain alertness on your trips, you should
explanation: Preventing drowsiness before a trip: Get adequate sleep (8 to 9 hours), prepare route, , schedule trips for the hours you are normally awake, drive with a passenger, avoid medications that cause drowsiness.

text: Where should you place your warning devices if you must stop on a one-way road or divided highway?
explanation: If you must stop on or by a one-way or divided highway, place warning devices 10 feet, 100 feet, and 200 feet toward the approaching traffic.

text: Letting the air out of hot tires
explanation: Air pressure increases with temperature. Do not let air out or the pressure will be too low when the tires cool off.

text: If you are traveling at 55 mph in a 30-foot vehicle, you should leave how many seconds of following distance?
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: You are checking your tires for a pre-trip inspection.  Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Tire problems include: too much or too little air pressure, bad wear, at least 4/32-inch tread depth on front tires, 2/32 inch on all other tires, no fabric should show through the tread or sidewall, cuts or other damage, tread separation, dual tires that come in contact with each other or parts of the vehicle, mismatched sizes, radial and bias-ply tires used together.

text: To give adequate warning when passing someone, you should?
explanation: Whenever you are about to pass a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicyclist, assume they don't see you. They could suddenly move in front of you. When it is legal, tap the horn lightly or, at night, flash your lights from low to high beam and back.

text: The purpose of a baffle is to:
explanation: Baffled liquid tanks have bulkheads in them with holes that let the liquid flow through. The baffles help to control the forward and backward liquid surge. Side-to-side surge can still occur.

text: Which of these statements about staying alert is true?
explanation: If you are drowsy, the only safe cure is to get off the road and get some sleep. If you don't, you risk your life and the lives of others.

text: Which of these items is checked in a pre-trip inspection?
explanation: During the pre-trip inspection the lights and covers need to be working and clean, wiper blades in good condition, and the cargo needs to be secured.

text: Brake fade:
explanation: Brake fade results from excessive heat causing chemical changes in the brake lining, which reduce friction, and also causing expansion of the brake drums

text: Retarders:
explanation: When your drive wheels have poor traction, the retarder may cause them to skid. Therefore, you should turn the retarder off whenever the road is wet, icy, or snow covered.

text: The first step to take if your vehicle catches fire while driving is:
explanation: The first step is to get the vehicle off the road and stop. In doing so: park in an open area, away from buildings, trees, brush, other vehicles, or anything that might catch fire. Don't pull into a service station!

text: You are driving in the right lane of a four-lane undivided road, you come over a hill and find a car stopped ahead in your lane. You do not have room to stop, and the hill blocks your view to the rear. Which of these is most likely the best action to take?
explanation: If the shoulder is clear, going right may be best. No one is likely to be driving on the shoulder, but someone may be passing you on the left. You will know if you have been using your mirrors. If you are blocked on both sides, a move to the right may be best. At least you won't force anyone into an opposing traffic lane and a possible head-on collision.

text: Which of these tell you that cargo contains hazardous materials?
explanation: Shipping papers listing a hazard class, hazardous material labels on the cargo, or placards on the vehicle all show the driver that the load contains hazardous material.

text: Medical certificates must be renewed every:
explanation: A DOT physical exam is valid for up to 24 months. The medical examiner may also issue a medical examiner's certificate for less than 24 months when it is desirable to monitor a condition, such as high blood pressure.

text: Which of these statements about overhead clearance is true?
explanation: The weight of a cargo van changes its height. An empty van is higher than a loaded one. That you got under a bridge when you were loaded does not mean that you can do it when you are empty.

text: If you are stopped at a roadside rest and found to have a BAC or blood alcohol concentration of .02 you will:
explanation: You will be put out-of-service for 24 hours if you have any detectable amount of alcohol under .04%.

text: You should avoid driving through deep puddles or flowing water. But if you must, which of these steps can help keep your brakes working?
explanation: Gently put on the brakes. This presses linings against brake drums or discs and keeps mud, silt, sand, and water from getting in.

text: Which of these describes how you should use the brake pedal on a steep downhill grade?
explanation: Once the vehicle is in the proper low gear, the following are the proper braking techniques: Apply the brakes just hard enough to feel a definite slowdown, when your speed has been reduced to approximately five mph below your "safe" speed, release the brakes. When your speed has increased to your "safe" speed, repeat.

text: Which of these statements about backing a heavy vehicle is true?
explanation: Because you cannot see everything behind your vehicle, backing is always dangerous. Avoid backing whenever you can. When you have to back, here are a few simple safety rules: Start in the proper position, look at your path, use mirrors on both sides, back slowly, back and turn toward the driver's side whenever possible, and use a helper with clear hand signals whenever possible.

text: An en-route inspection should include checking:
explanation: For an en-route Inspection. Check the, tires, wheels, and truck body for signs of heat whenever you stop during a trip. Also check for cargo securement at each stop.

text: To avoid a crash, you had to drive onto the right shoulder. You are now driving at 40 mph on the shoulder. How should you move back onto the pavement?
explanation: If the shoulder is clear, stay on it until your vehicle has come to a stop. Signal and check your mirrors before pulling back onto the road.

text: What is average effective braking distance time?
explanation: Braking distance is the distance your vehicle will travel, in ideal conditions; while you are braking. At 55 mph on dry pavement with good brakes, it can take about 216 feet.

text: Overloading can have a bad effect on?
explanation: Overloading can have bad effects on steering, braking, and speed control. Overloaded trucks have to go very slowly on upgrades, and they may gain too much speed on downgrades. Stopping distance increases. Brakes can fail when forced to work too hard.

text: "Reaction distance" is the distance traveled from the time:
explanation: ¾ second to 1 second. At 55 mph this accounts for 61 feet traveled.

text: Controlled braking:
explanation: Controlled Braking: applying the brakes as hard as you can without locking the wheels. Keep steering wheel movements very small while doing this. If you need to make a larger steering adjustment or if the wheels lock, release the brakes, brakes should only be locked for a short period of time. Re-apply the brakes as soon as you can.

text: You are starting your vehicle in motion from a stop, as you apply power to the drive wheels they start to spin. You should:
explanation: Skids caused by acceleration usually happen on ice or snow. Taking your foot off the accelerator can easily stop them. If it is very slippery, push the clutch in. Otherwise, the engine can keep the wheels from rolling freely and regaining traction.

text: Where should your ignition key be during your pre-trip inspection?
explanation: Make sure the parking brake is set, turn off the engine, and take the key with you when you are not in the cab during your vehicle inspection.

text: When driving in fog, you should be going?
explanation: You should always be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead. Fog, rain, or other conditions may require that you slowdown to be able to stop in the distance you can see.

text: Which of these is a good rule to follow when steering to avoid a crash?
explanation: Do not turn any more than needed to clear whatever is in your way. The more sharply you turn, the greater the chances of a skid or rollover.

text: Every time you park your vehicle and shut the engine off you should:
explanation: Never leave your vehicle unattended without applying the parking brakes or chocking the wheels. Your vehicle might roll away and cause injury and damage

text: Which item below lists almost all or all the emergency equipment that is required on a school bus?
explanation: In addition to checking for spare electrical fuses (if equipped), three red reflective triangles, and a properly charged and rated fire extinguisher, a school bus must also have: emergency kit, and  body fluid cleanup kit

text: The key principle in balancing cargo weight is to keep the load:
explanation: As part of your vehicle inspection, make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured properly.

text: Which of these can help you stay alert while driving?
explanation: To maintain alertness while driving: protect yourself from glare and eyestrain with sunglasses, keep cool, avoid heavy foods, be aware of down time during the day, have another person ride with you, take periodic breaks, or stop driving and get some rest or take a nap.

text: When checking the bus seats, what are you looking for?
explanation: The seats must be safe for riders. All seats must be securely fastened to the bus, and not lose or missing.

text: You are checking your steering and exhaust systems for a pre-trip inspection. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Steering wheel play of more than 10 degrees (approximately 2 inches movement at the rim of a 20-inch steering wheel) can make it hard to steer.

text: If you have a hazardous fire, you should?
explanation: When you discover a fire, call for help. You may use the fire extinguisher to keep minor truck fires from spreading to cargo before firefighters arrive.

text: Blocking your cargo to keep it from sliding must be done:
explanation: Blocking is used in the front, back, and/or sides of a piece of cargo to keep it from sliding. Blocking is shaped to fit snugly against cargo. It is secured to the cargo deck to prevent cargo movement.

text: You should use your mirrors to check:
explanation: If you're carrying open cargo, you can use the mirrors to check it. Special situations require more than regular mirror checks including; lane changes, turns, merges, and tight maneuvers. Use your mirrors to make sure the gap in traffic is large enough for you to enter safely.

text: For your safety, when setting out reflective triangles you should:
explanation: When putting out the triangles, hold them between yourself and the oncoming traffic for your own safety, so other drivers can see you.

text: What is the average perception distance?
explanation: ¾ seconds. At 55 mph this accounts for 142 feet traveled.

text: Which of these pieces of emergency equipment should always be carried in your vehicle?
explanation: Vehicles must be equipped with emergency equipment: Fire extinguisher, spare electrical fuses unless the vehicle is equipped with circuit breakers, and warning devices for parked vehicles.

text: Which of these statements about accelerating is true?
explanation: Speed up smoothly and gradually so the vehicle does not jerk. Rough acceleration can cause mechanical damage. When pulling a trailer, rough acceleration can damage the coupling.

text: The most important reason for being alert to hazards is so:
explanation: You look for the hazards in order to have time to plan a way out of any emergency. When you see a hazard, think about the emergencies that could develop and figure out what you would do.

text: Which of these statements about downshifting for a long downhill grade is true?
explanation: The use of brakes on a long and/or steep downgrade is only a supplement to the braking effect of the engine.

text: Whether you load and secure your load yourself, you are responsible for:
explanation: As part of your Vehicle inspection, make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured properly.

text: Cargo inspections:
explanation: Re-check the cargo and securing devices as often as necessary during a trip to keep the load secure. You need to inspect again: after you have driven for 3 hours or 150 miles, and after every break you take during driving.

text: Which of these will help keep an engine cool in hot weather?
explanation: Do a normal Vehicle inspection but pay special attention to the following items: tires, engine oil, engine coolant, engine belts, and hoses. High speeds create more heat for tires and the engine. In desert conditions the heat may build up to the point where it is dangerous.

text: Hazardous materials placards are
explanation: Placards are signs put on the outside of a vehicle that identify the hazard class of the cargo. A placarded vehicle must have at least four identical placards. They are put on the front, rear, and both sides.

text: The minimum tire tread depth for front tires is
explanation: The steering tires must have traction to steer the vehicle. Enough tread is especially important in winter conditions. You must have at least 4/32-inch tread depth in every major groove on front tires and at least 2/32 inch on other tires.

text: An antilock braking system (ABS) will
explanation: ABS is a computerized system that keeps your wheels from locking up during hard brake applications.

text: Which of the following statements about retarders are correct?
explanation: Retarders help slow a vehicle, reducing the need for using your brakes. When your drive wheels have poor traction, the retarder may cause them to skid. Therefore, you should turn the retarder off whenever the road is wet, icy, or snow covered.

text: You are driving a heavy vehicle and must exit a highway using an off ramp that curves downhill. You should
explanation: Slow down to a safe speed, and downshift to the right gear before entering the curve. This lets you use some power through the curve to help the vehicle be more stable while turning.

text: If you do not have a CB radio, what is the first thing you should do at an accident scene?
explanation: The basic steps to be taken at any accident are to: protect the area, notify authorities, care for the injured.

text: The oil pressure gauge should come up to normal within how long after starting the engine?
explanation: Oil pressure. Should come up to normal within seconds after engine is started.

text: The distance your vehicle travels from the time your eyes see a hazard to the time your brain recognizes it is called _______ distance.
explanation: The distance your vehicle travels, in ideal conditions; from the time your eyes see a hazard until your brain recognizes it is perception distance.

text: Stab braking should not be used on vehicles with
explanation: In stab braking you deliberately keep locking and unlocking the wheels, and ABS is a computerized system that keeps your wheels from locking up during hard brake applications. Do not use Stab braking with ABS, use controlled braking instead.

text: When should you downshift an automatic transmission?
explanation: You can select a low range to get greater engine braking when going down grades. The lower ranges prevent the transmission from shifting up beyond the selected gear.

text: What is a "pull-up?"
explanation: When a driver stops and reverses direction to get a better position while backing, it is scored as a "pull-up". Stopping without changing direction does not count as a pull-up.

text: When must you wear a seat belt?
explanation: Federal regulations require the driver and all passengers in a moving CMV to wear seat belts unless, the vehicle isn't equipped with them.

text: When you drive in the mountains, you will have to use lower gears to drive safely down grades. Which of these does NOT affect your choice of gears?
explanation: Your most important consideration is to select a speed that is not too fast for the: total weight of the vehicle and cargo, length of the grade, steepness of the grade, road conditions, and weather.

text: Serious traffic violations that can cause your CDL license to be suspended include
explanation: Serious traffic violations are excessive speeding, reckless driving, improper or erratic lane changes, following a vehicle too closely, traffic offenses committed in a CMV in connection with fatal traffic accidents, etc.

text: There are certain types of vehicle inspection, including
explanation: Pre-trip occurs before you leave for your trip, En-route occurs during any break you take while driving, and post-trip occurs when your route is finished.

text: Why should you signal continuously while turning?
explanation: Signal continuously. You need both hands on the wheel to turn safely. Don't cancel the signal until you have completed the turn.

text: You are driving on a two-lane road. An oncoming vehicle drifts into your lane and is headed straight for you. Which of the following should you do?
explanation: If an oncoming driver has drifted into your lane, a move to your right is best. If that driver realizes what has happened, the natural response will be to return to his or her own lane.

text: Which of these statements is true about causes of vehicle fires?
explanation: The following are some causes of vehicle fires: spilled fuel, improper use of flares, under-inflated tires , duals that touch, short circuits, loose connections, improper fueling, loose fuel connections, flammable cargo, improperly sealed or loaded cargo or with poor ventilation.

text: What three things related to cargo are the driver's responsibility?
explanation: You must make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured before each trip.

text: What is your most valuable way to see the sides and rear while driving?
explanation: It's important to know what's going on behind and to the sides. Check your mirrors regularly. Check more often in special situations.

text: When checking lights, you should
explanation: Make sure the parking brake is set, turn off the engine, and take the key with you. Turn on headlights (low beams) and four-way emergency flashers and get out of the vehicle.

text: On a two-way, two-lane road, if you must stop on a hill, how far back should you place your reflective triangles?
explanation: If line of sight view is obstructed due to hill or curve, move the rear-most triangle to a point back down the road (up to 500 feet) so warning is provided.

text: Name some things you should check on the FRONT of your vehicle during your pre-trip inspection.
explanation: Go to front of vehicle and check that low beams are on and both of the four-way flashers are working, check that high beams, parking, clearance, side-marker, and identification lights work. Turn on right turn signal and start walk-around inspection, then check the left turn.

text: Which of the following best describes how sleep debt can be paid off?
explanation: If you don't sleep enough, you "owe" more sleep to yourself. This debt can only be paid off by sleeping.

text: How can you avoid wet brake problems?
explanation: Avoid driving through deep puddles or flowing water if possible. If not, you should: slow down and place transmission in a low gear, and gently put on the brakes.

text: When you start to drive down a steep downgrade, which of the following should influence your choice of speed?
explanation: Your most important consideration is to select a speed that is not too fast for the: total weight of the vehicle and cargo, length of the grade, steepness of the grade, road conditions, and weather.

text: Always try to back toward the driver's side because
explanation: Back to the driver's side so that you can see better. Backing toward the right side is very dangerous because you can't see as well. If you back and turn toward the driver's side, you can watch the rear of your vehicle by looking out the side window.

text: On which of the following types of fires should you NOT use a B:C fire extinguisher?
explanation: The B:C type fire extinguisher is designed to work on electrical fires and burning liquids, not on wood.

text: You do not have a hazardous materials endorsement on your commercial driver's license. You are asked to haul hazardous materials in a placarded vehicle. You should:
explanation: You can drive a vehicle that carries hazardous materials if it does not require placards. If it requires placards, you cannot drive it unless your driver license has the hazardous materials endorsement.

text: You are testing the stopping action of service brakes on a hydraulic system. Which of these can mean there is a problem?
explanation: With hydraulic systems a delay in braking, spongy or unusual feeling pedal, or pulling to one side or the other can mean there are issues.

text: The center of gravity of a load:
explanation: When more cargo is piled up in a truck, the "center of gravity" moves higher up from the road. The truck becomes easier to turn over.

text: As part of your pre-trip inspection; you must
explanation: As part of your Vehicle inspection, make sure the truck is not overloaded and the cargo is balanced and secured properly.

text: Which of these statements about double clutching and shifting is true?
explanation: Use either the tachometer or the speedometer and upshift/downshift at the right rpm or road speed.

text: Gross combination weight rating or GCWR means the following?
explanation: The Gross Combination Weight Rating (GCWR) is the value specified by the manufacturer of the power unit and the towed unit(s), including load,  or any combination thereof.

text: Which of these statements about managing space is true?
explanation: Remember that when the road is slippery, you need much more space to stop, and should keep more space in front of your vehicle.

text: The road you are driving on becomes very slippery due to glare ice, which of these is a good thing to do in such a situation?
explanation: If the surface is icy, reduce speed to a crawl and stop driving as soon as you can safely do so.

text: Which of these statements about downshifting is true?
explanation: Slow down to a safe speed, and downshift to the right gear before entering the curve. This lets you use some power through the curve to help the vehicle be more stable while turning. It also allows you to speed up as soon as you are out of the curve.

text: If a straight vehicle goes into a front wheel skid it will:
explanation: In a front-wheel skid, the front end tends to go in a straight line regardless of how much you turn the steering wheel. On a very slippery surface, you may not be able to steer around a curve or turn.

text: Which of these statements about cold weather driving is true?
explanation: Use windshield washer antifreeze to prevent freezing of the washer liquid.

text: Retarders are used by?
explanation: There are four basic types of retarders: exhaust, engine, hydraulic, and electric.

text: You must drive on a slippery road. Which of these is a good thing to do in such a situation?
explanation: I It will take longer to stop, and it will be harder to turn without skidding, when the road is slippery. Wet roads can double stopping distance at any speed, reduce speed, and slow down gradually on a wet road.

text: Which of these is a sign of tire failure?
explanation: When driving a loud bang, vibrations, or heavy steering can indicate that a tire has failed.

text: Which of these statements about marking a stopped vehicle is true?
explanation: If line of sight view is obstructed due to hill or curve, move the rear-most triangle to a point back down the road so warning is provided up to 500 feet.

text: Which of these is a good driving rule for work zones?
explanation: Driving in work zones require extra care, and because of the workers risking their lives to keep the road in good condition you should drive slowly, use your hazards, and use your brake lights as communication devices.

text: To transport cargo safely, which of the following are you NOT responsible for?
explanation: You cannot inspect sealed loads, but you should check that you don't exceed gross weight and axle weight limits, and that the cargo is properly secured.

text: Which of the following CMVs are most likely to get stuck at a raised railroad crossing?
explanation: These trailers can get stuck on raised crossings: Low slung units (lowboy, car carrier, moving van, possum-belly livestock trailer). Single-axle tractor pulling a long trailer with its landing gear set to accommodate a tandem-axle tractor.

text: All of the following behaviors are signs of aggressive driving EXCEPT
explanation: Aggressive driving is the act of operating a motor vehicle in a selfish, bold, or pushy manner, without regard for the rights or safety of others. The frequent changes of speed is a distracted driver trait.

text: Which of these statements about escape ramps is true?
explanation: Escape ramps are made to stop runaway vehicles safely without injuring drivers and passengers. Escape ramps save lives, equipment and cargo.

text: How often must you stop to check your cargo while on the road?
explanation: Inspect the cargo and its securing devices again within the first 50 miles after beginning a trip, and you will need to inspect again after you have driven for 3 hours or 150 miles and after every break you take during driving.

text: Bridge formulas permit
explanation: A bridge formula permits less maximum axle weight for axles that are closer together. This is to prevent overloading bridges and roadways

text: As your blood alcohol concentration (BAC) rises, what happens?
explanation: Alcohol affects more and more of the brain as BAC builds up. The first part of the brain affected controls judgment and self-control.

text: Which of these statements about cargo loading is true?
explanation: You must keep weights within legal limits. States have maximums for GVWRs, GCWRs, and axle weights.

text: What is the most important thing to remember about emergency braking?
explanation: Emergency braking does not mean pushing down on the brake pedal as hard as you can. That will only keep the wheels locked up and cause a skid. If the wheels are skidding, you cannot control the vehicle.

text: What are the regulations on the height of splash guards?
explanation: Currently, there are no federal regulations governing the height of splash guards. Each state is free to issue its own regulations, and those regulations vary from one state to another.

text: The tendency for long commercial vehicles to swing wide on turns is called
explanation: When a vehicle goes around a corner, the rear wheels follow a different path than the front wheels. This is called off-tracking. Longer vehicles will off-track more, the rear wheels of the powered unit will off-track some, and the rear wheels of the trailer will off-track even more.

text: What is hydroplaning?
explanation: In some weather, water or slush collects on the road. When this happens, your vehicle can hydroplane. It's like water skiing--the tires lose their contact with the road and have little or no traction. You may not be able to steer or brake.

text: When driving a commercial vehicle with a height over 13 feet, you should
explanation: Hitting overhead objects is dangerous. Make sure you always have overhead clearance.

text: Your blood alcohol concentration (BAC) depends on your
explanation: BAC is determined by the amount of alcohol you drink, how fast you drink, and your weight.

text: The purpose of retarders is to
explanation: Retarders help slow a vehicle, reducing the need for using your brakes. They reduce brake wear and give you another way to slow down.

text: Which of the following provide extra gears on some trucks?
explanation: Multi-speed rear axles and auxiliary transmissions are used on many vehicles to provide extra gears. You usually control them by a selector knob or switch on the gearshift lever of the main transmission.

text: Which one of these is not part of the engine compartment pre-trip inspection?
explanation: Oil Level: Indicate where dipstick is located and see that oil level is within safe operating range. Engine Compartment Belts Check belts for snugness up to 3/4-inch play at center of belt with no cracks or frays. Hoses: Inspect hoses for condition and leaks. Valve clearance is the incorrect answer.

text: How do you test hydraulic brakes for leaks?
explanation: If the vehicle has hydraulic brakes, pump the brake pedal three times. Then apply firm pressure to the pedal and hold for five seconds. The pedal should not move. If it does, there may be a leak or other problem.

text: Which of the following is most likely to cause a vehicle fire?
explanation: The following are some causes of vehicle fires: Spilled fuel not properly cleaned up; improper use of flares; under-inflated tires and duals that touch; short circuits due to damaged insulation or loose connections; driver smoking in or around the vehicle; improper fueling and loose fuel connections; flammable cargo, improperly sealed or loaded cargo, with poor ventilation.

text: If the vehicle in front of you is smaller than yours, it can probably
explanation: Remember, if the vehicle ahead of you is smaller than yours, it can probably stop faster than you can. You may crash if you are following too closely.

text: How far ahead should you be looking while driving in town?
explanation: Most good drivers look at least 12 to 15 seconds ahead. That means looking ahead the distance you will travel in 12 to 15 seconds. At lower speeds, that's about one block.

text: Name two causes of fires.
explanation: The following are some causes of vehicle fires: spilled fuel, improper use of flares, under-inflated tires, duals that touch, short circuits, loose connections, improper fueling, loose fuel connections, flammable cargo that is improperly sealed or loaded cargo or has poor ventilation.

text: When should you review the post-trip inspection report from the previous driver?
explanation: Before performing the pre-trip inspection on your vehicle, you must review the inspection report made by the previous driver. Only if defects reported earlier have been certified as repaired or not needed to be repaired, should you sign the previous driver's report.

text: While driving, you see a small square cardboard box ahead in your lane. You should:
explanation: Steering around the object when it is safe to do is the safest action.

text: In which of the following two situations should you downshift?
explanation: Special conditions where you should downshift are: before starting down a hill slow down and shift down to a speed that you can control without using the brakes hard, and before entering a curve slow down to a safe speed and downshift to the right gear before entering the curve.

text: Which of these can cause the vehicle to skid?
explanation: Turning too sharply, not enough weight on the front axle, and over acceleration are all example of how to cause vehicle skids.

text: When looking ahead of your vehicle while driving you should
explanation: Good drivers shift their attention back and forth, near and far.

text: Which of these statements about engine overheating is true?
