explanation: Never remove the radiator cap or any part of the pressurized system until the system has cooled. Steam and boiling water can spray under pressure and cause severe burns.

text: Escape ramps are
explanation: Escape ramps are made to stop runaway vehicles safely without injuring drivers and passengers.

text: The braking effect of the engine is greatest when the engine is _____ the governed rpm and the transmission is in the ______ gears.
explanation: The braking effect of the engine is greatest when it is near the governed rpms and the transmission is in the lower gears.

text: Which of the following materials can be used in a Class ABC fire extinguisher to put out fires?
explanation: An ABC fire extinguisher is a multi-purpose extinguisher and usually uses dry chemicals.

text: You are checking your brakes and suspension system for a pre-trip inspection. What are you looking for?
explanation: Brake linings must not be loose or soaked with oil or grease, and must not be dangerously thin, broken, or missing.

text: How does the height of the vehicle's center of gravity affect safe handling?
explanation: High center of gravity means that much of the load's weight is carried high up off the road. This makes the vehicle top-heavy and easy to roll over.

text: In rain, you should reduce your speed by?
explanation: Wet roads can double stopping distance. You must drive slower to be able to stop in the same distance as on a dry road. Reduce speed by about one-third on a wet road.

text: Gross vehicle weight rating or GVWR means the following:
explanation: The Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) is the value specified by the manufacturer as the loaded weight of a single vehicle.

text: Which of these statements about tires and hot weather driving is true?
explanation: Inspect the tires more often than usual, or every two hours or every 100 miles when driving in very hot weather.

text: Tank vehicles are used to carry any liquid and are dangerous to drive because:
explanation: Hauling liquids in tanks requires special skills because of the high center of gravity and liquid movement.

text: How often should you check your mirrors?
explanation: You should check your mirrors every 8 to 10 seconds to know what is going on around your vehicle at all times.

text: How can you determine if your vehicle is equipped with an antilock braking system (ABS)?
explanation: Most vehicles now have an ABS light on the instrument panel that will light up briefly when the vehicle is started to alert you that the vehicle has ABS, you can check to see if the vehicle was built on or after March 1, 1998, or you can check for wires from your brakes.

text: If a straight vehicle (no trailer or articulation) goes into a front-wheel skid, it will
explanation: In a front-wheel skid, the front end tends to go in a straight line regardless of how much you turn the steering wheel.

text: When driving at night, use your low beams when an oncoming vehicle is within
explanation: Dim your lights within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle and when following another vehicle within 500 feet.

text: What are some things to do at an accident scene to prevent another accident?
explanation: Protect the accident area: if your vehicle is involved in the accident, try to get it to the side of the road, if you're stopping to help, park away from the accident, put on your flashers, and set out reflective triangles to warn other traffic.

text: Why should you be in the proper gear before starting down a hill?
explanation: Do not try to downshift after your speed has already built up. You will not be able to shift into a lower gear. You may not even be able to get back into any gear and all engine braking effect will be lost.

text: When driving through roadway work zones, you should
explanation: When people are working on the road, it is a hazard. There may be narrower lanes, sharp turns, or uneven surfaces.

text: To prevent skidding, you should
explanation: Don't over steer, over accelerate, or over brake by adjusting to conditions while driving. These include traction, curves, visibility, traffic and hills.

text: A car suddenly cuts in front of you, creating a hazard. What should you NOT do?
explanation: What you should do is make a lane change or slow down to prevent a crash if the car suddenly cuts in front of you, and you should not honk or stay close behind the car.

text: A key principle to remember about loading cargo is to keep the load
explanation: Poor weight balance can make vehicle handling unsafe. The cargo should be loaded so that it is balanced in the cargo area.

text: What can you do at an accident scene to help avoid another accident?
explanation: When you're in an accident and not seriously hurt, you need to act to prevent further damage or injury. The basic steps to be taken at any accident are to: protect the area, notify authorities, and care for the injured.

text: As the blood alcohol concentration or BAC goes up, what will happen?
explanation: Accident statistics show that the chance of a crash is much greater for drivers who have been drinking than for drivers who have not.

text: When does hydroplaning occur?
explanation: Hydroplaning is more likely if tire pressure is low, or the tread is worn. The grooves in a tire carry away the water; if they aren't deep, they don't work well.

text: The height of the vehicle center of gravity is very important for safe handling. A high center of gravity means you are more likely to do the following:
explanation: The height of the vehicle's center of gravity is very important for safe handling. A high center of gravity means you are more likely to tip over. It is most dangerous in curves, or if you have to swerve to avoid a hazard.

text: Which of these statements about vehicle fires is true?
explanation: A burning tire must be cooled. Lots of water may be required.

text: Cargo should have at least one tie down for each:
explanation: Cargo should have at least one tie-down for each ten feet of cargo. Make sure you have enough tiedowns to meet this need.

text: During a pre-trip inspection you discover an exhaust leak or power steering leak, you should:
explanation: If you find anything unsafe during the Vehicle inspection, get it fixed, or use a different vehicle. Federal and state laws forbid operating an unsafe vehicle.

text: Cargo that is not loaded or secured properly can cause many problems. Which of these statements is true?
explanation: Loose cargo that falls off a vehicle can cause traffic problems and others could be hurt or killed. Loose cargo could hurt or kill you during a quick stop or crash. Your vehicle could be damaged by an overload.

text: You are driving a vehicle that could safely be driven at 55 mph on an open road. Traffic is now heavy and moving at 35 mph although the speed limit is 55. The safest speed for your vehicle in this situation is most likely:
explanation: Going with the flow of traffic is safer and easier.

text: Name two situations in which the legal maximum weights may not be safe to haul.
explanation: During bad weather or in mountains, it may not be safe to operate at legal maximum weights. Take this into account before driving.

text: What things should you check during a trip?
explanation: For safety you should: watch gauges for signs of trouble, use your senses to check for problems (look, listen, smell, feel), and check critical items when you stop.

text: It is important to shift gears correctly to keep
explanation: Correct shifting of gears is important. If you can't get your vehicle into the right gear while driving, you will have less control.

text: If you feel drowsy while driving, what should you do?
explanation: If you are drowsy, the only safe cure is to get off the road and get some sleep. If you don't, you risk your life and the lives of others.

text: When the engine is at operating RPM's, the _____ pressure is built up from 80 to 100 PSI within 45 seconds in a dual system?
explanation: When the engine is at operating rpms, the air pressure should build from 85 to 100 psi within 45 seconds in dual air systems.

text: You are driving a 40-foot vehicle at 35 mph. Driving conditions are ideal. What is the least amount of space that you should keep in front of your vehicle to be safe?
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: Which of the following statements about tank vehicles is true?
explanation: Hauling liquids in tanks requires special skills because of the high center of gravity and liquid movement.

text: Which fires can you use water to put out?
explanation: Water can be used on wood, paper, or cloth, but don't use water on an electrical fire or a gasoline fire. A burning tire must be cooled. Lots of water may be required.

text: Which of these is NOT caused by rough acceleration?
explanation: Rough acceleration can cause mechanical damage. When pulling a trailer, rough acceleration can damage the coupling.

text: Which of the following should you NOT do in the event of hydraulic brake failure?
explanation: When the system won't build up pressure here are some things you can do: downshift, pump the Brakes, use the parking brake, and find an Escape Route.

text: How many different hazard classes are there?
explanation: Hazardous materials are categorized into nine major hazard classes and additional categories for consumer commodities and combustible liquids.

text: What placard must be used with an inhalation hazard load?
explanation: If the words inhalation hazard appear on the shipping paper or package, the rules require display of the poison inhalation hazard or poison gas placards, as appropriate.

text: A truck carrying explosives has crashed with another vehicle. You should not pull them apart until?
explanation: Remove all explosives before separating vehicles involved in a collision. Place the explosives at least 200 feet from the vehicles and occupied buildings. Stay a safe distance away.

text: The shippers' package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to the regulations. The only exception is when:
explanation: The signed shipper's certification appears on the original shipping paper. The only exceptions are when a shipper is a private carrier transporting their own product and when the package is provided by the carrier.

text: Which signals may be used to warn of a stopped vehicle that contains explosives?
explanation: You might break down and have to use stopped vehicle signals. Use reflective triangles or red electric lights, never use burning signals, such as flares or fuses around a tank used for Class 3 (Flammable Liquids) or Division 2.1 (Flammable Gas) whether loaded or empty, and any vehicle loaded with Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 Explosives.

text: Class 8 is placarded as?
explanation: Class 8 - Corrosive Materials.

text: The transport index of a radioactive material:
explanation: Radiation surrounds each package, passing through all nearby packages. To deal with this problem, the number of packages you can load together is controlled. Their closeness to people, animals, and unexposed film is also controlled. The transport index tells the degree of control needed during transportation.

text: A properly prepared uniform hazardous waste manifest:
explanation: When transporting hazardous wastes, you must sign by hand and carry a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest

text: An "A or W" the first column of the hazardous materials table means:
explanation: Means the hazardous material described in Column 2 is subject to the HMR only when offered or intended for transport by air and/or water unless it is a hazardous substance or hazardous waste.

text: When is it necessary to ground the trailer?
explanation: Ground a cargo tank correctly before filling it through an open filling hole. Ground the tank before opening the filling hole and maintain the ground until after closing the filling hole.

text: The total transport index of all radioactive material packages in a single vehicle must not exceed _____.
explanation: The total transport index of all packages in a single vehicle must not exceed 50.

text: Which of the following must drivers have in their possession while transporting class 1.1 or 1.3 explosives?
explanation: If transporting Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives, you must have a written route plan and follow that plan. Carriers prepare the route plan in advance and give the driver a copy

text: On a public roadway with a placarded tanker hauling flammable liquids, when can you transfer from one tanker to another?
explanation: Don't transfer flammable liquid from one vehicle to another on a public roadway except in an emergency.

text: If in a materials class is the word "forbidden", what kinds of materials are you still allowed to haul?
explanation: Column 3 shows a material's hazard class or division, or the entry "Forbidden." Never transport a "Forbidden" material.

text: What is the shape and size of a placard?
explanation: Placards must be readable from all four directions. They must be at least 250 mm square, turned upright on a point, in a diamond shape.

text: When handling packages of explosives, you must:
explanation: Use extra care to protect explosives. Never use hooks or other metal tools. Never drop, throw, or roll packages. Protect explosive packages from other cargo that might cause damage

text: Which of the following, if required, must the shipper put on the package.
explanation: Shippers print required markings directly on the package, an attached label, or tag. When required, the shipper will put the following on the package: the name and address of shipper or consignee, the hazardous material's shipping name and identification number, and the labels required.

text: Whenever your vehicle is placarded, do not drive near open fires unless you:
explanation: Never drive a placarded vehicle near open fires unless you can safely pass without stopping.

text: A hazard class name or ID number may not be used to describe a:
explanation: A hazard class name or ID number may not be used to describe non-hazardous materials.

text: Column 1 or the symbols column the "I" means:
explanation: Identifies a proper shipping name that is used to describe materials in international transportation. A different shipping name may be used when only domestic transportation is involved.

text: What action should you take if you discover your hazardous materials shipment leaking at a rest stop but there is no phone available?
explanation: Never continue driving with hazardous materials leaking from your vehicle in order to find a phone booth, truck stop, help, or similar reason. If hazardous materials are spilling from your vehicle: Park it, secure the area, stay there, send someone else for help.

text: In what location must you keep your shipping papers that describe any hazardous materials?
explanation: You must identify shipping papers related to hazardous materials or keep them on top of other shipping papers. You must also keep shipping papers: In a pouch on the driver's door, or in clear view within reach while driving, or on the driver's seat when out of the vehicle.

text: You must never smoke or perform any activity involving fire within 25 feet of
explanation: Do not smoke within 25 feet of a placarded cargo tank used for Class 3 (flammable liquids),Division 2.1 (gases), or Class 1 (Explosives) Class 3 (Flammable Liquids) Class 4 (Flammable Solids) Class 4.2 (Spontaneously Combustible). Oxidizers fall under flammable solids.

text: What is the purpose of a driver placarding their vehicle?
explanation: The three intents are: Contain the Material; Communicate the Risk; and Assure Safe Drivers and Equipment.

text: If you are already carrying 100 pounds of silver cyanide, what precautions must you take if you are given papers at a dock to carry 100 cartons of battery acid?
explanation: Acids, corrosive materials, or other acidic materials which could release hydrocyanic acid. For Example: Cyanides, Inorganic, Silver Cyanide, or Sodium Cyanide.

text: The other two places where the hazardous material identification number must appear are
explanation: In addition to the two main locations for the hazardous material identification number, you must also display it on the cargo tanks and on all bulk packaging.

text: When there is a hazardous materials emergency, what should you do?
explanation: Follow this checklist: Check to see that your driving partner is OK, keep shipping papers with you, keep people far away and upwind, warn others of the danger, and call for help.

text: Whose signatures must be on a Hazardous Waste Manifest?
explanation: When transporting hazardous wastes, you must sign by hand and carry a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest. Shippers must prepare, date, and sign by hand the manifest. Each carrier transporting the shipment must sign by hand the manifest. Each copy must have all needed signatures and dates, including those of the person to whom you delivered the waste.

text: To obtain a hazardous materials endorsement, you must know how to
explanation: The hazardous materials test required if you want to haul hazardous materials as defined in 49 CFR 383.5. In order to obtain this endorsement, you are also required to pass a Transportation Security Administration (TSA) background check.

text: A shipping paper for hazardous materials must include
explanation: A shipping paper for hazardous materials must include: Page numbers if the shipping paper has more than one page, a proper shipping description for each hazardous material, and shipper's certification signed by the shipper saying they prepared the shipment according to the regulations.

text: The driver must always also carry on his person a dated certificate signed by the employer. This certificate must also be updated every
explanation: The regulations require that hazardous materials employees be trained and tested at least once every three years

text: If an X or RQ is in the HM column of a shipping paper entry, the:
explanation: If a shipping paper describes both hazardous and non-hazardous products, the hazardous materials must be: Entered first. Highlighted in a contrasting color or identified by an "X" placed before the shipping description in a column captioned "HM". The letters "RQ" may be used instead of "X" if a reportable quantity needs to be identified.

text: When there is a hazardous materials emergency. You should:
explanation: Follow this checklist: Check to see that your driving partner is OK, keep the shipping papers with you, keep people far away and upwind, warn others of the danger, and call for help.

text: The placard is shaped?
explanation: Placards must be readable from all four directions. They must be at least 10 3/4 inches square, turned upright on a point, in a diamond shape.

text: Which of the following choices is a hazard class you are not allowed to smoke around?
explanation: Do not smoke within 25 feet of a placarded cargo tank used for Class 3 (flammable liquids),Division 2.1 (gases), or Class 1 (Explosives) Class 3 (Flammable Liquids) Class 4 (Flammable Solids) Class 4.2 (Spontaneously Combustible). Oxidizers fall under flammable solids.

text: Which of the following is one of the three keys to identify which placard to use?
explanation: The three keys are: Materials Hazard Class, Amount Being Shipped, and amount of all Hazardous materials of all classes on your vehicle.

text: If there is an "RQ" before or after the item description on the shipping paper, it means the:
explanation: The letters RQ may appear before or after the basic description. You or your employer must report any spill of these materials, which occurs in a reportable quantity.

text: Symbol column, the letter "G" stands for?
explanation: Means this hazardous material described in Column 2 is a generic shipping name. A generic shipping name must be accompanied by a technical name on the shipping paper.

text: Drivers of placarded vehicles transporting hazardous materials must stop _____ to _____ feet from the nearest rail of a railroad.
explanation: You must stop 15 to 50 feet before the nearest rail. Proceed only when you are sure no train is coming, and you can clear the tracks without stopping. Don't shift gears while crossing the tracks.

text: Carriers must give each driver who transports class 1.1 or 1.3 explosives:
explanation: A carrier must give each driver transporting Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives a copy of Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations (FMCSR), Part 397.

text: Animals and human foodstuffs should not be loaded in the same vehicle with?
explanation: Poison or poison inhalation hazard labeled material should not be loaded with animal or human food unless the poison package is over packed in an approved way.

text: A safe haven is one place you can leave a vehicle unattended that is hauling explosives. What is the best description of a safe haven?
explanation: A safe haven is an approved place for parking unattended vehicles loaded with explosives. Designation of authorized safe havens is usually made by local authorities.

text: Who is responsible for finding out what permits or special routes you will need to haul hazardous materials?
explanation: The driver makes sure the shipper has identified, marked, and labeled the hazardous materials properly. Refuses leaking packages and shipments. Placards vehicle when loading, if required. Safely transports the shipment without delay. Follows all special rules about transporting hazardous materials. Keeps hazardous materials shipping papers and emergency.

text: You do not have a hazardous materials endorsement on your commercial driver license. When can you legally haul hazardous materials?
explanation: The rules require all drivers of placarded vehicles to learn how to safely load and transport hazardous products. They must have a commercial driver license with the hazardous materials endorsement.

text: Nitric acid cannot be loaded:
explanation: Do not load nitric acid above any other product.

text: When shippers package the material, they are trying to
explanation: The shipper must package, mark, and label the materials; prepare shipping papers; provide emergency response information; and supply placards. Making the load easy to identify.

text: To determine if you need to use placards, which of the following is NOT something you need to know?
explanation: To decide which placards to use, you need to know: The hazard class of the materials, the amount of hazardous materials shipped the total weight of all classes of hazardous materials in your vehicle.

text: Which of the following is NOT an acceptable type of marking for hazardous materials?
explanation: Names shown in italics are not proper shipping names and not acceptable, those in roman type above it are.

text: Which of the following three hazard classes should NOT be placed into a temperature-controlled trailer (one with a heater or air conditioner unit)?
explanation: The three hazard classes that must be kept in a trailer without automatic heating and cooling are: explosives (1), flammable gases (2.1), and flammable liquids (3), because these are particularly volatile.

text: What are the major differences between cargo tanks and portable tanks?
explanation: Cargo tanks are bulk packaging permanently attached to a vehicle. Cargo tanks remain on the vehicle when you load and unload them. Portable tanks are bulk packaging, which are not permanently attached to a vehicle.

text: The Hazardous Materials Table has ______ columns.
explanation: The Hazardous Materials Table lists every type of material that the US Department of Transportation has designated as a hazardous material for the purpose of transportation. There are eight columns in the Hazardous Materials Table, describing the mode of transportation (air, ground, water, etc.), the name of the material, its hazard class, its identification number, and so on.

text: To communicate the risk of hazardous materials, shippers do all of the following EXCEPT
explanation: To communicate the risk of hazardous materials, regulations require shippers to put placards on trucks, put hazard warning labels on packages, provide proper shipping papers, and supply emergency response information.

text: A hazardous material's identification number is determined by
explanation: The shipper uses the hazardous materials regulations to determine the product's: Identification number, proper shipping name, hazard class, packing group, correct packaging, correct label and markings, and correct placards.

text: You have loaded a hazardous material into a cargo tank. What must you do before you move the vehicle?
explanation: Close all manholes and valves before moving a tank of hazardous materials, no matter how small the amount in the tank or how short the distance.

text: You have a vehicle without racks to hold cylinders or compressed gas. You may load such cylinders only if they are
explanation: If your vehicle doesn't have racks to hold cylinders, the cargo space floor must be flat. The cylinders must be: Held upright, in racks attached to the vehicle or in boxes that will keep them from turning over and may be loaded in a horizontal position if it is designed so the relief valve is in the vapor space.

text: When transporting chlorine in cargo tanks, what must you have?
explanation: A driver transporting chlorine in cargo tanks must have an approved gas mask in the vehicle. The driver must also have an emergency kit for controlling leaks in dome cover plate fittings on the cargo tank.

text: When can the shipping name or the hazardous material identification number be abbreviated on the shipping paper?
explanation: Identification number, shipping name, and hazard class must not be abbreviated unless specifically authorized in the hazardous materials regulations

text: Your engine runs a pump when you are delivering compressed gas. After finishing the delivery, when should you turn off the engine?
explanation: Unless your engine runs a pump for product transfer, turn it off when loading or unloading. If you use the engine, turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose.

text: Which of the following materials are acceptable floor liners for transporting Division 1.1 or 1.2 materials?
explanation: Use a floor lining with Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3. The floors must be tight, and the liner must be either nonmetallic material or non-ferrous metal.

text: Where are the two main places that the hazardous material identification number appears?
explanation: Always make sure that the shipper shows the correct basic description on the shipping paper and verifies that the proper labels are shown on the packages.

text: You can't haul 1.1 explosives with?
explanation: Do not transport Division 1.1 or 1.2 in vehicle combinations if: There is a marked or placarded cargo tank in the combination. The other vehicle in the combination contains: Division 1.1 A (Initiating Explosives). Packages of Class 7 (Radioactive) materials labeled "Yellow III." Division 2.3 (Poisonous Gas) or Division 6.1 (Poisonous) materials. Hazardous materials in a portable tank, on a DOT Spec 106A or 110A tank.

text: When fueling a placarded vehicle, someone must always be:
explanation: Turn off your engine before fueling a motor vehicle containing hazardous materials. Someone must always be at the nozzle, controlling fuel flow.

text: What is Chemtrec's primary job?
explanation: The Chemical Transportation Emergency Center (CHEMTREC) in Washington also has a 24-hour toll-free line. CHEMTREC was created to provide emergency personnel with technical information about the physical properties of hazardous materials.

text: How many identical placards is a vehicle hauling hazardous materials required to display and where?
explanation: A placarded vehicle must have at least four identical placards. They are put on the front, rear, and both sides.

text: With most hazardous materials, you may park within 5 feet of the road briefly, if your work requires it. The materials that are the exception and do not allow parking this close to the road are?
explanation: Never park with Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives within five feet of the traveled part of the road.

text: Who is responsible for checking to be sure the shipper correctly named, labeled, and marked a hazardous materials shipment?
explanation: The carrier prior to transportation, checks that the shipper correctly described, marked, labeled, and otherwise prepared the shipment for transportation.

text: There are three lists that drivers, shippers and carriers use to find out if a material is regulated as hazardous.  Which one of these choices is not one of those three lists?
explanation: There are three main lists used by shippers, carriers, and drivers when trying to identify hazardous materials. Hazardous Materials Table, List of Hazardous Substances and Reportable Quantities, List of Marine Pollutants.

text: Who is responsible for safely transporting a hazardous material shipment without delay and keeping the shipping papers in the right place?
explanation: The driver cakes sure the shipper has identified, marked, and labeled the hazardous materials properly. Refuses leaking packages and shipments. Placards vehicle when loading, if required. Safely transports the shipment without delay. Follows all special rules about transporting hazardous materials. Keeps hazardous materials shipping papers and emergency

text: When a spill occurs of an "RQ" substance the carrier has within _____ to report the spill.
explanation: Carriers must make detailed written reports within 30 days of an incident

text: You are going to be hauling hazardous materials over a route you do not know well. When should you check the route and get the permits needed for the trip:
explanation: It is your job as driver to find out if you need permits or must use special routes. Make sure you have all needed papers before starting.

text: A placard is required for a combustible liquid at?
explanation: The tank vehicle test required if you want to haul any liquid or gaseous materials in a tank or tanks having an individual rated capacity of more than 119 gallons.

text: Column 1 or the symbols column the "D" sign means:
explanation: Means the proper shipping name is appropriate for describing materials for domestic transportation but may not be proper for international transportation.

text: What is a technical name?
explanation: A technical name is a specific chemical that makes the product hazardous

text: Which of the following hazard classes uses a transport index to determine how much of it can be loaded on a single vehicle for transport?
explanation: Some packages of Class 7 (Radioactive) materials bear a number called the "transport index." The shipper labels these packages Radioactive II or Radioactive III and prints the package's transport index on the label.

text: Which of the following are necessary qualifications for non-bulk packaging?
explanation: Non-bulk packaging: A maximum capacity of 450 L (119 gallons) as a receptacle for a liquid; A maximum net mass less than 400 kg (882 pounds) and a maximum capacity of 450 L (119 gallons) or less as a receptacle for a solid; or A water capacity greater than 454 kg (1,000 pounds) or less as a receptacle for a gas.

text: Why does the government regulate the transportation of hazardous materials?
explanation: Hazardous materials are products that pose a risk to health, safety, and property during transportation. Hazardous materials include explosives, various types of gas, solids, flammable and combustible liquid, and other materials. Because of the risks involved and the potential consequences these risks impose, all levels of government regulate the handling of hazardous materials.

text: A properly prepared uniform hazardous waste manifest
explanation: When transporting hazardous wastes, you must sign by hand and carry a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest. Shippers must prepare, date, and sign by hand the manifest. Each carrier transporting the shipment must sign by hand the manifest. Each copy must have all needed signatures and dates, including those of the person to whom you delivered the waste.

text: Identification numbers are preceded by the letters "UN" or "NA." The letters "NA" are associated with proper shipping names that are used where?
explanation: The letters "NA" are associated with proper shipping names that are only used within the United States and to and from Canada

text: You may not park a vehicle carrying hazardous materials within _____ feet of an open fire.
explanation: Do not park within 300 feet of: A bridge, tunnel, building, a place where people gather, or an open fire. If you must park to do your job, do so only briefly.

text: What type fire extinguisher is required in the power unit of a placarded vehicle?
explanation: The power unit of placarded vehicles must have a fire extinguisher with a UL rating of 10 B:C or more.

text: To find out if a shipment includes a hazardous material, you should:
explanation: Learn to recognize shipments of hazardous materials. To find out if the shipment includes hazardous materials, look at the shipping paper.

text: Cylinders containing compressed gases may only be loaded onto a vehicle without a flat floor or platform if the cylinders are:
explanation: The cylinders must be: Held upright, or in racks attached to the vehicle

text: You are hauling hazardous materials and you find that one of your tires is leaking. You must:
explanation: Do not drive with a tire that is leaking or flat except to the nearest safe place to fix it.

text: Shipping papers must always be kept in one place when driving with hazardous materials. Where when wearing your seat belt?
explanation: Drivers are to keep hazardous materials shipping papers: In a pouch on the driver's door, or in clear view within immediate reach while the seat belt is fastened while driving, or on the driver's seat when out of the vehicle.

text: The hazardous material identification numbers must be displayed on portable tanks, cargo tanks, and other bulk packaging. The regulations require black _______ numbers.
explanation: The rules require black 100 mm (3.9 inch) numbers on orange panels, placards, or a white, diamond-shaped background if no placards are required.

text: The Emergency Response Guidebook (ERG)
explanation: The Department of Transportation has a guidebook for firefighters, police, and industry workers on how to protect themselves and the public from hazardous materials. The guide is indexed by proper shipping name and hazardous materials identification number. Emergency personnel look for these things on the shipping paper. That is why it is vital that the proper shipping name, identification number, label, and placards are correct.

text: You should stop before crossing a railroad grade if your vehicle is carrying _______ of chlorine.
explanation: Stop before a railroad crossing if your vehicle: Is placarded, carries any amount of chlorine, has cargo tanks, whether loaded or empty used for hazardous materials.

text: If you are in an accident involving hazardous materials, you should:
explanation: As a professional driver, your job at the scene of a crash or an incident is to: Keep people away. Limit the spread of material, if you can safely do so. Communicate the danger of the hazardous materials Provide emergency responders with the shipping papers and emergency response information.

text: It is recommended that when hauling a load of explosives 1.1 or 1.3 to use the fusees instead of the warning triangles if in an emergency or breakdown situation?
explanation: You might break down and have to use stopped vehicle signals. Use reflective triangles or red electric lights, never use burning signals, such as flares or fuses around a tank used for Class 3 (Flammable Liquids) or Division 2.1 (Flammable Gas) whether loaded or empty, and any vehicle loaded with Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 Explosives.

text: Who is responsible for packaging, labeling, and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers for a common carrier?
explanation: Must package, mark, and label the materials; prepare shipping papers; provide emergency response information; and supply placards.

text: Class 1.1 explosives must not be transported in a combination vehicle if the vehicle includes:
explanation: Do not transport Division 1.1 or 1.2 in vehicle combinations if: There is a marked or placarded cargo tank in the combination. The other vehicle in the combination contains: Division 1.1 A (Initiating Explosives). Packages of Class 7 (Radioactive) materials labeled "Yellow III." Division 2.3 (Poisonous Gas) or Division 6.1 (Poisonous) materials. Hazardous materials in a portable tank, on a DOT Spec 106A or 110A tank.

text: A vehicle contains 500 pounds each of explosives 1.1 and 1.3, you must use:
explanation: Use the larger or more dangerous placard of explosives 1.1.

text: A railroad crossing is ahead of you and you are hauling hazardous material. When you stop for the crossing, you can stop as far away as _____ feet from the nearest rail.
explanation: You must stop 15 to 50 feet before the nearest rail. Proceed only when you are sure no train is coming, and you can clear the tracks without stopping. Don't shift gears while crossing the tracks.

text: The hazardous waste manifest should be?
explanation: Hazardous waste need to be kept on top of all other paperwork and tabbed differently so that it is easy to find.

text: Column 1 or the symbols column the "+" sign means:
explanation: The "+" sign shows the proper shipping name, hazard class, and packing group to use, even if the material doesn't meet the hazard class definition

text: How often should you check the tires on a placarded trailer that has dual tires?
explanation: Check the tire mounting and air pressure. Inspect the tires every two hours, every 100 miles, and each time you stop.

text: Whenever you load or unload a cargo tank, a qualified person must be
explanation: The person in charge of loading and unloading a cargo tank must be sure a qualified person is always watching. This person watching the loading or unloading must: Be alert, have a clear view of the cargo tank, be within 25 feet of the tank, know of the hazards of the materials involved, know the procedures to follow in an emergency, and be authorized to move the cargo tank and able to do so.

text: Containment rules specify
explanation: Many hazardous products can injure or kill on contact. To protect drivers and others from contact, the rules tell shippers how to package safely. Similar rules tell drivers how to load, transport, and unload bulk tanks. These are containment rules.

text: Smoking must be at least _____ feet from most hazardous material?
explanation: Do not smoke within 25 feet of a placarded cargo tank used for Class 3 (flammable liquids),Division 2.1 (gases), or Class 1 (Explosives) Class 3 (Flammable Liquids) Class 4 (Flammable Solids) Class 4.2 (Spontaneously Combustible).

text: In the hazardous material table, column 3, any shipping name written in italics means?
explanation: Names shown in italics are not proper shipping names, those in roman type above it are.

text: If hazardous material is spilling from your vehicle, do not move your vehicle:
explanation: If hazardous materials are spilling from your vehicle, do not move it any more than safety requires.

text: How often must a driver that has the Hazmat Endorsement on their CDL retake the state Hazmat written test?
explanation: Generally, you must renew every five years, although some states may require more frequent reviews based on shorter license cycles. You will be required to submit new fingerprints at the time of renewal of the endorsement.

text: Do you need to stop before a railroad crossing if you are hauling 100 pounds of Division 4.3 materials?
explanation: Stop before a railroad crossing if your vehicle: Is placarded, carries any amount of chlorine, has cargo tanks whether loaded or empty used for hazardous materials.

text: You are transporting hazardous materials. When you are not behind the wheel, the shipping papers must be on the driver's seat or
explanation: You must also keep shipping papers: In a pouch on the driver's door, or in clear view within reach while driving, or on the driver's seat when out of the vehicle.

text: Which of the following is the proper definition of a cargo tank?
explanation: Cargo tanks are bulk packaging permanently attached to a vehicle. Cargo tanks remain on the vehicle when you load and unload them.

text: A truck has hazardous material, when can it be moved without the placards on the truck?
explanation: You are only allowed to move an improperly placarded vehicle during an emergency, in order to protect life or property

text: The basic description of a hazardous material includes the hazard class, the id number, and the proper shipping name. Which one must appear first on the shipping paper?
explanation: The basic description of hazardous materials includes the identification number, proper shipping name, hazard class or division, and the packing group, if any, in that order.

text: A vehicle contains 500 pounds each of Division 1.1 explosives and Division 1.2 explosives. You must have
explanation: The Placards Table 1 contains explosives, so any amount being transported needs to have placards displayed on the vehicle, with the proper division number.

text: When hauling hazardous materials, you must check any dual tires
explanation: If you have a placarded trailer with dual tires, you should check the tires at the start of each trip and each time that you stop for any reason, whether to rest or to refuel. Use a tire pressure gauge to get an accurate reading.

text: Which three hazard classes should not be loaded into a trailer that has a heater or air conditioner unit.
explanation: Anything explosive or flammable should not be loaded into a trailer with heaters or air conditioners because of the possibility of a malfunction creating a spark that could lead to an explosion.

text: The intent of hazardous materials regulations is to ensure safety, to contain the material, and:
explanation: The three intents are: Contain the Material; Communicate the Risk; and Assure Safe Drivers and Equipment.

text: If you are carrying Division 1.2 or 1.3 materials, how far away must you park from the traveled portion of the roadway?
explanation: Never park with Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives within five feet of the traveled part of the road.

text: Why must the shipper sign the shipping paper?
explanation: A statement on a shipping paper, signed by the shipper, saying they prepared the shipment properly according to law.

text: You find an overheated tire during an enroute inspection. If you are hauling hazardous materials, you must:
explanation: Remove any overheated tire. Place it a safe distance from your vehicle. Don't drive until you correct the cause of the overheating.

text: You must stop your bus between:
explanation: Stop your bus between 15 and 50 feet before railroad crossings. Listen and look in both directions for trains.

text: You need to evacuate your bus in an emergency, passengers should be directed to a safe place no less than _____ feet from the bus.
explanation: Passengers should be moved at least 100 feet from the bus.

text: Off tracking of the rear wheels is affected by:
explanation: Off tracking can be changed by speed, the sharpness of the turn, and the distance between the front and rear wheels.

text: Your bus is disabled. The bus, with riders aboard, may be towed or pushed to a safe place only:
explanation: Do not tow or push a disabled bus with riders aboard the vehicle, unless getting off would be unsafe. Only tow or push the bus to the nearest safe spot to discharge passengers.

text: When driving at more than 40 mph, what is the safe minimum following distance for ideal daytime conditions?
explanation: One good rule says you need at least one second for each 10 feet of vehicle length at speeds below 40 mph. At greater speeds, you must add 1 second for safety.

text: If you have riders aboard, you must never fuel your bus:
explanation: Avoid fueling your bus with riders on board unless absolutely necessary. Never refuel in a closed building with riders on board.

text: Carry-on baggage cannot be stored:
explanation: Do not allow riders to leave carry-on baggage in a doorway or aisle. There should be nothing in the aisle that might trip other riders.

text: The driver's seat should be adjusted:
explanation: Seat adjustments should only be made when the vehicle is stopped.

text: Buses may have recapped or re-grooved tires:
explanation: Recapped or re-grooved tires can be on any axle except the front or steer axel.

text: When you are not sure who has the right of way, the safest course of action is to:
explanation: When in doubt be courteous and allow the other driver the right of way.

text: Commercial bus drivers must have a CDL if:
explanation: You must have a CDL to operate a vehicle designed to transport 16 or more passengers including the driver.

text: The interlock system for the brake and accelerator on a transit coach may be used:
explanation: Urban mass transit coaches may have a brake and accelerator interlock system. The interlock applies the brakes and holds the throttle in idle position when the rear door is open, releasing when the door is closed. Do not use this safety feature in place of the parking brake.

text: According to federal regulations, the minimum depth required for tire tread on steering axle tires is _____ of an inch.
explanation: The minimum tread depth for commercial vehicles on the front or steer axel is 4/32 of an inch.

text: Which of these forms of signaling is not recommended?
explanation: Some drivers try to help out others by signaling when it is safe to pass. You should not do this. You could cause an accident. You could be blamed, and it could cost you many thousands of dollars.

text: The standee line is:
explanation: Buses designed to allow standing must have a two-inch line on the floor or some other means of showing riders where they cannot stand. This is called the standee line. All standing riders must stay behind it.

text: All the following should be in good working order before driving. Which item must be in good working order before the bus can leave?
explanation: The service brake should be tested and in working order before leaving.

text: At the end of each shift you should:
explanation: At the end of your shift you should do your post trip inspection, reporting any defects or repairs needed, and take all of your personal belongings.

text: Before you start to drive, you must inspect all of the following EXCEPT
explanation: Before you start to drive, you must check all the critical systems such as steering, brakes, tires, and emergency equipment to be sure they work properly. For the passengers' safety, you must also check such interior items as seats, handholds, railings, signaling devices, and emergency exits

text: The traffic light on a drawbridge is green. You should
explanation: You do not need to stop, but must slow down and make sure it's safe, when: there is a traffic light showing green, or the bridge has an attendant or traffic officer who controls traffic whenever the bridge opens

text: If you are performing your vehicle inspection test on a coach or transit bus, you will have to inspect the baggage compartments and make sure that the baggage compartment doors ________ securely.
explanation: As you check the outside of the bus, close any open emergency exits. Also, close any open access panels (for baggage, restroom service, engine, etc.) before driving, and make sure all are latched properly.

text: Bus accidents often happen
explanation: Bus accidents often happen at intersections. Use caution, even if a signal or stop sign controls other traffic.

text: When stopping for railroad tracks, you must stop no closer than how many feet before the nearest track?
explanation: You must stop between 15 and 50 feet from the nearest rail at a railroad crossing.

text: When transporting passengers on charter or intercity buses, you should explain passenger comfort and safety rules
explanation: Many charter and intercity carriers have passenger comfort and safety rules. Mention rules about smoking, drinking, or use of radio and tape players at the start of the trip. Explaining the rules at the start will help to avoid trouble later on.

text: Which of the following statements are true?
explanation: Do not tow or push a disabled bus with riders aboard the vehicle, unless getting off would be unsafe. Only tow or push the bus to the nearest safe spot to discharge passengers.

text: Federal regulations set an axle weight limit of _____ pounds on a tandem axle that is four feet apart.
explanation: Federal law states that single axles are limited to 20,000 pounds, and axles spaced more than 40 inches and not more than 96 inches apart (tandem axles) are limited to 34,000 pounds.

text: At drawbridges with no signal you must stop at least:
explanation: Stop at drawbridges that do not have a signal light or traffic control attendant. Stop at least 50 feet before the draw of the bridge. Look to make sure the draw is completely closed before crossing.

text: A bus may carry baggage or freight only if it is secured so that:
explanation: Secure baggage and freight in ways that avoid damage and: allow the driver to move freely and easily, allow riders to exit by any window or door in an emergency, and protect riders from injury if carry-ons fall or shift.

text: In general, standees on a bus are not permitted:
explanation: Buses designed to allow standing must have a two-inch line on the floor or some other means of showing riders where they cannot stand. This is called the standee line. All standing riders must stay behind it.

text: Rules require shippers to mark containers of hazardous materials with:
explanation: The rules require shippers to mark containers of hazardous material with the material's name, identification number, and hazard label.

text: The following are some situations that require special attention from all drivers. Which one is an even bigger problem for the driver of a vehicle with a low ground clearance?
explanation: With the low ground clearance humps or dips in the road would present more of a hazard.

text: A straight vehicle weighing 20,000 pounds which hauls 20 passengers falls into which CDL class:
explanation: A vehicle with no articulation (Class A has articulation), weighing less than 26,001 pounds (Class B is 26,001 or more) would fall under Class C.

text: Prohibited practices on the bus include:
explanation: Avoid fueling your bus with riders on board unless absolutely necessary, never refuel in a closed building with riders on board, don't talk with riders or engage in any other distracting activity while driving, and do not tow or push a disabled bus with riders aboard the vehicle unless getting off would be unsafe.

text: You must put a loaded bus into motion from a stopped position on a steep upgrade. Which of these is the best technique to follow when you have a bus with a manual transmission?
explanation: Releasing the clutch slowly until you feel the vehicle start to pull against the parking brake, and then releasing the parking brake should allow the vehicle to move without rolling.

text: You may sometimes haul small arms ammunition, emergency shipments of drugs, or hospital supplies on a bus. The total weight of all such hazardous materials must not be greater than _____ pounds.
explanation: A bus cannot carry more than 500 pounds total of allowed hazardous materials, and no more than 100 pounds of any one class.

text: Which of these is a good rule for backing a straight bus?
explanation: Because you cannot see everything behind your vehicle, backing is always dangerous. Avoid backing whenever you can. When you have to back, here are a few simple safety rules: start in the proper position, look at your path, use mirrors on both sides, back slowly, back and turn toward the driver's side whenever possible, and use a helper whenever possible.

text: How are buses to handle most railroad grade crossings?
explanation: Stop your bus between 15 and 50 feet before railroad crossings. Listen and look in both directions for trains.

text: Which of the following statements about hazardous materials are FALSE?
explanation: Buses may carry small-arms ammunition labeled ORM-D, emergency hospital supplies, and drugs that are properly labeled. You can carry small amounts of some other hazardous materials if the shipper cannot send them any other way. Most hazardous material may not be carried on a bus.

text: If a passenger is drunk or disruptive, you may
explanation: Occasionally, you may have a drunk or disruptive rider. You must ensure this rider's safety as well as that of others. Don't discharge such riders where it would be unsafe for them. It may be safer at the next scheduled stop or a well-lighted area where there are other people.

text: Always check the interior of the bus before driving to ensure rider safety. What are you looking for?
explanation: Aisles and stairwells should always be clear. The following parts of your bus must be in safe working condition: The "Emergency Exit" sign on an emergency door must be clearly visible, the seats must be safe for riders and securely fastened to the bus, among others.

text: To prevent theft and vandalism on a charter bus,
explanation: Charter bus drivers should not allow riders on the bus until departure time. This will help prevent theft or vandalism of the bus.

text: When pulling out of a bus stop,
explanation: School and mass transit buses sometimes scrape off mirrors or hit passing vehicles when pulling out from a bus stop. Remember the clearance your bus needs and watch for poles and tree limbs at stops.

text: Which of the following answers lists the three types of emergency equipment you must have on your bus?
explanation: You must have a fire extinguisher 10 BC or better, spare electrical fuses, and emergency reflective triangles.

text: Do to the extreme size of a bus, the driver must watch for:
explanation: Remember the clearance your bus needs and watch for poles and tree limbs at stops.

text: When do you sign post inspection reports?
If a rider wants to bring a car battery or a can of gasoline aboard your bus, you should:
explanation: Riders sometimes board a bus with an unlabeled hazardous material. Do not allow riders to carry on common hazards such as car batteries or gasoline

text: When arriving at the destination announce:
explanation: When arriving at the destination or intermediate stops announce: the location, reason for stopping, next departure time, and bus number.

text: In a pressurized cooling system, coolant level should be checked:
explanation: Never remove the radiator cap or any part of the pressurized system until the system has cooled. Steam and boiling water can spray under pressure and cause severe burns. If you can touch the radiator cap with your bare hand, it is probably cool enough to open.

text: When hauling hazardous material on a bus, the total weight for any one type of substance cannot exceed?
explanation: More than 500 pounds total of allowed hazardous materials, and no more than 100 pounds of any one class.

text: If your bus has an emergency exit door, it must:
explanation: As you check the outside of the bus, close any open emergency exits. Also, close any open access panels (for baggage, restroom service, engine, etc.) before driving.

text: When must a driver's inspection report be completed?
explanation: Drivers inspections repots must be completed at the end of every working day.

text: Federal regulations do not allow a driver to drive if he or she is:
explanation: If it interferes with the ability to drive safe, you will not be allowed to drive.

text: Which of the following types of cargo can never be carried on a bus with riders?
explanation: Buses must never carry: division 2.3 poison gas, liquid Class 6 poison, tear gas, irritating material, more than 100 pounds of solid Class 6 poisons, explosives in the space occupied by people except small arms ammunition and labeled radioactive materials in the space occupied by people.

text: You do not have to stop but slow down at:
explanation: You do not have to stop but must slow down and carefully check for other vehicles: at streetcar crossings, where a policeman or flagman is directing traffic, if a traffic signal is green, at crossings marked as "exempt" or "abandoned."

text: An oncoming vehicle keeps its high beams on. You should deal with this hazard by:
explanation: Do not look directly at lights of oncoming vehicles. Look slightly to the right at a right lane or edge marking, if available.

text: In general, the best braking technique to use in maintaining a safe speed when driving on a downgrade is:
explanation: Snub braking involves applying the brakes firmly enough to feel a definite slowing down which reduces your speed by 5 mph in about 3 seconds and then releasing the brakes climbing back up to your safe speed, and then repeat.

text: Which of these statements about gear ranges in buses with automatic transmissions is true?
explanation: Lower ranges result in higher RPM's at a slower speed helping you maintain speed on a downgrade.

text: The proper time to cancel your turn signal for a lane change or turn is:
explanation: Put your turn signal on before changing lanes. Change lanes slowly and smoothly. Once the lane change is complete cancel your signal.

text: If your bus becomes disabled on a freeway or expressway and you must stop on the left side of the road, it is best to stop so that the bus is:
explanation: You should stop at an angle with your door facing the downstream (cars that have already passed) side of traffic.

text: Which of the following must be closed while the bus is in motion?
explanation: As you check the outside of the bus, close any open emergency exits. Also, close any open access panels (for baggage, restroom service, engine, etc.) before driving

text: If your bus leans toward the outside on a banked curve, you are:
explanation: If your bus leans toward the outside on a banked curve, you are driving too fast.

text: How many folding aisle seats are allowed in a bus?
explanation: Folding aisle seats are not allowed on passenger busses.

text: While crossing railroad tracks, you should _________ if your bus has a manual transmission.
explanation: If your bus has a manual transmission, never change gears while crossing the tracks.

text: During your pre-trip inspection, you should sign the previous driver's report only if no defects are listed on it.
explanation: Don't sign a previous driver's inspection report unless the defects listed on it have been certified as either repaired or not needing to be repaired.

text: Can a bus carry 100 pounds each of class 1, class 3, class 4, and class 5 hazardous materials?
explanation: Passenger buses can carry up to 100 pounds of each class of hazardous materials and up to 500 pounds total. However, there are some exceptions. Division 2.3 (poison gas) and liquid Class 6 (poison) cannot be carried in any amounts. Explosives and radioactive cannot be carried in the passenger area.

text: Which of these statements about speed management and braking is true?
explanation: When you double your speed from 20 to 40 mph the impact is 4 times greater. The braking distance is also 4 times longer.

text: You do not have to stop _________, but you must slow down and check for other vehicles.
explanation: You do not have to stop but must slow down and carefully check for other vehicles: at streetcar crossings, where a policeman or flagman is directing traffic, if a traffic signal is green, and at crossings marked as "exempt" or "abandoned."

text: It is best to wear your seat belt:
explanation: The driver's seat should have a seat belt. Always use it for safety.

text: Which of these is the best general rule for night driving?
explanation: Adding an extra second in your normal daytime following distance would be the safest option.

text: Buses may carry
explanation: Buses may carry small-arms ammunition labeled ORM-D, emergency hospital supplies, and drugs.

text: As the driver of a bus, how can you best avoid hindering other traffic on the highway?
explanation: Staying to the right side of the road will reduce the effect of the buss on traffic.

text: The posted speed on a curve is
explanation: In good weather, the posted speed is safe for cars, but it may be too high for many buses. With good traction, the bus may roll over; with poor traction, it might slide off the curve.

text: Which of the following statements about the roof hatches is true?
explanation: You may lock some emergency roof hatches in a partly open position for fresh air. Do not leave them open as a regular practice. Keep in mind the bus's higher clearance while driving with them open.

text: Charter drivers should:
explanation: When driving a charter, you should load passengers right before departure, and explain the rules once they have boarded.

text: A disruptive passenger:
explanation: At times it is necessary to discharge an unruly passenger, but it should not be done in an area that can be considered unsafe for them.

text: When it is carried on a bus, hazardous material must be:
explanation: The rules require shippers to mark containers of hazardous material with the material's name, identification number, and hazard label.

text: You must be alert for road hazards so that
explanation: You will have more time to act if you see hazards before they become emergencies. Being prepared reduces the danger.

text: Before leaving your stop, you should
explanation: Know the size of the gap your bus needs to accelerate and merge with traffic. Wait for the gap to open before leaving the stop. Never assume other drivers will brake to give you room when you signal or start to pull out.

text: When you inspect your bus, you must make sure that:
explanation: During your inspection you want to make sure that handholds and railing are secure, rider signaling devices are operational, and that the emergency exits are secure.

text: Charter bus drivers should
explanation: Remind riders to take carry-ons with them if they get off the bus.

text: Which of the following statements about the interior of a bus is FALSE?
explanation: The "Emergency Exit" sign on an emergency door must be clearly visible. If there is a red emergency door light, it must work. Turn it on at night or any other time you use your outside lights

text: When the roads are slippery you should?
explanation: With slippery roads you should keep more space in front of your vehicle while driving compared to ideal conditions.

text: Which seats must be equipped with a seat belt?
explanation: The driver's seat in every bus must be equipped with a seat belt in good working order. Always wear your seat belt whenever your bus is in motion.

text: You have passed all the requirements for a class B bus driver's license.  Which of these vehicles are you not license to drive?
When doing the pre-trip, you should tell the examiner each item you are checking and 2-3 things you check each item for.
explanation: Try not to overdo the item or what you are checking it for. You only get 30 minutes to check everything on the vehicle.

text: Should you do an overview of your vehicle as you approach it (including looking for leaks, leaning, or excessive damage)?
explanation: Overviews give you a chance to catch major problems early.

text: When checking the lights at the front of the vehicle what should you check for?
explanation: All lights will need checked at the front, back and sides of the vehicle. They will need checked for covers, cleanliness, secureness, and that they work.

text: When checking the windshield, what should you be looking for?
explanation: Mentioning the windshield and checking it for cracks, chips, and cleanliness is not enough. You will have to check the seals and for leaks to get it marked as checked.

text: When opening the hood, you should check?
explanation: The hood will always need checked, mainly for damage, but don't forget the hinges, catches, and springs or you might not get credit.

text: Checking the mirrors on the outside of the vehicle you should check for?
explanation: Don't forget you can have mirrors on the hood, and on the doors of the cab. While outside check for mounts, cracks, and cleanliness. You should also check them again when inside the vehicle to make sure they are properly adjusted.

text: In the engine compartment there are four fluids you must check, they are?
explanation: It is best to check all your fluids during a pre-trip inspection, but the fluids you have to say are oil, coolant, power steering, and checking for moisture and leaks.

text: In the engine compartment there are four major belt or gear driven components. What are they?
explanation: It is best to check all your belt and gear driven components, but the power steering, alternator, air compressor, and water pump are all that appear on the CDL pre-trip.

text: What should you check each of the fluids in the engine compartment for?
explanation: All fluids should be at the proper level, clean, free of debris, with no leaks. You can also add proper color to this list. Some coolants are red, green and orange. Red or clear for most power steering. Oil can be difficult, it should be tan in color, but normally after use of the vehicle it gets black.

text: What are the main components of the steering controls found in the engine compartment?
explanation: The steering linkage consists of upper steering linkage, universal joints, power steering pump, pitman arm, drag link, tie rod ends, tie rod. They will all need checked separately.

text: What should you check the steering linkage for?
explanation: Along with the castle nuts, cotter pins, and excessive play you can check for illegal welds and cracks.

text: Which of the following does not get checked in the engine compartment?
explanation: Oil pressure is checked on the inside of the cab using the oil pressure gauge.

text: Which of the following is not an engine compartment belt?
explanation: The safety belt secures the driver in the cab.

text: The leaf springs, shocks, U-bolts, shackles, and air bags are part of what system?
explanation: These are all components of the suspension system and will need checked independently in your pre-trip exam.

text: Hoses in the engine compartment should be checked for?
explanation: Make sure the lines are connected and secure, no frays, cracks, tears, and no leaks to get hoses marked off. The trick is you need to say horses for the air compressor, power steering, water pump independently to get credit.

text: The engine belts should be checked for?
explanation: Check each belt individually. There can be belts for a few different components under the hood. The excessive play of more than 3/4 of an inch.

text: Brake lines should be checked for?
explanation: As with all hoses, make sure the connections are tight and secure. That there are no frays, cracks, or damage to the lines with no leaks. With an airline leaks can be found by suspicious clean spots on the hose.

text: Air chambers are?
explanation: Also called brake chambers, they are found on most commercial vehicles, and filled with air to apply the brakes.

text: Air chambers should be checked for?
explanation: Also called brake chambers, they need to be mounted tight and secure, not excessively damaged, and not leaking.

text: The slack adjuster is part of the braking system, what is the maximum play allowed with the brakes off?
explanation: There should be no more than 1 inch of play in any slack adjuster on the vehicle, with the brakes off.

text: Brake pads should be checked for?
explanation: All answers are true. They should have at least a 1/4 inch of life left on the brake pad, since they can wear unevenly also check that they are not dangerously thin, and no cracks or breaks.

text: Brake drums?
explanation: Make sure they are not misshapen (out of round), no illegal welds, and no cracks.

text: Brake drums (or discs) must not have cracks longer than:
explanation: The area where the brake shoes touch the drum is considered the friction area, there should be no cracks 1/2 the width of that area.

text: Tire tread depth depends on the axel. The depth should be no less than ______ for the steer axel, and _____ for any other axel.
explanation: All tread depth needs to be said in thirty-seconds. The front axle or steer axel must have at least 4/32, and the rest of the axles can have no less than 2/32.

text: Retreaded tires can be on any axel.
explanation: The front or steer axel is not allowed to have retreaded tires.

text: Tires should be checked for?
explanation: Tires should not have any cuts, cracks, or bulges. You will need to make sure the tire pressure is up to manufacturer specifications, and that it has the proper tread depth.

text: You should check rims for dents, cracks, illegal welds, valve stem, and that they are tight and secure.
explanation: Rims should not have dents, cracks, illegal welds. This is a great time to mention your valve stem since it comes through the rim, and the rims need to be tight and secure.

text: All rims have a spacer.
explanation: The front axle does not have a spacer because it does not have dual tires on each side. Also bud rims are made so that there is no need for a spacer. Only on Dayton setups would you have a spacer.

text: Lug nuts (that hold the rim to the axle) should be checked for?
explanation: Lug nuts need to be tight and secure, no stripped or worn threads. The rust marks will start at the best of the lug nut and run to the outside of the rim  and indicate a loose lug nut.

text: The hub oil seal should be?
explanation: Some hub oil seals will have a plastic or glass area, so you are able to tell that the oil is at the proper level. If there is not an area to view the oil it will still need to be said. It will have several bolts, and they need to be tight and secure. Also, the seal needs to be in good condition and not leaking.

text: Differences between the steer axel and the rest of the axles are: tread depth, spacers or budding rims, space between the tires, torsion bars, and air bags.
explanation: Steer axels are as simple as an axel can get. They only have one tire on each side, so no spacers, space, or bud rims are required. The tread depth is different from any other axel, and it usually does not have a torsion bar or air bags.

text: You can leave the hood open when moving on with the pre-trip.
explanation: Open the hood to do the engine compartment,  and close it before walking away. You should never leave the engine compartment with the hood open.

text: After the engine compartment it is best to move to the driver's side of vehicle, and then head into the cab.
explanation: This is true. It is best to get to the inside cab early. If you do not make it to the inside can you can automatically fail for not doing the CDL air brakes test.

text: At the side of the cab you should not check.
explanation: All these items will all appear on your CDL pre-trip. The correct answer is none of these.

text: The fuel and air tanks are checked for the same items except?
explanation: Both the fuel and air tanks should be checked for proper mount, no leaks, and good hoses. Only the fuel tank will have a cap so you may fill the tanks.

text: Steps leading into the vehicle should be?
explanation: Make sure the steps are properly mounted, no damage, and bolts are present and secure.

text: Handrails should be checked for?
explanation: Handrails will need to be tight and secure, properly mounted, all bolts in place, and no excessive damage.

text: Is it best to check the seat and the seatbelts before sitting down?
explanation: We suggest checking the seat and belt for securement and damage before sitting down. You will be in a hurry, and it is best to mention them when you see them.

text: The first thing you should check or do when you sit in the driver's seat is?
explanation: As soon as you sit in the driver's seat please buckle your seatbelt. If you move the vehicle at all without your seatbelt fastened it is an automatic fail.

text: Check your mirrors from the inside of the cab for?
explanation: If you checked them for mounts, cracks, and clean while outside of the vehicle there is no need to state those again. Adjustment can only be checked from the driver's seat, but if you forgot to mention the mirrors on the outside of the vehicle you can check them now for everything.

text: Items inside the cab you must make function in order to get credit for them are?
explanation: If you just mention the wipers and do not check to make sure they work you will not get credit for them. This is true with all the answers here.

text: The steering wheel should be checked for?
explanation: Steering wheels should not have more than 2" of play or 10 degrees in a 20" wheel.

text: Before you start the vehicle you should check?
explanation: Making sure the vehicle is in neutral, parking brake set, and seat belt on is steps to a safe start of the vehicle. You should also apply the clutch if equipped before starting.

text: These are all gauges that will need checked except?
explanation: We do not check the water pressure gauge, instead you should check the water temperature gauge.

text: The air pressure in a commercial vehicle should rise to governor cutout of?
explanation: Air pressure should rise to between 120-140 PSI.

text: Which gauge should be between 12-14, and in the green?
explanation: The voltmeter should be between 12-14, and in the green to be working properly.

text: What should the water temperature gauge do when you start the vehicle?
explanation: The temperature gauge will more than likely not move until the vehicle starts to warm up.

text: You don't have to make both the air and city horns work to get credit for them.
explanation: You need to make both the air and city horns work to get credit for checking them.

text: What interior lights do you need to check?
explanation: You will need to check and make each light indicator work in order to get credit. While you are checking lights, it might not be a bad idea to check the dome light as well.

text: What items are part of your safety equipment?
explanation: All these items are part of your safety equipment.

text: To test the parking brake you should have the trailer air supply in, trailer hand break released, and the parking brake _____?
explanation: The parking brake will need to be out or engaged to test the parking brake.

text: Testing the parking brake requires you to?
explanation: The vehicle will need to be stopped with parking brake applied, and the trailer air supply pushed in. Put the vehicle in gear and gently pull against the parking brake with no movement.

text: To test the service brake test you will need to have the trailer air supply valve in, parking brake in, and the hand brake  released. How do you test the service brake?
explanation: Moving slowly, less than 5 MPH, and apply the service brake with steady pressure. The vehicle should come to a smooth and even stop with no pull to one side or the other.

text: The next step is the CDL air brake test. This portion is done with the engine off, but should you have air pressure built up to governor cut of 120-140?
explanation: Yes, the pressure should be built up to governor cutoff to ensure you have enough air pressure to complete the CDL air brake test.

text: During the CDL air brakes tests the first step is to check for air leakage?
explanation: It is best to check the air leakage rate first or you will have to rebuild pressure after draining tanks to make sure the trailer air supply and parking brake set on their own.

text: To test air leakage the air valves should be?
explanation: To properly test the air leakage you will need trailer air supply in to supply air to the trailer, parking brake in filling the red line with air, steady pressure applied to the brake pedal filling the blue line with air, and the trolley released so no extra air is supplied to the blue line.

text: Step 1 of the CDL air brake tests makes sure the leak rate of the red and blue line is not unsafe. With the parking brake released and the brake pedal applied the leak rate should be no more than?
explanation: The air leak rate should be no more than 2 PSI for the red line, 1 PSI for the blue line. In a non- combination vehicle that is 3. For a combination vehicle you will need to add 1 PSI for the trailer bringing the total to 4.

text: Next you should fan the brakes down to make sure all air brake safety devices are working, but what should you do before starting to fan the brakes?
explanation: You will need to have the key on to accessory or to the position right before you start the vehicle. This needs to be done so that the alarm and light for the low air warning can come on alerting of low air pressure. If the light and alarm does not come on when you are taking your test you may automatically fail.

text: While fanning down the alarm and light indicating low air pressure should come on at?
explanation: The low air pressure warning light and alarm should come on when the air pressure is around 60 PSI.

text: Fanning the brakes down further after the alarm and buzzer come on should result in the parking brake popping out for a single vehicle, and the parking brake and the trailer air supply popping out for a combination vehicle at?
explanation: The parking brake for vehicles with no trailer, and the parking brake and trailer air supply valve for combination vehicles should set or pop out at 40 PSI.

text: Before you leave the inside can you should not check the exterior lights.
explanation: It is best to check the lights before getting out, and it will allow the air pressure to build back up if you safely restart the engine.

text: Exterior lights that need checked are on which side of the vehicle?
explanation: Lights will need to be checked on the front, both sides, rear of tractor on combination vehicles, and the rear of the vehicle.

text: What lights will you need to check on the exterior?
explanation: You need to check the hazard, left turn, right turn, headlights high and low beam, and the clearance lights. Please remember to check each at the front of the vehicle except brake lights, all but headlights and brake lights on both sides, and all except headlights at the back of the vehicle. If this is a combination vehicle check the rear of the tractor for all except headlights.

text: What should you remember to check while exiting the vehicle?
explanation: Best to make sure you check the handrails, steps, fuel tank, and air tanks at this time.

text: Hand rails and steps should be checked for?
explanation: The handrails and step need to be secure, not damaged, and able to support the weight you are applying to them when entering the vehicle.

text: When you check the Fuel tank you can also say you would check the same items on the air tanks. What should these two items be checked for?
explanation: Please make sure that you point at or touch both tanks to insure you receive credit for them, and they both need checked for security, bands and grommets, the lines between tanks, and leaks.
