text: You should mention you would check all light covers and reflectors for damage on the side of the vehicle, both the tractor and the trailer, again to cover the side since you have only checked the front at this time?
explanation: This is true, on each side of the vehicle you need to check the light lenses and reflectors, and you should get credit for both the tractor and the trailer if you tell the examiner you would check both at this time. The lenses and reflectors need to be mounted, clean, and not damaged or missing.

text: When checking the air and electrical lines between the tractor and the trailer what should you be checking for?
explanation: Make sure to check the connection at the tractor, the lines for breaks cracks frays, the connection to the trailer, and that none of these are leaking. Group them together at the tractor, between the vehicle, and at the trailer to make this part go quickly.

text: What are a few items you want to check while between the tractor and trailer?
explanation: Frame needs checked for cracks, bends, illegal welds, that it is secure, and mounting bolts. Catwalk needs to be in good condition with all mounting bolts in place. Exhaust needs to be secure; all pipes and connections need to be secure and free of leaks. The driveshaft needs to be properly mounted, tight sand secure, u-joints in good condition and move freely, and there is no excessive play.

text: The rest of the axels on the vehicle are nearly the same as the front steer axels, with a few differences. What are the differences?
explanation: There are FOUR differences between the steer axel, and the tractor and trailer axels. They are the Torsion bar, the space between the axels so bud rims or spacers, tread depth changes from four thirty seconds to two thirty seconds, and retreads are allowed on every axel but the steer axel, and the air bags portion of the suspension.

text: The apron is the metal plate welded on the bottom of the trailer that gives the 5th wheel a smooth surface for vehicle connection. What should you check the apron for?
explanation: The apron itself should not be bent, cracked, or damaged. It will be welded to the bottom of the trailer, but it should not have welds on the surface that the 5th wheel contacts, and it should be securely mounted.

text: The kingpin is a cylindric piece of metal protruding down from the apron, it has a base which is near the apron, shank or the narrow section of the kingpin where the locking jaws connect, and the head or the part furthest from the apron. What should you check the kingpin for?
explanation: The kingpin is part of what holds the tractor and trailer together, it needs to be straight with no damage. You should not have any cracks in the metal, and there should not be any welds on the visible portion of the kingpin.

text: The 5th wheel is mounted on the back of the tractor, it lifts the trailer slightly when it contacts the apron when you are coupling the vehicles, and it houses the locking jaws which wrap around the shank of the kingpin to securely attach the trailer. True or False: The 5th wheel should be greased, no illegal welds, not damaged, and tilt with enough pressure.
explanation: This is true. The 5th wheel tilts so that the coupling can withstand unlevel surfaces, it cannot have welds or cracks, and needs to be secure.

text: You should check to make sure that the locking jaws are completely wrapped around the shank of the kingpin during your pre-trip inspection?
explanation: This is true. The kingpin needs to be completely extended and around the shank of the kingpin.

text: When coupled there should be a slight gap between the 5th wheel and the apron, meaning you should be able to see light between the two.
explanation: To have a properly connected vehicle there can be no gap between the 5th wheel and the apron. If there is a gap you should disconnect the tractor, lower the front of trailer, and recouple the tractor. A gap could mean the locking jaws went around the head of the kingpin instead of the shank. This is dangerous because a large bump may cause the trailer to lift, and the kingpin to slide out of the locking jaws.

text: The 5th wheel has a release arm built into the side of it, it automatically locks the jaws around the kingpin when you connect the vehicles, but it can be pulled out to release the locking jaws for uncoupling. What is the correct position of the release arm when the vehicle is coupled?
explanation: The release arm needs to be pushed completely in with the locking mechanism secured. You want to make sure this is secure because if it is not the trailer might break free of the locking jaws.

text: Most 5th wheels are adjustable for weight distribution and turn clearance, these are called sliding 5th wheels, they have a release are that needs to be completely in and locked, and a locking mechanism. What locks the 5th wheel in place?
explanation: Locking pins extend through holes in the platform to lock the sliding 5th wheel in place.

text: The 5th wheel platform needs to be checked for?
explanation: The platform needs to be free of illegal welds, be tight and secure, properly mounted, not damaged, and all bolts need to be in place and secure.

text: At the rear of the tractor there are still a few things to check, what are they?
explanation: The only things you needed to check at the rear of the tractor are the light lens covers, reflectors, and the mud flaps.

text: What do the red flashing lights on an eight-light system bus signify to other drivers?
explanation: Red flashing lights on a school bus indicate that the bus is stopped to pick up or discharge students.

text: While looking through the interior flat mirror the driver should have a clear view of?
explanation: The large flat mirror hanging above the driver's head is there for them to see all of the students onboard at one time.

text: How far prior to a bus pick up location must the amber warning lights be turned on?
explanation: If the school bus is so equipped, activate alternating flashing amber warning lights at least 200 feet or approximately 5-10 seconds before the school bus stop or in accordance with state law.

text: If an unruly student is threatening the safety of others how should you handle the situation?
explanation: Tips on handling serious problems: stop the bus parking in a safe location off the road perhaps a parking lot or a driveway, secure the bus, take the ignition key with you if you leave your seat, stand up and speak respectfully to the offender or offenders. Speak in a courteous manner with a firm voice. Remind the offender of the expected behavior. Do not show anger but do show that you mean business.

text: The school buses stop arm is to only be operated with the:
explanation: Activate alternating red lights when traffic is a safe distance from the school bus and ensure stop arm is extended.

text: If a bus is being operated on a divided highway or a highway with four lanes or more which of the following describes the procedures for pick up and discharge of students?
explanation: Students could only be picked up from the resident side of the road, meaning the right side, they will not allow students to cross a major highway to get to the bus.

text: When leaving a freeway, you should begin to slow down?
explanation: You should start to slow down at least 100 feet in advance.

text: When is it required to stop a bus, look, listen, open the driver's side window and service door?
explanation: At the Crossing: Stop no closer than 15 feet and no farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail, place the transmission in Park. turn off all radios and noisy equipment, silence the passengers, and open the service door and driver's window. Look and listen for an approaching train.

text: In flat mirrors you should see?
explanation: Ensure that the flat mirrors are properly adjusted so you can see: 200 feet or 4 bus lengths behind the bus, along the sides of the bus, and the rear tires touching the ground.

text: During your pre-trip vehicle inspection test, make sure that
explanation: Make sure that all emergency exits are not damaged, operate smoothly, and close securely from the inside. Check that any emergency exit warning devices are working.

text: Which of the following statements is true about students who must cross in front of the bus after exiting it?
explanation: Wait for your signal before crossing the roadway. Upon your signal, the students should: Cross far enough in front of the school bus to be in your view.

text: A passive railroad crossing
explanation: This type of crossing does not have any type of traffic control device. You must stop at these crossings and follow proper procedures. However, the decision to proceed rests entirely in your hands.

text: As part of your vehicle inspection test, you will have to check _________ to be sure that it is undamaged, operating smoothly, and able to be closed securely from the inside.
explanation: As part of your vehicle inspection test, you will have to check one emergency exit to be sure that it is undamaged, operating smoothly, and able to be closed securely from the inside. You will also have to point out the other emergency exits and describe how they operate.

text: Properly adjusted, the outside left and right side convex mirrors will let you see
explanation: You should position these mirrors to see: the entire side of the bus up to the mirror mounts, the front of the rear tires touching the ground, and at least one traffic lane on either side of the bus.

text: You can change the location of a school bus stop
explanation: You should never change the location of a bus stop without written approval from the appropriate school district official.

text: When you inspect the battery and battery box during your pre-trip vehicle inspection test, make sure that
explanation: Wherever located, see that battery(s) are secure, connections are tight, and cell caps are present. Battery connections should not show signs of excessive corrosion.

text: The left and right danger zones extend up to _______ from the left and right sides of the school bus.
explanation: The danger zones may extend as much as 30 feet from the front bumper with the first 10 feet being the most dangerous, 10 feet from the left and right sides of the bus and 10 feet behind the rear bumper of the school bus.

text: When driving a school bus in high winds, you should
explanation: Strong winds affect the handling of the school bus! The side of a school bus acts like a sail on a sailboat. Strong winds can push the school bus sideways. They can even move the school bus off the road or, in extreme conditions, tip it over.

text: The convex, crossover, and flat mirrors should be viewed
explanation: All mirrors should be viewed in a logical sequence to ensure that a child or object is not in any of the danger zones.

text: What is the "golden rule" for school buses when approaching and crossing railroad tracks?
explanation: At the Crossing: stop no closer than 15 feet and no farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail, place the transmission in Park, turn off all radios and noisy equipment, silence the passengers, open the service door and driver's window, and look and listen for an approaching train.

text: Which of the following people can remove or suspend a student from riding the bus?
explanation: Follow your school's procedures for discipline or refusal of rights to ride the bus. If you feel that the offense is serious enough that you cannot safely drive the bus, call for a school administrator or the police to come and remove the student.

text: What would be considered proper procedure at a highway railroad crossing?
explanation: When approaching turn on the hazard lights, and do not shift when crossing the tracks.

text: When getting onto a highway you should?
explanation: When entering a highway, you should make sure there are no sudden changes by the car in front of you, while checking the mirrors for the proper gap to merge.

text: When is the use of alcohol, tobacco, and non-prescription drugs allowed on the school bus?
explanation: The use of alcohol, tobacco, and non-prescription drugs should not be allowed on the school bus.

text: What do Anti-Lock Brakes do?
explanation: ABS is a computerized system that keeps your wheels from locking up during hard brake applications.

text: While the bus is running when is the driver allowed to exit the bus?
explanation: If the vehicle is running the driver is not allowed to leave the vehicle.

text: Which of the following statements about evacuation procedures is true?
explanation: If time allows, notify dispatch office of evacuation location, conditions, and type of assistance needed.

text: When unloading students at a school, you should
explanation: Have the students remain seated until told to exit.

text: The blind spot behind the bus may extend up to ________, depending on the length and width of the bus.
explanation: The blind spot behind the bus extends 5o to 150 feet and could extend up to 400 feet depending on the length and width of the bus.

text: When can you put a misbehaving student off the bus?
explanation: Never put a student off the bus except at school or at his or her designated school bus stop. If you feel that the offense is serious enough that you cannot safely drive the bus, call for a school administrator or the police to come and remove the student.

text: Before leaving, make sure the students are in plain view
explanation: Tell students to exit the bus and walk at least 10 feet away from the side of the bus to a position where the driver can plainly see all students.

text: You have stopped your bus to load students. Before you signal them to approach the bus, you should
explanation: Monitor all mirrors continuously.

text: Why is it so critical for you to understand the loading and unloading procedures?
explanation: More students are killed while getting on or off a school bus each year than are killed as passengers inside of a school bus. As a result, knowing what to do before, during, and after loading or unloading students is critical.

text: In an emergency, you should consider NOT evacuating students from your school bus
explanation: A decision to evacuate should include consideration of the following conditions: fire or danger of fire; smell of raw or leaking fuel; the chance the bus could be hit by other vehicles; downed power lines;  exposing students to speeding traffic, severe weather, or a dangerous environment; when moving a student complicate injury; or a hazardous spill.

text: To load students after you have stopped the school bus,
explanation: Count the number of students at the bus stop and be sure all board the bus. If possible, know names of students at each stop. If there is a student missing, ask the other students where the student is.

text: Which school bus accidents should be reported?
explanation: All school bus accidents must be reported whether or not they involve death, injury, or property damage.

text: Who is responsible for handling discipline problems on your school bus?
explanation: Tips on handling serious problems: follow your school's procedures for discipline or refusal of rights to ride the bus, stop and park in a safe location off the road, secure the bus taking the ignition key with you if you leave your seat.

text: An active railroad crossing is
explanation: This type of crossing has a traffic control device installed at the crossing to regulate traffic at the crossing. These active devices include flashing red lights, with or without bells and flashing red lights with bells and gates.

text: The tail swing of a school bus can be as much as
explanation: A school bus can have up to a three-foot tail swing. You need to check your mirrors before and during any turning movements to monitor the tail swing.

text: In addition to spare electrical fuses (if equipped), three red reflective triangles, and a working fire extinguisher, emergency equipment for a school bus includes
explanation: In addition to checking for spare electrical fuses, three red reflective triangles, and a properly charged and rated fire extinguisher, school bus drivers must also inspect the following emergency equipment: Emergency Kit Body, and Fluid Cleanup Kit

text: How could one distinguish a blind person among other pedestrians?
explanation: A white cane is typically used by the blind to help them find their way around without tripping.

text: When, according to the pupil transportation operation and safety rules, are the four-way hazard lights supposed to be used?
explanation: Four-way flashers or hazard lights are to be used at railroad crossings, when parked along the road from a breakdown, and whenever you are backing up or turning around.

text: When is the only time the red warning lights can be used?
explanation: The red flashing lights are for picking up and discharging as student only.

text: Front tire tread depth should be at least?
explanation: Front axle or steer axel tires must have a tread depth of at least 4/32 of an inch.

text: What color are all school buses painted?
explanation: The National Highway Transportation Safety Administration issued guidelines to the states on pupil transportation safety. One of these guidelines is that all school buses should be painted National School Bus Glossy Yellow.

text: When several students are exiting the bus, the driver should count the students, if one is missing?
explanation: Count the number of students while unloading to confirm the location of all students before pulling away from the stop. If you cannot account for a student outside the bus, secure the bus, and check around and underneath the bus.

text: Before you proceed through a railroad crossing, make sure that your bus will have enough containment to completely clear the railroad tracks. As a general rule, adequate containment is
explanation: As a general rule, add 15 feet to the length of the school bus to determine an acceptable amount of containment or storage area.

text: What is the most important reason for performing a school bus inspection?
explanation: The purpose of school bus inspections is to ensure safety. A vehicle defect found during an inspection could save you problems later. Federal and state laws require drivers to inspect their vehicles.

text: During your post-trip inspection of your school bus, you should check
explanation: During your post-trip inspection, you should check all the mechanical and operational systems of the school bus for any problems. Immediately report any problems to your supervisor or school authorities.

text: In most states, school buses are equipped with ________ warning light system to control traffic at school bus stops.
explanation: Federal regulations require school buses to be equipped with a warning light system. Each bus must have two flashing red lights in front and two in the rear, and optionally two flashing amber lights in front and two in the rear.

text: The front danger zone extends up to ________ from the front bumper of the school bus.
explanation: The danger zones may extend as much as 30 feet from the front bumper with the first 10 feet being the most dangerous, 10 feet from the left and right sides of the bus and 10 feet behind the rear bumper of the school bus.

text: When approaching a school bus stop, you should
explanation: When approaching the stop, you should: approach cautiously at a slow rate of speed; look for pedestrians, traffic, or other objects before, during, and after coming to a stop; continuously check all mirrors; activate alternating flashing amber warning lights at least 200 feet or approximately 5-10 seconds before the school bus stop or in accordance with state law.

text: Is the bus driver allowed to pick up or discharge students at other than their assigned locations?
explanation: If the school district allows this, and there is a written note from the parent, then yes, the driver can discharge at another location.

text: Who is the person responsible for the daily pre-trip bus inspections?
explanation: The bus driver is responsible for preforming the pre-trip inspection daily.

text: Students may never walk within how many feet of the bus at any time?
explanation: The danger zone is the area on all sides of the bus where children are in the most danger of being hit, either by another vehicle or their own bus. The danger zones may extend as much as 30 feet from the front bumper with the first 10 feet being the most dangerous, 10 feet from the left and right sides of the bus and 10 feet behind the rear bumper of the school bus.

text: After you decide on the best type of evacuation, what must you do next?
explanation: Secure the bus by: Placing transmission in Park, setting parking brakes, shut off the engine, remove ignition key, and activate hazard-warning lights.

text: Most states require school bus drivers to take a physical examination
explanation: Most states require school bus drivers to have an annual physical (including a vision test) to confirm that they are still fit to perform the duties of a school bus driver.

text: Which mirrors let you view the front danger zone?
explanation: Crossover Mirrors are mounted on both left and right front corners of the bus. They are used to see the front bumper "danger zone" area directly in front of the bus that is not visible by direct vision.

text: During your vehicle inspection test, you must check that the entry door is undamaged. Also check that it
explanation: Check that the entry door is not damaged, operates smoothly, and closes securely from the inside.

text: During your vehicle inspection test, if your school bus is equipped with a handicap lift, you should check
explanation: If equipped with a handicap lift, look for leaking, damaged, or missing parts and explain how lift should be checked for correct operation. Lift must be fully retracted and latched securely.

text: When under the age of 21 years old a driver will be considered intoxicated at?
explanation: There is a zero-tolerance policy for having alcohol in your system as the driver of a school bus.

text: The right front mirror should be adjusted to see?
explanation: Ensure that the mirrors are properly adjusted so you can see: The entire area in front of the bus from the front bumper at ground level to a point where direct vision is possible, direct vision and mirror view vision should overlap, the right and left front tires touching the ground, and the area from the front of the bus to the service door.

text: You MUST evacuate everyone from your school bus if
explanation: The driver must evacuate the bus when: the bus is on fire or there is a threat of a fire, the bus is stalled on or adjacent to a railroad highway crossing, the position of the bus may change and increase the danger, there is an imminent danger of collision, and there is a need to quickly evacuate because of a hazardous materials spill.

text: Immediately after stopping, you should first
explanation: Activate alternating red lights when traffic is a safe distance from the school bus and ensure stop arm is extended.

text: If a railroad crossing signal is malfunctioning, you should
explanation: If there is no police officer, and you believe the signal is malfunctioning, call your dispatcher to report the situation and ask for instructions on how to proceed.

text: At a railroad crossing, you must stop at the stop line. If there is no stop line, you must stop
explanation: Stop no closer than 15 feet and no farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail, where you have the best view of the tracks.

text: You're about to unload students at a bus stop. Tell them that once they've exited the bus, they should
explanation: Tell students to exit the bus and walk at least 10 feet away from the side of the bus to a position where the driver can plainly see all students.

text: When loading or unloading students, you should check your mirrors
explanation: Monitor all mirrors continuously.

text: When approaching a railroad crossing you should turn on the hazards?
explanation: Activate hazard lights approximately 200 feet before the crossing. Make sure your intentions are known.

text: When is the most dangerous time during a bus ride?
explanation: When loading or unloading students they can be three times more likely to be injured.

text: Which of the following statements about the special dangers of loading and unloading is true?
explanation: Students should be told to leave any dropped object and move to a point of safety out of the danger zones and attempt to get the driver's attention to retrieve the object.

text: When approaching a bus stop on a multilane roadway, you should maintain your position _________ of the rightmost lane.
explanation: Move as far as possible to the right on the traveled portion of the roadway.

text: While you're loading students, if you cannot account for a student, what should you do?
explanation: If you cannot account for a student outside, secure the bus, take the key, and check around and underneath the bus.

text: How can a driver determine if a bus is equipped with Anti-Lock Brakes?
explanation: Your school bus will have a yellow ABS malfunction lamp on the instrument panel if it is equipped with ABS.

text: If the bus is on fire, you must
explanation: Lead students upwind of the bus if fire is present.

text: Is the bus driver allowed to crack or open the service door prior to stopping to pick up a pupil?
explanation: The door needs to remain shut until the bus has come to a complete stop to pick up or drop off a student.

text: You should activate your alternating flashing amber warning lights ________, depending on the laws and regulations of your state.
explanation: If the school bus is so equipped, activate alternating flashing amber warning lights at least 200 feet or approximately 5-10 seconds before the school bus stop or in accordance with state law.

text: As part of your post-trip inspection of your bus, check for each of the following EXCEPT
explanation: Make sure that all emergency exits are not damaged, operate smoothly, and close securely from the inside.

text: In an emergency, you MUST evacuate everyone from your school bus
explanation: The driver must evacuate the bus when: the bus is on fire or there is a threat of a fire, the bus is stalled on or adjacent to a railroad highway crossing, the position of the bus may change and increase the danger, there is an imminent danger of collision, or there is a need to quickly evacuate because of a hazardous materials spill.

text: When turning around at a railroad crossing a driver should?
explanation: Hazard lights would be required for both the turnaround, and the railroad crossing.

text: Hauling liquids in tankers requires special care for two reasons. One reason is the _____ center of gravity that tankers have.
explanation: Hauling liquids in tanks requires special skills because of the high center of gravity and liquid movement.

text: You are driving a tank truck and the front wheels begin to skid. Which of these is most likely to occur?
explanation: In a front-wheel skid, the front end tends to go in a straight line regardless of how much you turn the steering wheel.

text: How far back does a helper stand when pumping or unloading liquids from a tanker?
explanation: The person watching the loading or unloading must: be alert, have a clear view of the cargo tank, be within 25 feet of the tank, know of the hazards of the materials involved, know the procedures to follow in an emergency, and be authorized to move the cargo tank and able to do so.

text: Outage means:
explanation: Liquids expand as they warm and you must leave room for the expanding liquid. This is called "outage."

text: Side to side surge can cause:
explanation: Side-to-side surge can cause a roll over.

text: You need to be extremely cautious when driving smooth bore tankers. This is especially true when you are:
explanation: Un-baffled liquid tankers (sometimes called "smooth bore" tanks) have nothing inside to slow down the flow of the liquid. Therefore, forward-and back surge is very strong.

text: Liquid in a tank with baffles can have what kind of surge?
explanation: The baffles help to control the forward and backward liquid surge. Side-to-side surge can still occur. This can cause a roll over.

text: When your cargo tank has baffles, what handling effect do you expect?
explanation: The baffles help to control the forward and backward liquid surge. Side-to-side surge can still occur. This can cause a roll over.

text: Which of these statements about stopping distance and speed is true?
explanation: Remember that wet roads double the normal stopping distance.

text: Which of these statements about tankers and their center of gravity is true?
explanation: High center of gravity means that much of the load's weight is carried high up off the road. This makes the vehicle top-heavy and easy to roll over. Liquid tankers are especially easy to roll over. Tests have shown that tankers can turn over at the speed limits posted for curves. Take highway curves and on ramp/off ramp curves well below the posted speeds

text: You must have a tank vehicle (N) endorsement to drive a vehicle with
explanation: A tank endorsement is required if your vehicle needs a Class A or B CDL and you want to haul a liquid or liquid gas in a tank or tanks having an individual rated capacity of more than 119 gallons and an aggregate rated capacity of 1000 gallons or more that is either permanently or temporarily attached to the vehicle or the chassis.

text: When should you inspect a tank vehicle?
explanation: You should inspect a tank vehicle before loading it, driving it, or unloading it. This is to ensure that it is safe to carry out these activities and the vehicle can safely hold its liquid or gas contents.

text: To control surge, what is the best way to brake?
explanation: Keep a steady pressure on the brakes. Do not release too soon when coming to a stop. Brake far in advance of a stop and increase your following distance.

text: When must a tank be un-baffled?
explanation: Un-baffled liquid tankers sometimes called "smooth bore" tanks have nothing inside to slow down the flow of the liquid. Un-baffled tanks are usually those that transport food products like milk. Sanitation regulations forbid the use of baffles because of the difficulty in cleaning the inside of the tank.

text: Which of these is the most important thing to remember about emergency braking?
explanation: If the wheels are skidding, you cannot control the vehicle.

text: If you must make an emergency stop, brake so that
explanation: You should brake in a way that will keep your vehicle in a straight line and allow you to turn if it becomes necessary. You can use the "controlled braking" method or the "stab braking" method.

text: If your vehicle has any of the following equipment, what needs to work properly?
explanation: If your vehicle has any of the following equipment, make sure it works: vapor recovery kits, grounding and bonding cables, emergency shut-off systems, or built in fire extinguisher.

text: If a hill or curve keeps drivers behind you from seeing the vehicle within 500 feet:
explanation: If line of sight view is obstructed due to hill or curve, move the rear-most triangle to a point back down the road so warning is provided up to 500 feet.

text: When you load the small tanks of a cargo tank equipped with bulkheads, you should check your:
explanation: Some liquid tanks are divided into several smaller tanks by bulkheads. When loading and unloading the smaller tanks, the driver must pay attention to weight distribution. Don't put too much weight on the front or rear of the vehicle.

text: When you unload the smaller tanks of a tank with bulk heads, be careful to check the:
explanation: When loading and unloading the smaller tanks, the driver must pay attention to weight distribution. Don't put too much weight on the front or rear of the vehicle.

text: How would you expect a truck with a cargo tank that has baffles to handle on the road?
explanation: Baffled liquid tanks have bulkheads in them with holes that let the liquid flow through. The baffles help to control the forward and backward liquid surge. Side-to-side surge can still occur. This can cause a roll over.

text: Separations between compartments inside tanks that have openings or holes in them are called?
explanation: Baffled liquid tanks have bulkheads in them with holes that let the liquid flow through. The baffles help to control the forward and backward liquid surge.

text: An emergency forces you to stop your tanker quickly or crash. You should:
explanation: You should brake in a way that will keep your vehicle in a straight line and allow you to turn if it becomes necessary. You can use the "controlled braking" method or the "stab braking" method.

text: The best way to take a curve with a tanker is to slow to a safe speed before entering the curve, then _____ as you go through it.
explanation: Slow down before curves, then accelerate slightly through the curve. The posted speed for a curve may be too fast for a tank vehicle.

text: When loading a tanker that has 3 or 4 valves at the bottom of the tanker, you should have the valves?
explanation: Never drive or load a tank vehicle with open valves or manhole covers.

text: Outage depends on
explanation: Since different liquids expand by different amounts, they require different amounts of outage. You must know the outage requirement when hauling liquids in bulk.

text: If you steer quickly while braking, your tanker may
explanation: Because of the high center of gravity and the surge of the liquid, you must start, slow down, and stop very smoothly. Also, make smooth turns and lane changes.

text: You may not be able to fill the tanks completely because
explanation: A full tank of dense liquid (such as some acids) may exceed legal weight limits. For that reason, you may often only partially fill tanks with heavy liquids. The amount of liquid to load into a tank depends on: the amount the liquid will expand in transit, the weight of the liquid, and the legal weight limits.

text: A vehicle placarded for hazardous materials must have placards on ____ sides.
explanation: You placard your vehicle to warn everyone around you that you are transporting hazardous materials. Therefore, you must have placards on all four sides of your vehicle.

text: You should know the outage needed for the liquids you carry because?
explanation: Liquids expand as they warm and you must leave room for the expanding liquid. This is called "outage." Since different liquids expand by different amounts, they require different amounts of outage.

text: You are driving on a clear night, you must dim your headlights from high to low, and should adjust your speed so you can stop within:
explanation: You should always be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead. Fog, rain, or other conditions may require that you slowdown to be able to stop in the distance you can see. At night, you can't see as far with low beams as you can with high beams. When you must use low beams, slow down.

text: Baffles in liquid cargo tanks do not usually prevent the movement of surge from:
explanation: Baffles slow front to back surge in tankers but have little effect on side to side surge.

text: Liquid tight separations between compartments inside tanks are called:
explanation: Some liquid tanks are divided into several smaller tanks by bulkheads.

text: The movement of the liquid in partially filled tanks is called
explanation: Liquid surge results from movement of the liquid in partially filled tanks. This movement can have bad effects on handling. When the wave hits the end or sides of the tank, it tends to push the truck in the direction the wave is moving.

text: A tank endorsement is required for certain vehicles that transport
explanation: A tank endorsement is required for certain vehicles that transport liquids or gases. The liquid or gas does not have to be a hazardous material.

text: When loading a tank with bulkheads, don't put too much weight on ________ of the vehicle.
explanation: Don't put too much weight on the front or rear of the vehicle.

text: Manhole covers should be
explanation: Never drive a tank vehicle with open valves or manhole covers.

text: If you do not have an automatic tank drain, when should you drain your air tanks?
explanation: If your vehicle does not have automatic air tank drains, drain your air tanks at the end of each working day to remove moisture and oil. Otherwise, the brakes could fail.

text: Exactly how should you inspect your tank vehicle?
explanation: Tank vehicles have special items that you need to check. Tank vehicles come in many types and sizes. You need to check the vehicle's operator manual to make sure you know how to inspect your tank vehicle.

text: A smooth bore tank is also known as
explanation: Un-baffled liquid tankers also called smooth bore" tanks have nothing inside to slow down the flow of the liquid.

text: Liquid surge is especially dangerous
explanation: If the truck is on a slippery surface such as ice, the wave can shove a stopped truck out into an intersection.

text: Which of these statements about emergency steering and tankers is true?
explanation: If you must make a quick steering movement, applying the brakes would cause liquid surge increasing the chance of a roll over.

text: You should be extremely cautious when driving smooth bore tankers, especially when you are:
explanation: Be extremely cautious in driving smooth bore tanks, especially when starting and stopping.

text: If you drive a tank vehicle that you know is leaking, you may
explanation: Don't carry liquids or gases in a leaking tank. To do so is a crime. You will be cited and prevented from driving further. You may also be liable for the clean-up of any spill.

text: If your vehicle is placarded, you must stop _______ feet before the nearest rail at a railroad crossing.
explanation: Stop before a railroad crossing if your vehicle: Is placarded, carries any amount of chlorine, has cargo tanks loaded or empty used for hazardous materials. You must stop 15 to 50 feet before the nearest rail. Proceed only when you are sure no train is coming, and you can clear the tracks without stopping. Don't shift gears while crossing the tracks.

text: To correct a drive-wheel braking skid, you should
explanation: Do the following to correct a drive-wheel braking skid: stop braking allowing the rear wheels roll again and keep the rear wheels from sliding, and counter-steer. As a vehicle turns back on course, it tends to keep on turning, unless you turn the steering wheel quickly the other way, you may find yourself skidding in the opposite direction.

text: When pre-tripping your tanker you should check for?
explanation: On all tank vehicles, the most important item to check for is leaks. Check under and around the vehicle for signs of any leaking. Don't carry liquids or gases in a leaking tank.

text: What does liquid surge do to the handling of a tanker?
explanation: Liquid surge results from movement of the liquid in partially filled tanks. This movement can have bad effects on handling. When the wave hits the end of the tank, it tends to push the truck in the direction the wave is moving.

text: When hauling 2 tankers, where should the weight be carried?
explanation: For the safest handling on the road, the more heavily loaded semitrailer should be in first position behind the tractor. The lighter trailer should be in the rear.

text: Hauling liquids in tank vehicles requires special care for two reasons. One reason is:
explanation: Hauling liquids in tanks requires special skills because of the high center of gravity and liquid movement.

text: Loaded tankers are said to have a high center of gravity. What does this mean?
explanation: High center of gravity means that much of the load's weight is carried high up off the road. This makes the vehicle top-heavy and easy to roll over. Tests have shown that tankers can turn over at the speed limits posted for curves.

text: A tanker has lost its brakes. When should the driver use a truck escape ramp?
explanation: Any driver who loses brakes going downhill should use an escape ramp if it's available. If you don't use it, your chances of having a serious crash may be much greater.
